findings.title: Residence narrative drops applicant's floor (renders "0th floor" in POSITIVE) — v1→v2 key drift addressFloor vs applicantStayingFloor
findings.detail: The v2 mobile residence form captures the floor under the key `addressFloor` for every outcome (LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:794 SHIFTED, :981 NSP, plus POSITIVE/ERT sets — `applicantStayingFloor` appears only as a select-options map entry at :331, never as an actual field). But the FIELD_REPORT narrative was ported from v1 and reads the OLD v1 residence key `applicantStayingFloor`: RESIDENCE_COLUMNS maps `applicant_staying_floor`→`residence.formData.applicantStayingFloor` (fieldReportDefaults.ts:128), and the body templates reference `applicant_staying_floor` (POSITIVE :174 `{{ordinal applicant_staying_floor}}`; SHIFTED/NSP :208/:228/:245/:265 `{{addressFloor applicant_staying_floor}}`). Every OTHER field slug correctly maps `address_floor`→`addressFloor` (office :324, business :518, RCO via applicant_staying_floor too, builder :1180, dsa :1375, noc :1556), so residence is the odd one out. Consequence per outcome: the column resolves to undefined; in SHIFTED/NSP the floor sentence silently vanishes (addressFloor helper returns '' for empty, helpers.ts:179); in POSITIVE the sentence becomes "...is staying on the 0th floor." because ordinal('') → Number('')=0 → "0th" (helpers.ts:18-24, :174). The agent's real answer is discarded AND replaced with a fabricated wrong value — worse than a blank. v1 parity: v1's comprehensiveFormFieldMapping.ts:311 confirms residence (formTypes POSITIVE/NSP) used `applicantStayingFloor`, so this is a v2 mobile-form rename that the report layer was never updated for.

LIVE PROOF (psql crm2_dev): POSITIVE residence task d1c71944 has form_data.residence.formData.addressFloor='5', no applicantStayingFloor key → narrative would render "0th floor" instead of "5th floor".

findings.title: Raw-sections view: Staying/Shifted Period omitted from their section and dumped into "Additional Details" (split value/unit + addressFloor/addressLocatable/remarks ungrouped)
findings.detail: The field-report has two parallel views. The narrative (canonicalized) recombines `stayingPeriodValue`+`stayingPeriodUnit`→`stayingPeriod` (canonicalize.ts:40-48,107-113), but the raw `sections` view is built from UN-canonicalized form_data (service.ts:28 `buildSections(ctx.formData)` vs :55 canonicalized narrative). The residence sectionMap places refs `stayingPeriod` (sectionMap.ts:46) and `shiftedPeriod` (:49) in "Met Person & Occupancy", but the device never submits those combined keys — it submits split value/unit. So those rows are absent from their proper section, and `stayingPeriodValue`/`stayingPeriodUnit` (+ `shiftedPeriodValue`/`shiftedPeriodUnit`) instead land in the trailing "Additional Details" catch-all (sections.ts:133-140) mislabeled "Staying Period Value"/"Staying Period Unit". Same fate for `addressFloor`, `addressLocatable`, and `remarks` — submitted but not in the residence sectionMap (which lists `applicantStayingFloor`, not addressFloor). No data is LOST (never-lose-a-field invariant holds), but the structured report mis-groups and relabels tenure + floor, which a verifier reads as the primary occupancy evidence. The fix is to either canonicalize before buildSections or add the split-key refs / addressFloor to the residence sectionMap.
findings.evidence: Split logic: /Users/mayurkulkarni/Downloads/crm2/apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/service.ts:28,55. SectionMap refs: /Users/mayurkulkarni/Downloads/crm2/apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sectionMap.ts:46,49,50. Catch-all: /Users/mayurkulkarni/Downloads/crm2/apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sections.ts:106-142. LIVE PROOF (psql): task d1c71944 form_data.residence.formData has stayingPeriodValue='8', stayingPeriodUnit='Year', addressFloor='5', remarks — and NO combined stayingPeriod/applicantStayingFloor key.

findings.title: Same report shows two different outcome strings: raw CODE in sections ("POSITIVE") vs verbose label in narrative ("Positive & Door Open")
findings.detail: The sections view leads with a "Verification Outcome" row taken verbatim from the raw form_data.residence.verificationOutcome (sections.ts:124-128,154 → the 5 uppercase codes), while the narrative paragraph below it derives the v1 verbose label via canonicalize (POSITIVE+houseStatus=Open → "Positive & Door Open"). On the same web Field Report a verifier therefore sees "Verification Outcome: POSITIVE" in the grouped section and "...POSITIVE & DOOR OPEN" in the narrative remark. Not a data defect (both are correct representations and the split is intentional per the code comments), but a label-consistency wrinkle that can read as a discrepancy. A small UX normalization (show the same human label in both, or label the section row "Outcome Code") would remove the ambiguity.
findings.evidence: Sections raw outcome: /Users/mayurkulkarni/Downloads/crm2/apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sections.ts:124-128,154. Narrative label derivation: /Users/mayurkulkarni/Downloads/crm2/apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/canonicalize.ts:52-72,96-100. LIVE PROOF (psql): task d1c71944 verificationOutcome='POSITIVE', houseStatus='Open' → narrative label "Positive & Door Open" while section shows "POSITIVE".

findings.title: Residence narrative floor clause always empty — template reads applicantStayingFloor but device emits addressFloor (canonicalize does not remap it)
findings.detail: The FIELD_REPORT narrative for RESIDENCE reads the floor via FORM_DATA_PATH 'residence.formData.applicantStayingFloor' (packages/sdk/src/fieldReportDefaults.ts:128, column applicant_staying_floor), used in the POSITIVE branch '{{customer_name}} is staying on the {{ordinal applicant_staying_floor}} floor' (fieldReportDefaults.ts:174) and in SHIFTED/NSP branches via '{{addressFloor applicant_staying_floor}}' (fieldReportDefaults.ts:208,228,245,265). The v2-native mobile RESIDENCE builder never emits applicantStayingFloor — every residence outcome field-set defines the floor field as name:'addressFloor' (LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:794 POSITIVE, :981 SHIFTED, :1147 NSP); grep for `name: 'applicantStayingFloor'` returns ZERO hits anywhere in the builders (it exists only as an option-list key at :331). canonicalize.ts (ADR-0057, wired at fieldReports/service.ts:55) only remaps the outcome code→label and recombines split tenure periods (PERIOD_BASES, canonicalize.ts:40-48); it does NOT rename addressFloor→applicantStayingFloor. render.ts:walkPath therefore returns undefined → '' (render.ts:18-22,49). Result: the floor clause renders empty in every residence narrative that has live floor data. This is registry AUDIT-3 (DEFERRED), but I confirm it was NOT closed by the ADR-0057 canonicalize fix and is live whenever the RESIDENCE layout is active (it is — report_layouts id1 FIELD_REPORT/RESIDENCE is the one active field layout).
findings.evidence: Report side: packages/sdk/src/fieldReportDefaults.ts:128 `F('applicant_staying_floor','Applicant Staying Floor','applicantStayingFloor')` + use at :174,:208,:228,:245,:265. Device side: LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:794/981/1147 all `name: 'addressFloor'`; `grep -n "name: 'applicantStayingFloor'"` → 0 results. Live DB proof: case_tasks d1c71944-... form_data->'residence'->'formData' has `"addressFloor":"5"` and NO applicantStayingFloor key (psql jsonb_pretty). canonicalize.ts:40-48,56-72 — no floor remap. v1 ground-truth shows v1 RESIDENCE used applicantStayingFloor (docs/specs/2026-06-16-v1-report-mapping.md:28; residenceFormFieldMapping.ts:30) — the v2 mobile rewrite renamed it to addressFloor, creating the drift.

findings.title: Raw sections view mis-groups split tenure (stayingPeriodValue/Unit, shiftedPeriodValue/Unit) into 'Additional Details' — buildSections uses un-canonicalized form_data
findings.detail: fieldReports/service.ts splits the report into two views: narrative from canonicalizeRenderContext(ctx) (line 55, recombines periods) but sections from RAW ctx.formData (line 28, buildSections). sectionMap.ts residence keys the tenure rows on the COMBINED refs 'stayingPeriod' (sectionMap.ts:46) and 'shiftedPeriod' (sectionMap.ts:49). The v2 device never emits those combined keys — it emits stayingPeriodValue/stayingPeriodUnit (LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:718-728) and shiftedPeriodValue/shiftedPeriodUnit (:917-927). Because buildSections does not recombine, mappedSections (sections.ts:106-142) cannot place the Value/Unit keys in 'Met Person & Occupancy', so the never-lose-a-field catch-all (sections.ts:133-140) drops them into the trailing 'Additional Details' section with toLabel()-derived labels ('Staying Period Value' / 'Staying Period Unit'). No data is lost, but the grouping/labels diverge from the ADR-0060 spec and from the narrative. Same class as registry AUDIT-2, but AUDIT-2 only tracked the narrative (now fixed by canonicalize); the sections path is the residual.
findings.evidence: service.ts:28 `const sections = buildSections(ctx.formData)` (RAW) vs :55 `renderNarrative(..., canonicalizeRenderContext(ctx))` (CANONICAL). sectionMap.ts:46 `{ ref: 'stayingPeriod', label: 'Staying Period' }`, :49 `{ ref: 'shiftedPeriod', ... }`. Device keys: LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:718 stayingPeriodValue / :724 stayingPeriodUnit; :917 shiftedPeriodValue / :923 shiftedPeriodUnit. Live DB: form_data->'residence'->'formData' has `stayingPeriodValue:'8'`, `stayingPeriodUnit:'Year'`, and NO `stayingPeriod` key (psql query returned spv=8, sp=NULL). sections.ts:117 `toValue(fieldsSource[ref])` returns null for combined ref → falls to extra[] at :134-140.

findings.title: Raw sections view: addressFloor and addressLocatable land in 'Additional Details' (sectionMap residence omits both)
findings.detail: In the same RAW sections path (service.ts:28), sectionMap.ts residence (lines 29-114) never lists the device key 'addressFloor' (it lists only 'applicantStayingFloor' at :50) and never lists 'addressLocatable' at all. The device emits addressFloor (LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:794) and addressLocatable (:642) on every residence outcome. Both are preserved by the never-lose-a-field catch-all but render under 'Additional Details' with generic toLabel() labels instead of their intended sections ('Property/Locality' for floor; the addressLocatable rating). Cosmetic/grouping only — no field dropped — but it is the same v2-device-key↔v1-section-map drift as F1 and should be fixed together when the floor key is reconciled.
findings.evidence: sectionMap.ts residence refs (lines 33-112): contains addressRating, applicantStayingFloor (:50), applicantStayingStatus (:51) but NOT addressFloor and NOT addressLocatable. Device emits both: LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:642 addressLocatable, :794 addressFloor. Live DB proof: form_data->'residence'->'formData' contains `"addressLocatable":"Easy to Locate"` and `"addressFloor":"5"` (psql jsonb_pretty), neither matching a mapped ref → routed to extra[] (sections.ts:134-140).

findings.title: Registry AUDIT-1 / AUDIT-2 are STALE — outcome-code→label and split-period narrative gaps already remediated by canonicalize.ts (ADR-0057); should be re-dispositioned FIXED
findings.detail: docs/COMPLIANCE_GAPS_REGISTRY.md:850 (AUDIT-1, outcome-vocabulary mismatch — narrative empty because templates branch on v1 verbose labels while the device sends 5 uppercase codes) and :861 (AUDIT-2, tenure clauses empty because templates read a single <period> while the device emits <period>Value/<period>Unit) are both still marked '🔴 CONFIRMED, owner decision pending'. Both are already fixed for the narrative: canonicalize.ts derives the v1 verbose label from (slug, code, houseStatus) — deriveLabel + doorSuffix (canonicalize.ts:52-72), covering all 5 RESIDENCE codes incl door-status expansion — and recombines every split tenure base back into the combined token (canonicalize.ts:40-48,106-113). It is wired into the live render at fieldReports/service.ts:55. So a residence narrative rendered from a real v2 device blob now matches the {{#eq outcome 'Positive & Door Open'}} branches and fills the {{pluralize staying_period}} clauses. Per the CLAUDE.md governance rule ('every audit finding ends FIXED/DEFERRED/RATCHET/WONTFIX, never silently dropped'), AUDIT-1/AUDIT-2 should be moved to FIXED (referencing ADR-0057 / canonicalize.ts) so the registry stops over-stating live risk. (AUDIT-3, the floor/secondary-ref drift, correctly remains open — that is my F1/F3.)
findings.evidence: Registry: docs/COMPLIANCE_GAPS_REGISTRY.md:850-859 (AUDIT-1), :861-864 (AUDIT-2). Remediation: canonicalize.ts:18 OUTCOME_CODES set; :56-72 deriveLabel (ENTRY_RESTRICTED→'ERT', UNTRACEABLE→'Untraceable', POSITIVE/SHIFTED/NSP + doorSuffix from houseStatus); :40-48 PERIOD_BASES incl stayingPeriod/shiftedPeriod; :107-113 recombine. Wiring: fieldReports/service.ts:4 import, :55 `renderNarrative(..., canonicalizeRenderContext(ctx))`. Narrative defaults that consume the canonicalized values: fieldReportDefaults.ts:165-295 RESIDENCE_BODY branches on verbose labels + reads combined staying_period/shifted_period (:117,:120).

findings.title: Field-path verification_outcome correctly bypasses chk_case_task_outcome — no field code can violate the column CHECK (NULL-only on field path, verified)
findings.detail: Confirming (refuting the candidate-drift, not a defect): the case_tasks.verification_outcome COLUMN CHECK chk_case_task_outcome restricts values to POSITIVE/NEGATIVE/REFER/FRAUD, which does NOT include the field outcome codes SHIFTED/NSP/ENTRY_RESTRICTED/UNTRACEABLE. On the RESIDENCE field path this is harmless: the device outcome (POSITIVE/SHIFTED/NSP/ENTRY_RESTRICTED/UNTRACEABLE) is stored ONLY as evidence inside form_data['residence'].verificationOutcome via submitVerificationForm's jsonb_set (cases/repository.ts:1418-1443) and the COLUMN is left NULL (ADR-0032 D1 single-layer). I verified the live row keeps verification_outcome=NULL while form_data.residence.verificationOutcome='POSITIVE'. There is no code path on the field submit that writes a field code into the column, so a 23514 check violation cannot occur for SHIFTED/NSP/ENTRY_RESTRICTED/UNTRACEABLE submissions. This part of the contract is correct.
findings.evidence: DB constraint: `chk_case_task_outcome CHECK (verification_outcome IS NULL OR verification_outcome IN ('POSITIVE','NEGATIVE','REFER','FRAUD'))` (psql pg_get_constraintdef). DB outcomes: verification_unit_outcomes for RESIDENCE = POSITIVE/SHIFTED/NSP/ENTRY_RESTRICTED/UNTRACEABLE (psql, 5 rows). Persist path writes only form_data, not the column: cases/repository.ts:1427-1432 UPDATE ... jsonb_set(form_data, ARRAY[$4], $5) — no SET verification_outcome. Live proof: row d1c71944-... verification_outcome COLUMN = NULL, form_data->'residence'->>'verificationOutcome' = 'POSITIVE' (psql).

findings.title: Raw report sections never recombine split tenure fields — 'Staying Period'/'Shifted Period' group rows are always empty; value+unit dumped into 'Additional Details'
findings.detail: sectionMap.ts places stayingPeriod (ref) under 'Met Person & Occupancy' and shiftedPeriod under the same section, but the v2-native device only ever emits the SPLIT keys stayingPeriodValue+stayingPeriodUnit (POSITIVE/NSP) and shiftedPeriodValue+shiftedPeriodUnit (SHIFTED). The combined stayingPeriod/shiftedPeriod key is reconstructed ONLY in canonicalizeRenderContext (canonicalize.ts:107-113, narrative path). buildSections operates on RAW ctx.formData (service.ts:28) with no recombination, so: (1) the spec'd 'Staying Period' row in 'Met Person & Occupancy' never resolves (toValue(fieldsSource['stayingPeriod']) is null), and (2) stayingPeriodValue + stayingPeriodUnit are not in the residence sectionMap at all, so the never-lose-a-field catch-all relabels them via toLabel() into a trailing 'Additional Details' section as two separate rows 'Staying Period Value' = '8' and 'Staying Period Unit' = 'Year'. The web shows BOTH the sections view and the narrative (CaseDetailPage.tsx:1847-1864), so the user sees a tenure that is split, ungrouped, mis-sectioned, and label-drifted in the raw view while the narrative reads it correctly — an obvious inconsistency on the same report. Affects every RESIDENCE field report on POSITIVE/NSP (staying) and SHIFTED (shifted).
findings.evidence: sectionMap.ts:46 ref 'stayingPeriod' label 'Staying Period' (and :49 'shiftedPeriod'); device emits split: LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:718 'stayingPeriodValue' + :724 'stayingPeriodUnit' (POSITIVE), :917 'shiftedPeriodValue' + :923 'shiftedPeriodUnit' (SHIFTED). Recombine is narrative-only: canonicalize.ts:40-48 PERIOD_BASES + :107-113; service.ts:28 buildSections(ctx.formData) RAW vs :55 renderNarrative(...canonicalizeRenderContext(ctx)). buildSections has no recombine (sections.ts:106-142). LIVE PROOF (psql crm2_dev case_tasks form_data->residence->formData): has stayingPeriodValue='8', stayingPeriodUnit='Year', NO combined stayingPeriod key. Set-diff (script): POSITIVE/NSP Additional-Details keys include stayingPeriodValue+stayingPeriodUnit; SHIFTED includes shiftedPeriodValue+shiftedPeriodUnit.

findings.title: Same report shows two different outcome strings: raw CODE 'POSITIVE' in sections vs verbose 'Positive & Door Open' in the narrative
findings.detail: On the combined web Field Report the leading 'Verification Outcome' row in the sections block shows the raw device CODE (e.g. 'POSITIVE'), because buildSections reads ctx.formData unchanged and unshift()s the raw verificationOutcome string. The narrative paragraph directly below shows the canonicalized v1 verbose label (e.g. 'Positive & Door Open', or 'ERT'/'Untraceable' for those outcomes) because renderNarrative runs on canonicalizeRenderContext. A reviewer reads the same task's outcome twice in two different vocabularies within one expanded card. This is intentional per code comments but is a UX label-consistency defect (the raw uppercase code is not a customer-facing label and reads as a different value from the narrative). Affects all 5 outcomes; the divergence is widest for ENTRY_RESTRICTED ('ENTRY_RESTRICTED' vs 'ERT') and the door-status outcomes (door suffix appears only in narrative).
findings.evidence: service.ts:28 const sections = buildSections(ctx.formData) (RAW); sections.ts:125-128 unshift raw verificationOutcome row; canonicalize.ts:55-72 deriveLabel maps POSITIVE+Open→'Positive & Door Open', ENTRY_RESTRICTED→'ERT', UNTRACEABLE→'Untraceable'; service.ts:55 narrative = renderNarrative(...canonicalizeRenderContext(ctx)). Web renders both: CaseDetailPage.tsx:1847-1858 (sections) immediately followed by :1859-1864 (narrative). DB chk: case_tasks.form_data.residence.verificationOutcome holds the CODE; the row's verification_outcome COLUMN is NULL (field path).

findings.title: Required captures addressFloor + addressLocatable are unmapped in both sectionMap and narrative — only ever surface ungrouped in 'Additional Details'; addressLocatable value is also contradicted by hardcoded narrative prose
findings.detail: addressFloor (required, FLOOR_OPTIONS) and addressLocatable (required, Title-cased values like 'Easy to Locate'/'Difficult to Locate') are captured on the device in POSITIVE/SHIFTED/NSP (addressLocatable in ENTRY_RESTRICTED too), but neither key appears in the residence sectionMap nor in the RESIDENCE narrative columns. They therefore only ever render via the trailing 'Additional Details' catch-all under auto-generated labels 'Address Floor' / 'Address Locatable', never grouped with the related 'Property / Nameplate & Documents' or 'Verification Outcome & Status' content where a reviewer would look. Note addressFloor is distinct from the sectionMap's applicantStayingFloor (different concept), so it is genuinely unmapped, not a rename. Worse for addressLocatable: the narrative prose hardcodes 'The given address is locatable and rated as {{address_rating}}' as static text, so if the agent captured 'Difficult to Locate' the narrative still asserts 'is locatable' — the captured value is ignored by the narrative and only visible (unlabelled-in-context) in Additional Details. No data is lost, but the grouping/label is wrong and the narrative can contradict the capture.
findings.evidence: sectionMap residence block (sectionMap.ts:29-114) contains NO addressFloor or addressLocatable (grep count 0 in block; applicantStayingFloor at :50 is a different ref). Narrative columns: fieldReportDefaults.ts:105-156 has no addressFloor/addressLocatable column (grep 0). Hardcoded prose: fieldReportDefaults.ts:168 (and :185/:202/:219/:236/:256/:273) 'The given address is locatable and rated as {{address_rating}}'. Device required: LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:642-645 addressLocatable required:true (×5 outcomes), :794-797 addressFloor required:true. LIVE PROOF: addressFloor='5', addressLocatable='Easy to Locate' present in form_data, neither in a named section.

findings.title: Field-photo upload idempotency replay leaks cross-user photo GPS + storage key via unscoped operation_id lookup (IDOR)
findings.detail: In the device field-photo upload path (POST /api/v2/verification-tasks/:id/attachments), the idempotency replay reads cached rows by the client-supplied operation_id with NO scoping to the actor's case/task/user. apps/api/src/modules/verification-tasks/service.ts:211-212 calls repo.fieldAttachmentsByOperation(operationBase) and, if any row matches, returns it verbatim via uploadResult(). repo.fieldAttachmentsByOperation (apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:1571-1579) filters ONLY on `split_part(operation_id, ':', 1) = $1 AND kind='FIELD_PHOTO'` — it does not constrain case_id, task_id, or uploaded_by. uq_case_attachments_operation is a GLOBAL unique index (proven via \d case_attachments), so operation_id values live in one flat namespace across every agent and case. The ownedCaseId(taskId) gate at service.ts:200 only proves the attacker owns SOME task; it does not bound the operation_id lookup. The replayed DeviceAttachment projection (FIELD_ATTACH_COLS, repository.ts:309-310) exposes `geo_location` (the victim agent's capture GPS), `storage_key AS url`, `original_name`, `photo_type`, and the attachment id — all returned in the JSON response (sync.ts:91-106 DeviceAttachment + 108-110 DeviceAttachmentUploadResult). A malicious or compromised field agent who replays an Idempotency-Key matching another agent's prior upload receives that victim's photo GPS coordinates and metadata. The same unscoped lookup is also hit on the concurrent-winner path at service.ts:269. The image BYTES stay protected (the /attachments/:id/url and /field-photos/:id/download routes re-check scope by attachment id), which caps this below CRITICAL, but GPS location PII + storage-key disclosure is a real cross-user leak.
findings.evidence: apps/api/src/modules/verification-tasks/service.ts:200 (ownedCaseId gate), :211-212 (cached = fieldAttachmentsByOperation(operationBase); return uploadResult(cached,...)), :269 (winner = fieldAttachmentsByOperation(operationBase)); apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:1571-1579 (lookup keyed ONLY on operation_id, no case/task/user filter), :309-310 (FIELD_ATTACH_COLS exposes geo_location + storage_key AS url + original_name); packages/sdk/src/sync.ts:91-106,108-110 (DeviceAttachment.url=storage key + geoLocation returned). DB proof: `SELECT operation_id ... FROM case_attachments WHERE kind='FIELD_PHOTO'` shows distinct per-row operation ids; `\d case_attachments` shows `uq_case_attachments_operation UNIQUE btree(operation_id) WHERE operation_id IS NOT NULL` — a single GLOBAL namespace, not per-task.

findings.title: Attachment transit-integrity result (hash_verified / client_sha256) is stored but never surfaced — tamper goes undetected by the office/verifier
findings.detail: On field-photo upload the server compares the device's client_sha256 against the bytes it actually received and persists the boolean result, but a mismatch is only logged, never rejected, AND the result is never exposed to any reader, so the integrity signal is effectively write-only. apps/api/src/modules/verification-tasks/service.ts:232 computes `hashVerified = clientSha !== null && clientSha === sha256(f.buffer)`; :233-234 logs a warn on mismatch but the upload still succeeds; :255 stores hashVerified into case_attachments.hash_verified (column exists per \d case_attachments). No read path surfaces it: grep of apps/web and packages/sdk for hash_verified/hashVerified/client_sha256 returns ZERO hits, and the field-photo read projections omit it — listFieldPhotos (repository.ts:1622-1624) selects photo_type/geo/address only; the field report context (fieldReports/repository.ts:53-70) selects no integrity field. Net effect: a photo whose hash did not match the device's claimed hash (in-transit corruption or a tampered/forged client) is rendered to the office reviewer and the final report identically to a clean, verified one — the verifier has no way to see that the integrity check failed. This weakens the photographic-evidence chain that the whole FIELD verification flow rests on. Per-type-agnostic; affects every FIELD_VISIT photo upload including all RESIDENCE outcomes.
findings.evidence: apps/api/src/modules/verification-tasks/service.ts:232 (hashVerified compare), :233-234 (mismatch logged, not rejected), :253-255 (sha256 + clientSha256 + hashVerified persisted); apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:1542,1556 (INSERT writes hash_verified); DB `\d case_attachments` shows columns client_sha256 + hash_verified (nullable) exist and live rows carry hash_verified=t. Negative proof: `grep -rn 'hash_verified|hashVerified|client_sha256' apps/web packages/sdk` → 0 results; listFieldPhotos projection (repository.ts:1622-1624) and fieldReports loadContext (fieldReports/repository.ts:53-70) omit any integrity field.

findings.title: OFFICE report shows EMPTY period rows + dumps the real tenure values into 'Additional Details' (v1 parity regression)
findings.detail: The device splits every tenure into <base>Value + <base>Unit (workingPeriodValue/Unit, establishmentPeriodValue/Unit for POSITIVE; currentCompanyPeriodValue/Unit, oldOfficeShiftedPeriodValue/Unit for SHIFTED). The OFFICE raw-section view (the ONLY OFFICE report path today, since OFFICE has no active FIELD_REPORT layout) references the v1 COMBINED keys (workingPeriod, establishmentPeriod, currentCompanyPeriod, oldOfficeShiftedPeriod). buildSections/mappedSections reads fieldsSource[ref] directly with NO Value/Unit recombination, so toValue(fieldsSource['workingPeriod']) is null -> the labelled 'Working Period'/'Establishment Period'/'Current Company Period'/'Old Office Shifted Period' rows render EMPTY (skipped), while the actual split keys are not 'placed' and fall into the trailing 'Additional Details' section with auto-generated labels ('Working Period Value','Working Period Unit',...) in the wrong group. canonicalizeRenderContext DOES recombine these (PERIOD_BASES) but is applied ONLY to the latent narrative path (service.ts:55), never to buildSections (service.ts:28). v1 did NOT have this problem: its backend preprocessCompositeFields merged each Value+Unit pair into the combined target at ingest BEFORE storing (e.g. workingPeriod='5 Years'), so the v1 report rendered the period correctly. crm2's verbatim-storage policy keeps the split blob, but the raw report never recombines -> a real parity regression in the OFFICE field report output for POSITIVE (working/establishment period) and SHIFTED (current-company/old-office period).
findings.evidence: DEVICE split keys: crm-mobile-native LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:2492 (workingPeriodValue) + :2499 (workingPeriodUnit), :2634/:2640 (establishmentPeriodValue/Unit), :2746/:2752 (currentCompanyPeriodValue/Unit), :2758/:2764 (oldOfficeShiftedPeriodValue/Unit). REPORT combined refs: crm2 apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sectionMap.ts:139 (workingPeriod), :146 (establishmentPeriod), :155 (oldOfficeShiftedPeriod), :157 (currentCompanyPeriod). NO recombine in raw path: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sections.ts:117 reads toValue(fieldsSource[ref]) directly; catch-all at :133-140 emits the split keys with toLabel. Recombine exists only for narrative: canonicalize.ts:40-48 PERIOD_BASES + :107-113, invoked only at service.ts:55 (narrative), not at service.ts:28 (buildSections). OFFICE has no active narrative layout: DB report_layouts has only id1 FIELD_REPORT/RESIDENCE + id2 CASE_REPORT (ran: SELECT id,kind,verification_type,is_active FROM report_layouts -> 2 rows, none OFFICE); report_templates 0 rows; so report.narrative=null and web renders ONLY report.sections (apps/web/src/features/cases/CaseDetailPage.tsx:1840,1847). v1 ingest merge: CRM-BACKEND/src/controllers/mobileFormController.ts:145-193 preprocessCompositeFields merges workingPeriodValue+Unit->workingPeriod etc (:172,:152-165) before store; v1 field def was a single combined workingPeriod (CRM-BACKEND/src/utils/comprehensiveFormFieldMapping.ts:684-691).

findings.title: Admin-edited OFFICE outcome labels/order never reach the field app (server sends long code 'OFFICE', device map keyed by short code 'OV')
findings.detail: The reference endpoint that the field app refreshes each sync emits verification_type_code straight from the DB column = 'OFFICE' (the long code). On the device, setOutcomesFromSync resolves each row via FORM_TYPE_KEY_BY_VTYPE_CODE, which is keyed by SHORT codes (RV/OV/EV/...). FORM_TYPE_KEY_BY_VTYPE_CODE['OFFICE'] is undefined, so 'if(!key) continue' skips EVERY OFFICE row (in fact every row for every type, since the server sends long codes for all of them). Net product effect: syncedOutcomesByFormType and syncedLabelByOutcome stay empty, and the device silently falls back to the embedded COMMON_LEGACY_OUTCOMES list and hardcoded getOutcomeLabel. The server-side admin feature to re-order or re-label OFFICE outcomes (verification_unit_outcomes.display_label/sort_order) is therefore DEAD for the field app. For OFFICE specifically the offered outcome SET is still correct only because the embedded fallback (POSITIVE/SHIFTED/NSP/ENTRY_RESTRICTED/UNTRACEABLE) happens to equal the DB set exactly - so this is a latent correctness gap, not a wrong-list-today bug, for OFFICE. Cross-cutting: same break for all 9 FIELD types.
findings.evidence: Server emits DB long code: crm2 apps/api/src/modules/reference/repository.ts:12-16 SELECT verification_type_code ... FROM verification_unit_outcomes (no transform in service.ts/controller.ts). DB value confirmed (ran: SELECT verification_type_code,outcome_code FROM verification_unit_outcomes WHERE verification_type_code='OFFICE' -> 5 rows, code literally 'OFFICE'). Device short-code map + skip: crm-mobile-native LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:154-164 (FORM_TYPE_KEY_BY_VTYPE_CODE keyed OV/RV/EV...), :184-185 (key = map[r.verificationTypeCode]; if(!key) continue). Sync feeds it from the same endpoint: SyncDownloadService.ts:242 (ENDPOINTS.REFERENCE.VERIFICATION_TYPE_OUTCOMES) -> :252-258 setOutcomesFromSync passing verificationTypeCode verbatim. Embedded fallback (matches DB for OFFICE): LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:69-75 COMMON_LEGACY_OUTCOMES + :134 office->COMMON, used at :221-225 getAllowedOutcomes when synced map empty.

findings.title: A few OFFICE device fields are absent from the office section map and land in 'Additional Details' (mis-grouped, not lost)
findings.detail: Some real OFFICE answer fields have no entry in SECTION_MAP.office, so they are surfaced only via the never-lose-a-field catch-all with auto-generated labels in the trailing 'Additional Details' section rather than their natural group: addressLocatable (emitted in ALL outcome field sets), workingStatus (POSITIVE), and officeExistence (NSP - distinct from the mapped officeExistsStatus). No data is lost (the catch-all guarantees that), but the OFFICE report's grouping is incomplete vs the curated section spec - e.g. workingStatus belongs under Employment/Office Details (it is mapped there for residence at sectionMap.ts:60 but missing for office), and officeExistence is the NSP-path existence answer that should sit beside the outcome/status rows.
findings.evidence: Device fields: LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:2452 addressLocatable (POSITIVE) + present in every outcome set (e.g. :2700, :2875, :3024); :2513 workingStatus (POSITIVE); :2900 officeExistence (NSP). Office section map omits them: ran grep over apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sectionMap.ts - 'workingStatus' appears only at :60 (residence), not in the office block (:116-207); 'addressLocatable' and 'officeExistence' appear nowhere in the file. Catch-all placement: sections.ts:133-140 ('Additional Details', toLabel).

findings.title: Latent: OFFICE SHIFTED narrative label would render 'Shifted & Door Locked' (canonicalize) vs v1 device label 'Shifted & Door Locked Shifted'
findings.detail: If/when an OFFICE FIELD_REPORT layout is activated, the narrative path canonicalizes outcome code SHIFTED via deriveLabel: prefix 'Shifted' + doorSuffix(officeStatus). For SHIFTED the device sets officeStatus='Shifted', and doorSuffix returns '& Door Locked' for any non-'open' value, yielding 'Shifted & Door Locked'. The device's own embedded label for SHIFTED is 'Shifted & Door Locked Shifted'. This is a cosmetic divergence in the verbose label vocabulary and is fully LATENT today (OFFICE has no active narrative layout, so report.narrative is null). Flagged only so it is verified before an OFFICE layout ships. POSITIVE/NSP/ENTRY_RESTRICTED/UNTRACEABLE labels are covered correctly.
findings.evidence: canonicalize.ts:52-53 doorSuffix (=='open' -> '& Door Open' else '& Door Locked'), :56-72 deriveLabel (SHIFTED -> 'Shifted' + doorSuffix(officeStatus)); STATUS_FIELD.office='officeStatus' :26. Device SHIFTED sets officeStatus from options ['Open','Closed','Shifted'] (LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:2389) and the embedded SHIFTED label is 'Shifted & Door Locked Shifted' (:119). Latent: no active OFFICE report_layouts (DB query above) -> service.ts:36-44 returns narrative:null.

findings.title: OFFICE period fields (workingPeriod/establishmentPeriod/currentCompanyPeriod/oldOfficeShiftedPeriod) render EMPTY in the field report; their split Value/Unit keys spill into 'Additional Details'
findings.detail: The mobile OFFICE form splits every tenure into <base>Value + <base>Unit on the device (workingPeriodValue/workingPeriodUnit, establishmentPeriodValue/Unit, currentCompanyPeriodValue/Unit, oldOfficeShiftedPeriodValue/Unit). The API stores the body verbatim under form_data['office'] (cases/repository.ts:1428 jsonb_set ARRAY[$4]). The report's RAW section view (the ONLY view that renders for OFFICE) is built by buildSections(ctx.formData) which reads fieldsSource[ref] directly (sections.ts:117 mappedSections) with NO Value/Unit recombination. But sectionMap.office references the COMBINED refs workingPeriod (sectionMap.ts:139), establishmentPeriod (:146), oldOfficeShiftedPeriod (:155), currentCompanyPeriod (:157) — keys the device never emits. Result for every OFFICE submission: the 4 labelled period rows resolve to null and are dropped (sections.ts:118 `if (value === null) continue`), while the 8 actual split keys are caught by the never-lose-a-field catch-all (sections.ts:133-140) and rendered under 'Additional Details' with auto-generated labels ('Working Period Value'/'Working Period Unit') in the wrong section. canonicalizeRenderContext DOES recombine these (canonicalize.ts:107-113) but it is wired ONLY to the narrative path (service.ts:55), NOT to buildSections (service.ts:28). Since OFFICE has no active FIELD_REPORT layout, narrative is null and buildSections is the entire report -> drift is 100% user-visible. POSITIVE outcome is worst-hit (workingPeriod + establishmentPeriod both apply); SHIFTED hits currentCompanyPeriod + oldOfficeShiftedPeriod.
findings.evidence: Device emits split keys: LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:2492 (name:'workingPeriodValue'),:2499 (workingPeriodUnit),:2634 (establishmentPeriodValue),:2640 (establishmentPeriodUnit),:2746 (currentCompanyPeriodValue),:2758 (oldOfficeShiftedPeriodValue) — full OFFICE builder field-name scan (lines 2450-3210) shows workingPeriod/establishmentPeriod/currentCompanyPeriod/oldOfficeShiftedPeriod COMBINED are never emitted. sectionMap references combined: sectionMap.ts:139,146,155,157. Raw read w/o recombine: sections.ts:106-122 (mappedSections), :133-140 (Additional-Details catch-all). canonicalize recombine is narrative-only: fieldReports/service.ts:28 (buildSections raw) vs :55 (narrative=render(canonicalize(ctx))); canonicalize.ts:40-48 PERIOD_BASES, :107-113 recombine. No OFFICE layout: DB `SELECT ... FROM report_layouts WHERE verification_type ILIKE '%office%'` -> 0 rows; only active = id1 FIELD_REPORT/RESIDENCE, id2 CASE_REPORT.

findings.title: Server-managed OFFICE outcome list/labels never reach the device: outcome-sync map keyed by stale short codes (OV) vs the endpoint's full code (OFFICE)
findings.detail: The /reference/verification-type-outcomes endpoint selects verification_type_code straight from verification_unit_outcomes (reference/repository.ts:12-17) and returns it verbatim in the v1 {success,data} envelope (reference/service.ts:10). The live value for OFFICE is the full string 'OFFICE' (DB: SELECT DISTINCT verification_type_code -> BUILDER/BUSINESS/DSA_CONNECTOR/NOC/OFFICE/PROPERTY_APF/PROPERTY_INDIVIDUAL/RESIDENCE/RESIDENCE_CUM_OFFICE; none of the short OV/RV/EV forms exist). The mobile SyncDownloadService.refreshVerificationTypeOutcomes passes r.verificationTypeCode straight into setOutcomesFromSync (SyncDownloadService.ts:252-258), which looks it up in FORM_TYPE_KEY_BY_VTYPE_CODE — a map keyed by the OLD short codes RV/RC/OV/EV/BV/NV/DV/PIV/PAV (LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:154-164). FORM_TYPE_KEY_BY_VTYPE_CODE['OFFICE'] is undefined, so `if (!key) continue` (:185) skips the row. Net: syncedOutcomesByFormType and syncedLabelByOutcome are NEVER populated for ANY type; the device permanently falls back to the embedded COMMON_LEGACY_OUTCOMES + hardcoded getOutcomeLabel. An admin re-ordering or re-labeling OFFICE outcomes on the server (e.g. 'No Such Person' -> 'Person Absent') has ZERO effect on the device dropdown. Note the SIBLING map BACKEND_FORM_TYPE_TO_KEY (formTypeKey.ts:36-56) WAS updated to the full codes (OFFICE->office), so form-template resolution works — only this outcome-sync map was left on the old short codes, a genuine intra-repo drift. Impact today is contained: COMMON_LEGACY_OUTCOMES = [POSITIVE,SHIFTED,NSP,ENTRY_RESTRICTED,UNTRACEABLE] matches the DB's 5 OFFICE rows by code, so the SET shown is correct; the defect is a dead server->device contract (re-label/re-order silently lost), not wrong data — yet.
findings.evidence: Endpoint emits full code: reference/repository.ts:12 (SELECT ... verification_type_code FROM verification_unit_outcomes), service.ts:10 (verbatim in data[]); DB proof: verification_unit_outcomes OFFICE rows all have verification_type_code='OFFICE' (5 rows), and DISTINCT over the table = 9 full-string codes, zero short codes. Mobile skips them: SyncDownloadService.ts:252-258 (maps r.verificationTypeCode in), LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:154-164 (FORM_TYPE_KEY_BY_VTYPE_CODE short-code keys), :184-185 (`const key = FORM_TYPE_KEY_BY_VTYPE_CODE[r.verificationTypeCode]; if (!key) continue`). Sibling map updated correctly for contrast: formTypeKey.ts:41 (OFFICE:'office'). Fallback that masks it: LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:70-76 COMMON_LEGACY_OUTCOMES, :130-146 LEGACY_OUTCOMES_BY_FORM_TYPE.office.

findings.title: Mobile stores verification_type = display NAME ('Office Verification'), not the CODE — consistency-only
findings.detail: In down-sync, SyncDownloadService binds the op-sqlite column verification_type from task.verificationUnit.name (SyncDownloadService.ts:617) while verification_type_code correctly binds from task.verificationUnit.code (:631). For OFFICE that means verification_type='Office Verification' (DB verification_units.name) and verification_type_code='OFFICE' (DB code). Form-type resolution prefers code (resolveFormTypeKey order formType -> verificationTypeCode -> verificationTypeName -> verificationType, formTypeKey.ts:137-141) and BACKEND_FORM_TYPE_TO_KEY['OFFICE']='office' (formTypeKey.ts:41), so the template still resolves correctly. The only risk is any future device code reading verification_type expecting a CODE token would get the human display name. No functional defect today — flagged for column-naming consistency.
findings.evidence: Mismatched bind vs column name: SyncDownloadService.ts:617 (verification_type <- task.verificationUnit?.name) and :631 (verification_type_code <- task.verificationUnit?.code); schema column verification_type TEXT (database/schema.ts:41). API source values: sync/repository.ts:66 (vu.code AS unit_code, vu.name AS unit_name); DB verification_units OFFICE row: code='OFFICE', name='Office Verification'. Resolution prefers code: formTypeKey.ts:137-141.

findings.title: Raw OFFICE report mis-groups every tenure field: named period rows render EMPTY, split Value/Unit keys dislocated into 'Additional Details' (ADR-0060 sectionMap vs v2 split keys)
findings.detail: The v2 device splits every tenure into <base>Value + <base>Unit (workingPeriodValue/Unit + establishmentPeriodValue/Unit for POSITIVE at LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:2492,2499,2634,2640; currentCompanyPeriodValue/Unit + oldOfficeShiftedPeriodValue/Unit for SHIFTED at :2746,2752,2758,2764). But SECTION_MAP.office references the COMBINED refs — 'workingPeriod' (sectionMap.ts:139), 'establishmentPeriod' (:146), 'oldOfficeShiftedPeriod' (:155), 'currentCompanyPeriod' (:157). sections.ts mappedSections reads fieldsSource[ref] verbatim with NO Value/Unit recombination (sections.ts:117-118): the combined key is absent, so toValue() is null and the labeled row is silently omitted (no empty row, just gone from its section). The two split keys then fall through to the never-lose-a-field catch-all and render under 'Additional Details' (sections.ts:133-140) with auto-generated labels ('Working Period Value' / 'Working Period Unit') — split into two rows, in the wrong group, at the bottom of the report. canonicalize.ts DOES recombine (lines 106-113) but is applied ONLY to the narrative path (service.ts:55), NOT to buildSections (service.ts:28); and OFFICE has no active FIELD_REPORT layout (report_layouts on dev has only id1 FIELD_REPORT/RESIDENCE, id2 CASE_REPORT — verified via DB), so the raw section view is the ONLY thing rendered for OFFICE. Net UX: for POSITIVE, the 'Employment & Office Details' section is missing its 'Working Period' and 'Establishment Period' rows; for SHIFTED, the 'Shifting Details' section shows only 'Current Company Name' and is missing both 'Old Office Shifted Period' and 'Current Company Period' — and shift periods are the entire substance of a SHIFTED office report. This is distinct from registry AUDIT-2, which scopes the arity drift to the narrative template ('for the last … years' clauses) and assumes 'raw captured fields always still display' (registry line 848) — true pre-ADR-0060, but the 2026-06-22 sectionMap regrouping reintroduced the combined refs on the raw path, so the period fields now render dislocated/empty there too.

Combined refs in map: /Users/mayurkulkarni/Downloads/crm2/apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sectionMap.ts:139,146,155,157. No recombine on raw path: /Users/mayurkulkarni/Downloads/crm2/apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sections.ts:117-118 (omit), :133-140 (Additional-Details catch-all). Recombine exists but narrative-only: /Users/mayurkulkarni/Downloads/crm2/apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/canonicalize.ts:106-113 vs service.ts:28 (buildSections, raw) vs service.ts:55 (narrative). No OFFICE layout: DB report_layouts has no FIELD_REPORT/OFFICE row (queried crm2_dev). Registry framing scoped narrative-only + 'raw still shows': /Users/mayurkulkarni/Downloads/crm2/docs/COMPLIANCE_GAPS_REGISTRY.md:848,861-864.

findings.title: 'Generated Report' section always renders an empty 'No report template configured for OFFICE' placeholder on every OFFICE field report
findings.detail: FieldReportBody (CaseDetailPage.tsx:1859-1869) renders the 'Generated Report' <h3> and a 'No report template configured for {verificationType}.' paragraph UNCONDITIONALLY whenever the report has any fields (or a narrative). OFFICE always has fields and never has a narrative (no active FIELD_REPORT/OFFICE layout — DB-verified), so every OFFICE report shows a permanently empty placeholder section under a heading. This is informational-by-design (it tells admins a layout could be activated) and is not OFFICE-specific — it hits all 8 field types lacking a layout — so it is a minor empty-section wart rather than a data/grouping defect. Flagging for the lens (empty sections) and to note it compounds the visual confusion of OFFICE-UX-1 (the real period rows are missing above while a dead 'Generated Report' block sits below).
findings.evidence: /Users/mayurkulkarni/Downloads/crm2/apps/web/src/features/cases/CaseDetailPage.tsx:1859-1869 (heading + placeholder render whenever hasFields || narrative!==null; OFFICE narrative is always null). Confirmed no FIELD_REPORT/OFFICE layout via crm2_dev report_layouts query.

findings.title: Attachment tamper flag (hash_verified) is captured but never surfaced — a transit-tampered OFFICE field photo is indistinguishable from a verified one to any reviewer
findings.detail: The mobile app computes a client-side SHA-256 of each captured photo (CRM-APP-MONOREPO-PROD/crm-mobile-native/src/services/CameraService.ts:297, sent via AttachmentUploader.ts:96 as the `clientSha256` multipart field). The API verifies it against the bytes it actually received and stores the result: uploadAttachments computes `hashVerified = clientSha !== null && clientSha === sha256(f.buffer)` and, crucially, on mismatch ONLY logs a warning and STILL persists the photo with hash_verified=false (verification-tasks/service.ts:232-234,255) — a deliberate 'logged, never rejected' v1-parity choice. The integrity bit is then DEAD: no SELECT in apps/api/src/modules or apps/api/src/platform (non-test) ever reads hash_verified or client_sha256 to surface it. The web Field Photos card query (cases/repository.ts:1611-1631 listFieldPhotos) and the single/zip download queries (listFieldPhotoFiles :1639-1664) select geo/address/photoType/unit but NOT hash_verified; nothing in apps/web/src references hashVerified/client_sha256. Net: a desk reviewer adjudicating OFFICE evidence (and the generated field/case report) sees a photo whose transit hash FAILED exactly the same as a verified one — the evidence-grade tamper detection the device went to the trouble of computing produces no reviewer-visible or report-visible signal. Affects all 9 FIELD_VISIT types; reported here for the OFFICE track (every OFFICE submission requires >=5 photos + 1 selfie).
findings.evidence: WRITE side: apps/api/src/modules/verification-tasks/service.ts:232-234 (`const hashVerified = clientSha !== null && clientSha === sha256(f.buffer); if (clientSha && !hashVerified) logger.warn('field photo hash mismatch'...)`), :255 persists `hashVerified` via cases/repository.ts:1542-1556 (INSERT ... hash_verified ...). DEAD-READ side: `grep -rn 'hash_verified|hashVerified' apps/api/src/modules apps/api/src/platform | grep -i select` returns NOTHING (only the INSERT at repository.ts:1542 and the idempotency-replay FIELD_ATTACH_COLS at :1573, which is consumed by the device, not the desk). apps/web/src has zero references to hashVerified/client_sha256. DB proof (crm2_dev): `SELECT kind,count(*),count(*) FILTER(WHERE client_sha256 IS NOT NULL) AS has_hash,count(*) FILTER(WHERE hash_verified) AS verified FROM case_attachments GROUP BY kind` → FIELD_PHOTO: 6 rows, 6 have client hash, 6 verified=true — the column is populated and meaningful, just never read for display. Mobile sender: CRM-APP-MONOREPO-PROD/crm-mobile-native/src/services/CameraService.ts:297, src/sync/uploaders/AttachmentUploader.ts:90-96.

findings.title: Form-submit slug is not checked against the task's verification unit — a buggy/malicious owned device can store OFFICE form_data under a different slug (or vice-versa), mis-grouping the rendered report
findings.detail: submitForm validates only that the posted formType is one of the 9 LOCKED slugs (verification-tasks/service.ts:175 against FORM_TYPE_SLUGS :40-50); it never checks the slug matches the task's actual verification_unit code. submitVerificationForm then writes the blob with jsonb_set(form_data, ARRAY[$formType], ...) (cases/repository.ts:1428) under whatever slug was sent. The report render reads the WHOLE form_data jsonb and picks the section grouping by the slug key present (fieldReports/service.ts:28 buildSections(ctx.formData) + canonicalize), while the authoritative verificationType comes from vu.code (loadContext repository.ts:51). So a device that posts e.g. form_data['residence'] onto an OFFICE task would have those fields rendered under residence sections even though the unit is OFFICE — an evidence-integrity / mislabeling gap, not a leak. This is NOT reachable in the normal flow (the real app's FormUploader keys the endpoint by the task's own form type) and is fully bounded by ownership (ownedCaseId, service.ts:180) so it can never cross a user or case boundary; impact is confined to the agent's own task. Recording it because the server places no unit↔slug consistency constraint on stored evidence.
findings.evidence: No unit match in the submit path: apps/api/src/modules/verification-tasks/service.ts:174-185 (only `if (!FORM_TYPE_SLUGS.includes(formType)) throw UNKNOWN_FORM_TYPE`, then ownedCaseId, then store). Store is slug-blind: apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:1428 (`jsonb_set(COALESCE(form_data,'{}'), ARRAY[$4], $5::jsonb, true)`). Render trusts the slug present in the blob but the unit code is server-truth: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/repository.ts:51-52 (`'verificationType', vu.code, 'formData', ct.form_data`), service.ts:28 buildSections(ctx.formData). Ownership bound: service.ts:180 ownedCaseId → repository taskForAssignee (service.ts:57-61), so no cross-user/cross-case reach.

findings.title: Raw field-report section view orphans & mislabels every split-tenure field (Staying/Shifted/Business Period) — the only RCO report users see today
findings.detail: For RCO the always-rendered raw section view is the ONLY field report a desk user gets (no active RCO report_layout → narrative=null, service.ts:36-44). buildSections runs on ctx.formData BEFORE canonicalization (service.ts:28, canonicalize only feeds renderNarrative at :55). The RCO sectionMap places combined refs `stayingPeriod`/`shiftedPeriod`/`businessPeriod` (sectionMap.ts:317,321 + Business section), but the v2-native device persists SPLIT keys `stayingPeriodValue`+`stayingPeriodUnit` etc. (ADR-0057; mobile LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:1565-1574). So mappedSections does `toValue(fieldsSource['stayingPeriod'])` → null → the named 'Staying Period' / 'Shifted Period' / 'Business Period' rows are SKIPPED (sections.ts:117-118), and the split keys instead fall into the trailing 'Additional Details' bucket auto-labelled 'Staying Period Value' / 'Staying Period Unit' as TWO disconnected rows (sections.ts:133-140). Business meaning: a tenure like '8 Years' is shown split apart, out of its Met-Person/Occupancy section, under a generic catch-all — degraded and confusing, and it silently breaks the section-grouping spec's audited invariant ('0 in Additional Details' for every slug, field-report-section-grouping-2026-06-22.md:328). NOT data loss (values survive), but a presentation/correctness regression vs the spec'd grouped report. Affects RCO most visibly (no narrative to mask it) and every field type with split tenure.
findings.evidence: Spec expects combined ref: docs/engineering/field-report-section-grouping-2026-06-22.md:272 `{ ref: "stayingPeriod", label: "Staying Period", columnKey: "staying_period" }` + :328 'Coverage check … 0 in Additional Details'. sectionMap reads combined: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sectionMap.ts:317 {ref:'stayingPeriod'},:321 {ref:'shiftedPeriod'}, Business section {ref:'businessPeriod'}. Device persists SPLIT (proven on LIVE crm2_dev RESIDENCE blob, identical pattern to RCO): `PGPASSWORD=postgres psql … SELECT jsonb_pretty(ct.form_data) … RESIDENCE` → `"stayingPeriodUnit": "Year", "stayingPeriodValue": "8"` (no combined `stayingPeriod`). Mobile split source: crm-mobile-native src/screens/forms/LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:1565-1574 (stayingPeriodValue/Unit). Sections built pre-canonicalize: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/service.ts:28 buildSections(ctx.formData) vs :55 canonicalizeRenderContext only on narrative. Skip-on-null + Additional-Details spill: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sections.ts:115-118,133-140. API persists verbatim (no recombine): apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:1428 jsonb_set.

findings.title: RCO has no usable narrative report path on prod-dev — rich 8-branch RCO_BODY never reaches runtime (no seed/activation)
findings.detail: The RCO narrative template (RCO_BODY, all 8 outcome branches) lives in the SDK as FIELD_REPORT_DEFAULTS['RESIDENCE_CUM_OFFICE'] but is consumed ONLY by tests and a web 'Load standard template' convenience button (apps/web/src/features/reportLayouts/ReportLayoutRecordPage.tsx:410-432). No migration or seed inserts an RCO row into report_layouts; the runtime service NEVER falls back to the SDK defaults (fieldReportService.render has zero reference to FIELD_REPORT_DEFAULTS). On crm2_dev only id1 (FIELD_REPORT/RESIDENCE) + id2 (CASE_REPORT) are active — RCO has none — so every RCO field report returns narrative=null (service.ts:36-44) and renders only raw sections (which, per RCO-CEO-1, are themselves partly mislabeled). Business impact: until an admin manually creates AND activates an RCO FIELD_REPORT layout per (client,product), RCO delivers no narrative verification report — the core deliverable of a field verification. This is a platform-wide latent state (8 of 9 field types share it: only RESIDENCE is activated), but RCO is in scope and affected for all 5 outcomes. Flagging as a completeness/operational-readiness gap, not a code bug — the activation path exists, it just hasn't been exercised for RCO, and there is no fallback-to-defaults to make narratives work out of the box.
findings.evidence: DB (crm2_dev 2026-06-23): `SELECT id,kind,verification_type,is_active FROM report_layouts` → only (1,FIELD_REPORT,RESIDENCE,t) + (2,CASE_REPORT,,t); RCO absent. Runtime never uses defaults: `grep -rn FIELD_REPORT_DEFAULTS apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/service.ts` → no match; only test files reference it. null-on-missing: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/service.ts:36-44. SDK default exists but seed-only: packages/sdk/src/fieldReportDefaults.ts:1714 `RESIDENCE_CUM_OFFICE: { templateBody: RCO_BODY, columns: RCO_COLUMNS }`; RCO_BODY :763-896 (8 branches). Web load-default button (manual): apps/web/src/features/reportLayouts/ReportLayoutRecordPage.tsx:410-432. No INSERT into report_layouts in migrations: `grep -rln 'INTO report_layouts' apps/api/migrations` → no match.

findings.title: RCO report reads floor as `applicantStayingFloor` but mobile captures it as `addressFloor` — floor blank in narrative, mis-placed in sections
findings.detail: Field-name mismatch at the report hop. The crm2 SDK RCO report column set maps `applicant_staying_floor` to ref `applicantStayingFloor` (packages/sdk/src/fieldReportDefaults.ts:700) and the RCO narrative (RCO_BODY) reads `{{ordinal applicant_staying_floor}}` / `{{addressFloor applicant_staying_floor}}` in the POSITIVE (lines 769,798), SHIFTED (815,835), and NSP (849,866) branches. The RCO section map also lists only `applicantStayingFloor` (apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sectionMap.ts:323). But the mobile RCO forms capture the floor under field name `addressFloor` in EVERY outcome that records it — POSITIVE (LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:1686), SHIFTED (:1881), NSP (:2074) — there is NO `applicantStayingFloor` field anywhere in the RCO form range (verified: 0 occurrences in lines 1510-2350; 3 `addressFloor` fields). FormSubmissionService posts `{...formValues}` verbatim with no rename (FormSubmissionService.ts:74-82), so the value persists as form_data['residence-cum-office'].formData.addressFloor while the report reads the absent `applicantStayingFloor`. Result: (a) the narrative floor token renders empty for POSITIVE/SHIFTED/NSP; (b) the raw sectioned view does NOT lose the value but dumps it into the trailing 'Additional Details' catch-all (sections.ts:130,140) instead of the labeled 'Applicant Staying Floor' row in the 'Met Person & Occupancy' section. v1 source-of-truth confirms `addressFloor` is the canonical RCO field (CRM-BACKEND/src/utils/comprehensiveFormFieldMapping.ts RCO_FORM_FIELDS line ~3915 id/name `addressFloor`), so the SDK template is the porting error, not the mobile form. SYSTEMIC: the same `addressFloor`-vs-`applicantStayingFloor` split exists in residence/office/business/builder/noc/dsa families (all capture `addressFloor`, all SDK columns read `applicantStayingFloor`). RCO is latent (DB report_layouts has no active RESIDENCE_CUM_OFFICE FIELD_REPORT row — only id1 RESIDENCE + id2 CASE_REPORT active), but RESIDENCE — which IS active — exhibits this live today.
findings.evidence: MISMATCH SIDE A (report reads applicantStayingFloor): packages/sdk/src/fieldReportDefaults.ts:700 `FD('residence-cum-office','applicant_staying_floor','Applicant Staying Floor','applicantStayingFloor')`; fieldReportDefaults.ts:769,798,815,835,849,866 narrative `{{... applicant_staying_floor}}`; apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sectionMap.ts:323 `{ ref:'applicantStayingFloor' }`. MISMATCH SIDE B (mobile captures addressFloor): /Users/mayurkulkarni/Downloads/CRM-APP-MONOREPO-PROD/crm-mobile-native/src/screens/forms/LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:1686 (POSITIVE), :1881 (SHIFTED), :2074 (NSP) all `name: 'addressFloor'`; zero `applicantStayingFloor` fields in RCO range (awk NR 1510-2350 returns empty). NO alias: grep for addressFloor->applicantStayingFloor in crm2 fieldReports/sdk returns none; FormSubmissionService.ts:74-82 posts formValues verbatim. v1 SoT: CRM-BACKEND/src/utils/comprehensiveFormFieldMapping.ts RESIDENCE_CUM_OFFICE_FORM_FIELDS (3801+) uses `addressFloor`. DB: report_layouts has no active RCO FIELD_REPORT layout (only id1 RESIDENCE active) -> RCO narrative latent; RESIDENCE active -> live.

findings.title: Tenure (Staying/Business/Shifted Period) never renders in its curated section — split into two auto-labeled rows in the trailing "Additional Details" group on every RCO report
findings.detail: The RCO sectionMap places tenure under curated rows: 'Staying Period' (stayingPeriod) and 'Staying Status' in "Met Person & Occupancy", and 'Business Period' (businessPeriod) in "Business Details", plus 'Shifted Period' (shiftedPeriod). But the mobile RCO form splits every tenure into two separate fields — stayingPeriodValue + stayingPeriodUnit, businessPeriodValue + businessPeriodUnit, shiftedPeriodValue + shiftedPeriodUnit (LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:1564-1575, 1588-1599, 1824-1834, 2009-2021) — and submits them verbatim. The raw sectioned view buildSections() runs on the UNMODIFIED ctx.formData (service.ts:28) and matches refs literally, so stayingPeriod/businessPeriod/shiftedPeriod are always empty (toValue→null, skipped) while the split *Value/*Unit keys land in the never-lose-a-field "Additional Details" catch-all (sections.ts:133-140) with toLabel()-derived labels: "Staying Period Value", "Staying Period Unit", etc. Recombination into the combined token only happens in canonicalizeRenderContext (canonicalize.ts:106-113, PERIOD_BASES), which produces a SEPARATE cloned ctx used ONLY for the narrative path (service.ts:55) and never touches the formData buildSections already consumed. Because RCO has NO active FIELD_REPORT layout (DB: report_layouts has only id1 FIELD_REPORT/RESIDENCE active), narrative is null and the raw sectioned view IS the entire RCO report. Net UX: a credit-underwriting-critical datum renders as two disjoint rows ("Staying Period Value: 5" / "Staying Period Unit: Year") at the very bottom under a generic heading instead of "Staying Period: 5 Year" inside Met Person & Occupancy. Confirmed systemic to the raw view: sectionMap contains zero *Value/*Unit refs (grep count 0).
findings.evidence: Split fields: mobile LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:1564-1575 (stayingPeriodValue/stayingPeriodUnit POSITIVE), :1588-1599 (businessPeriodValue/Unit), :1824-1834 (shiftedPeriodValue/Unit), :2009-2021 (NSP staying). sectionMap combined refs: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sectionMap.ts:319 (stayingPeriod), :322 (shiftedPeriod), :333 (businessPeriod). Raw view runs on unmodified formData: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/service.ts:28 vs canonicalize-only narrative path :55. Recombination is narrative-only on a clone: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/canonicalize.ts:76-78,106-113,118. Catch-all auto-label: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sections.ts:133-140, toLabel :38-48. DB proof (ran): report_layouts has only id1 FIELD_REPORT/RESIDENCE + id2 CASE_REPORT active → RCO narrative null. grep: zero *Value/*Unit refs in sectionMap.

findings.title: RCO-captured fields addressLocatable, addressFloor, documentType have no RCO sectionMap row — fall to "Additional Details" with auto-labels (addressFloor/documentType are grouped for sibling types)
findings.detail: The mobile RCO form captures addressLocatable (required in POSITIVE/SHIFTED/NSP), addressFloor (required in POSITIVE/SHIFTED/NSP), and documentType (conditional on documentShown=Showed in POSITIVE), but the RCO sectionMap block (sectionMap.ts:301-391) omits all three. So in the raw sectioned view they are emitted by the never-lose-a-field catch-all under "Additional Details" with toLabel() auto-labels ("Address Locatable", "Address Floor", "Document Type") instead of sitting with their semantic group (e.g. addressFloor belongs in Property/Nameplace, documentType next to documentShown which IS mapped at :354). This is a grouping/labeling gap, not data loss. Notably addressFloor IS curated in 7 other slugs' sections (sectionMap.ts:193,285,449,619,711,792) and documentType is curated elsewhere (:75) — the RCO block simply forgot them. addressLocatable is absent from the ENTIRE sectionMap (grep count 0) so its auto-label "Address Locatable" reads acceptably, lowering its weight. There is also a harmless dead ref: sectionMap.ts:323 maps applicantStayingFloor which the RCO mobile form never produces (it captures applicantStayingStatus, no floor) — renders nothing, no defect, just noise.
findings.evidence: Mobile captures: LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts addressLocatable :1512/:1785/:1970, addressFloor :1686/:1881/:2074, documentType :1671-1677 (conditional documentShown=Showed). RCO sectionMap block apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sectionMap.ts:301-391 contains none of the three. Sibling-type curation proving the omission: addressFloor at sectionMap.ts:193,285,449,619,711,792; documentType at :75. grep: addressLocatable count 0 across whole sectionMap; documentType count 1 (non-RCO). Dead ref applicantStayingFloor: sectionMap.ts:323 (no mobile producer). Catch-all path: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sections.ts:133-140.

findings.title: TPC field labels drift between outcomes inside the RCO mobile form — SHIFTED/NSP mislabel the relation-select as "Third Party Confirmation" and the name-text as "TPC Met Person"
findings.detail: For the same two underlying fields, the RCO mobile form shows DIFFERENT and semantically wrong labels depending on outcome. tpcMetPerson1 is a select whose options are the third-party RELATION (Neighbour/Security/Other Staff, alias→tpcMetPerson, LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:1482-1483,2387) and tpcName1 is a free-text NAME. In POSITIVE they are labeled correctly: tpcMetPerson1 = "TPC Met Person 1" (:1627), tpcName1 = "Name of TPC 1" (:1629-1630). But in SHIFTED the relation-select tpcMetPerson1 is labeled "Third Party Confirmation 1" (:1836-1838) and the name-text tpcName1 is labeled "TPC Met Person" (:1841-1842); NSP repeats the same swap (:2023-2025 / :2028-2029). So the agent sees the person-type picker captioned as a "Confirmation" and the name box captioned "Met Person" — a confusing label swap that can produce mis-entry (agent may type a relation into the name field). The report sectionMap labels them sensibly and consistently (tpcMetPerson1→"TPC 1 Relation", tpcName1→"TPC 1 Name", sectionMap.ts:367-368), so the drift is purely on the capture side and the data still lands in the right report rows — but the mobile-capture experience is inconsistent across outcomes.

SHIFTED swapped labels: :1836-1838 (tpcMetPerson1 'Third Party Confirmation 1'), :1841-1842 (tpcName1 'TPC Met Person'). NSP swapped: :2023-2025, :2028-2029. tpcMetPerson1 is a relation select: alias :1482-1483 → options 'Neighbour','Security','Other Staff' :2387. Report labels (consistent): apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sectionMap.ts:367 (tpcName1 'TPC 1 Name'), :368 (tpcMetPerson1 'TPC 1 Relation').

findings.title: RCO has no active FIELD_REPORT layout, so the canonicalized v1 narrative (RCO_BODY) never renders — the raw sectioned view is the whole report (amplifies RCO-UX-1/2)
findings.detail: The SDK ships an RCO narrative body+columns (fieldReportDefaults.ts RCO_BODY) but it is a SEED source, not a runtime fallback: fieldReportService returns narrative=null when no active report_layout exists (service.ts:36-44), and the DB has no active FIELD_REPORT layout for RESIDENCE_CUM_OFFICE (verified: only id1 FIELD_REPORT/RESIDENCE + id2 CASE_REPORT active). Consequence for my lens: the polished, outcome-aware narrative (which would render "Staying Period: 5 Year" via canonicalize and a door-aware label like "Positive & Door Open") is dormant, so the ONLY thing a user sees for an RCO task is the raw sectioned view — which is exactly where RCO-UX-1 (split tenure in Additional Details) and RCO-UX-2 (un-grouped fields) bite. Not a defect of the RCO mapping itself (8 of 9 FIELD types share this latent state) and the activation/seeding pipeline is owned by the report-layout admin path, so flagging as INFO context that raises the practical severity of RCO-UX-1.
findings.evidence: narrative=null when no layout: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/service.ts:36-44. SDK RCO body is seed-only (no runtime fallback wired): packages/sdk/src/fieldReportDefaults.ts RCO_BODY (~:1714). DB proof (ran): SELECT id,kind,verification_type,is_active FROM report_layouts → only (1,FIELD_REPORT,RESIDENCE,t) and (2,CASE_REPORT,,t); no RESIDENCE_CUM_OFFICE row.

findings.title: FIELD_REPORT route is case-grain scoped, not task-grain — a field agent reads a sibling task's full PII form_data (cross-agent IDOR)
findings.detail: GET /api/v2/cases/:id/tasks/:taskId/field-report is gated case.view (which FIELD_AGENT holds — DB role_permissions: FIELD_AGENT|case.view) and then loads the task via fieldReportRepository.loadContext, whose ONLY scope guard is the case-grain caseScopePredicate: a case is visible iff `cs.created_by = ANY(scope.userIds) OR EXISTS(a task on the case assigned to an in-scope user)`. The requested `ct.id = $1` is NOT required to be assigned to the actor. FIELD_AGENT resolves to hierarchyMode=SELF → scope.userIds=[self]. So a field agent assigned task T1 (e.g. an OFFICE/KYC task) on case C satisfies the EXISTS leg, and requesting the field-report for a SIBLING RCO task T2 on C — assigned to a DIFFERENT field agent — returns T2's full TaskRenderContext: applicant.name/mobile/pan (repository.ts:81-87), the device-submitted RCO form_data blob (met-person name, relation, residence/business setup, occupancy, telephonic, TPC, the GPS-bearing evidence) and address. This is the classic IDOR shape (right perm, wrong object). It is INCONSISTENT with the field-PHOTO routes on the same case (listFieldPhotos / listFieldPhotoFiles / fieldPhotoForGeocode at repository.ts:1611,1639,1736), which all use the TASK-grain taskScopePredicate via `EXISTS(... ct.id = ca.task_id AND <task-scope>)` and therefore correctly restrict a SELF agent to their OWN tasks' photos. The form_data (text PII) is thus more broadly exposed than the photos of the very same sibling task. DB confirms reachability: there is no constraint forcing one agent per case (pg_constraint on case_tasks shows only value CHECKs, no per-case-assignee uniqueness), cases already carry multiple tasks, and assignment is per-task (eligibleAssignees / assignableUsers are :taskId routes). No mobile consumer exists (grep of crm-mobile-native for field-report = none), so exploitation is a direct API call by an authenticated field agent — but it is a real cross-user PII disclosure, not theoretical. Applies to all 9 FIELD_VISIT types and every RCO outcome (POSITIVE/SHIFTED/NSP/ENTRY_RESTRICTED/UNTRACEABLE).
findings.evidence: LEAK PATH: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/repository.ts:25-33 (caseScopePredicate — case-grain OR-leg) + :96 WHERE clause `WHERE ct.id = $1 AND ct.case_id = $2 ${pred}` (requested task NOT ownership-filtered) + :81-87 (applicant name/mobile/pan returned) + :52 (ct.form_data returned). Service has no extra guard: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/service.ts:23-25 (resolveScope → loadContext, then renders). CORRECT COUNTEREXAMPLE on the same case: apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:1614-1619 / 1642-1647 / 1746-1752 use taskScopePredicate (task-grain) for photos. SCOPE FACTS (DB crm2_dev): `SELECT code,hierarchy_mode FROM roles` → FIELD_AGENT|SELF; `role_permissions` → FIELD_AGENT|case.view present; taskScopePredicate hierarchy leg = `ct.assigned_to = ANY($) OR cs.created_by = ANY($)` (apps/api/src/platform/scope/index.ts:84). Test gap: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/__tests__/fieldReports.api.test.ts:201-218 asserts only cross-CASE isolation using SUPER_ADMIN (ALL scope) — the same-case cross-AGENT case is untested.

findings.title: Attachment tamper signal (hash_verified) is captured at upload but never surfaced — mismatched photo evidence renders identically to verified
findings.detail: On device photo upload the service computes `hashVerified = clientSha !== null && clientSha === sha256(receivedBytes)`, logs a warning on mismatch, and PERSISTS the result: case_attachments stores sha256 (server hash of stored bytes), client_sha256, and hash_verified (DB columns confirmed). This is a correct integrity capture across the upload boundary (verified-or-logged-never-rejected — the photo is evidence). However, hash_verified is never read back into any web-facing surface: the CaseFieldPhoto SDK type (packages/sdk/src/cases.ts:599-610) carries id/taskId/unitName/photoType/originalName/geoLocation/reverseGeocodedAddress/createdAt but NO hashVerified, listFieldPhotos' SELECT (apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:1622-1624) omits it, and no web code references it (grep of apps/web/src for hashVerified/hash_verified/tamper/integrity → only an unrelated DataGrid hit). Net: a photo whose client-supplied hash did NOT match the bytes the server received — the exact signal of in-transit tampering or a corrupted/substituted artifact — is shown in the Field Photos card and report indistinguishably from a verified photo. The tamper-detection mechanism exists but produces no actionable output for the verifier; only a server log line records it. Affects FIELD_PHOTO evidence for all 9 FIELD types incl every RCO outcome that requires the 5 photos + selfie.
findings.evidence: Capture+persist: apps/api/src/modules/verification-tasks/service.ts:232-234 (hashVerified compute + warn-only log) and :253-255 (sha256/clientSha256/hashVerified passed to insertFieldAttachment). DB: `information_schema.columns table_name='case_attachments'` → sha256, client_sha256, hash_verified all present. Not surfaced: packages/sdk/src/cases.ts:599-610 (CaseFieldPhoto lacks hashVerified); apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:1621-1631 (listFieldPhotos SELECT omits hash_verified); grep apps/web/src for hashVerified/tamper/integrity → no field-photo reference.

findings.title: Business Approx Area renders EMPTY in both report views — mobile emits officeApproxArea, the BUSINESS report reads approxArea (and the render test hides it with a wrong-key fixture)
findings.detail: The mobile BUSINESS POSITIVE form captures the business floor area under the key `officeApproxArea` (CRM-MOBILE LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:3398; it is the ONLY area key in the whole business field-set 3310-4066 — grep-confirmed, no `approxArea`/`businessApproxArea`). But the v2 BUSINESS report reads `approxArea` on BOTH projections: the raw-section row 'Business Approx Area' (sectionMap.ts:238 ref `approxArea`) and the narrative column `business_approx_area`→`approxArea` (fieldReportDefaults.ts:503), consumed by the narrative clause 'The business area is approximately {{area business_approx_area}}' (fieldReportDefaults.ts:556). Net product effect: the agent measures the shop area, but the client-facing BUSINESS report shows NO area in the 'Business Details' section AND an empty area sentence in the generated narrative; the captured value instead surfaces, mislabeled, under the 'Additional Details' catch-all as 'Office Approx Area' (sections.ts:130-140). This is BUSINESS-specific: office/builder/dsa correctly read `officeApproxArea` (sectionMap.ts:149/570/665) and RCO correctly reads `approxArea` (its mobile form emits `approxArea`, LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts ~1658) — only BUSINESS has the read-key vs emit-key crossed. It is also masked by the test suite: the BUSINESS render test feeds `approxArea: 600` (a key the real device never emits) and asserts 'business area is approximately 600 sq. feet' (defaults.office-family.render.test.ts:155,188), so the suite is green while production renders empty. v1 canonical BUSINESS_FORM_FIELDS used `businessApproxArea` (comprehensiveFormFieldMapping.ts:1068) — neither the report nor the mobile form matches v1's key, so this is a genuine v2 fidelity regression for the business area datum. The raw-section defect fires on ANY submitted business form (no layout needed); the narrative defect fires once a BUSINESS layout is activated.

findings.title: Raw-section 'Business Period' is empty for every submitted BUSINESS form — sections.ts does not recombine the split businessPeriodValue/Unit (only the narrative does)
findings.detail: The mobile BUSINESS form emits the business tenure ONLY as split `businessPeriodValue` + `businessPeriodUnit` (LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:3386,3392); there is no combined `businessPeriod` field anywhere in the business field-set (grep-confirmed). The narrative path recombines it (canonicalize.ts:107-113 with `businessPeriod` in PERIOD_BASES:43), so the narrative clause '{{pluralize business_period}}' is fine. But the raw-section view does NOT run canonicalize — `buildSections` reads `fieldsSource[ref]` directly (sections.ts:117), and the BUSINESS sectionMap row 'Business Period' references the combined `businessPeriod` (sectionMap.ts:234). So the named 'Business Period' row in the 'Business Details' section renders EMPTY on every business submission, while the actual value shows in 'Additional Details' as two rows 'Business Period Value' + 'Business Period Unit'. Same divergence applies to currentCompanyPeriod and oldOfficeShiftedPeriod for SHIFTED (split-emitted at LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:3593/3606/3619/3625, but read combined at sectionMap.ts:247-249). Product effect: the structured business report shows a split, unfriendly tenure instead of the labelled 'Business Period: 3 Years'. Latent on the narrative side (no layout) but the section-view defect is live on every submission. Same root cause as BIZ-1: the raw-section projection lacks the period recombination the narrative has.

findings.title: Raw-section 'Verification Outcome' shows the uppercase v2 CODE (POSITIVE) while the narrative shows the v1 label (Positive & Door Open) — inconsistent outcome display within one report
findings.detail: Within a single rendered field-report, the leading 'Verification Outcome' section row shows the un-canonicalized device CODE, e.g. 'POSITIVE'/'ENTRY_RESTRICTED'/'UNTRACEABLE' (sections.ts:154 reads `blob['verificationOutcome']` verbatim; service.ts:28 calls buildSections on the RAW ctx, NOT the canonicalized one), whereas the 'Generated Report' narrative below it — once a BUSINESS layout is active — says the v1 verbose label 'Positive & Door Open' (canonicalize.ts:98-99 via deriveLabel). So the same report surfaces the outcome two different ways. The web render does no normalization (CaseDetailPage.tsx:1853-1854 prints the value as-is). This is a presentation-consistency / polish issue, not a data-loss one (no field dropped), and the uppercase code is at least unambiguous. Flagging for the lens because a client-facing BUSINESS report showing 'POSITIVE' in one block and 'Positive & Door Open' in another reads as unpolished; confirm whether the section row should show the display label (verification_unit_outcomes.display_label='Positive', DB-confirmed) instead of the raw code.

findings.title: Two captured BUSINESS POSITIVE fields (addressStatus, addressLocatable) have no named-section or narrative home — always dumped to 'Additional Details'
findings.detail: The mobile BUSINESS POSITIVE form captures `addressLocatable` (LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:3312) and `addressStatus` (premises-held status, :3373). Neither appears in any BUSINESS section of the sectionMap (sectionMap.ts:209-299 — grep-confirmed empty for both) nor in the business narrative columns (fieldReportDefaults.ts:490-541 — grep-confirmed absent), so both always land in the trailing 'Additional Details' catch-all (sections.ts:130-140) and never appear in the narrative at all. `addressStatus` IS modelled for the sibling builder/dsa types as 'Premises Held Status' (sectionMap.ts:571,664), so the omission is a per-type gap, not an intentional drop. findings.title: BUSINESS area DROPPED from BOTH report projections: device emits `officeApproxArea`, sectionMap + narrative both read `approxArea`
findings.detail: For FIELD_VISIT BUSINESS, outcome POSITIVE, the mobile form field for the premises area is named `officeApproxArea` (the BUSINESS field-sets reuse the office-family builder). The crm2 report side reads `approxArea` in BOTH independent projections: (1) the raw-section row labelled 'Business Approx Area' has ref `approxArea`; (2) the narrative column `business_approx_area` maps to device key `approxArea`, and the narrative body emits `{{area business_approx_area}}`. canonicalize.ts does NOT alias officeApproxArea->approxArea (its only key transforms are the outcome label and split-tenure recombination). Net: the agent's captured business area renders EMPTY in the 'Business Details' section row AND empty in the narrative 'business area is approximately ...' clause. The value survives only in the raw-view 'Additional Details' catch-all (mislabelled 'Office Approx Area'); in the narrative it is lost entirely. Contrast office/builder/dsa-connector, whose section + narrative correctly use `officeApproxArea` (sectionMap.ts:149; fieldReportDefaults.ts:316/1168/1365) — BUSINESS is the lone type whose report refs disagree with its own device emission. ARCHITECTURE-FROZEN-SAFE FIX: change the two report-side refs to `officeApproxArea` (sectionMap.ts:238 ref + fieldReportDefaults.ts:503 device key), OR add `officeApproxArea`->`approxArea` to a canonicalize alias and also fix the raw-section ref; do NOT change the frozen mobile contract or stored blobs.
