findings.title: Raw-section 'Business Period' renders empty: device emits split businessPeriodValue/Unit, sectionMap reads combined `businessPeriod` which only the narrative recombines
findings.detail: The mobile BUSINESS POSITIVE form emits the business tenure ONLY as split fields `businessPeriodValue` + `businessPeriodUnit`; no combined `businessPeriod` field is emitted (grep-confirmed: zero `name: 'businessPeriod'` in the builders). The narrative path recombines these via canonicalize.ts PERIOD_BASES (which includes 'businessPeriod'), so `{{pluralize business_period}}` renders correctly. But the raw-section projection (sections.ts mappedSections) does NOT recombine split tenures — it reads `fieldsSource['businessPeriod']` directly. Result: the 'Business Details' section row 'Business Period' (ref `businessPeriod`) renders EMPTY; the captured value surfaces only under the 'Additional Details' catch-all as two rows 'Business Period Value' + 'Business Period Unit'. This is a raw-view/narrative divergence — the same tenure shows recombined in the narrative but split-and-unlabelled in the section table. Value not lost, but mis-sectioned and inconsistent between the two projections shown on the same page. FROZEN-SAFE FIX: have sections.ts apply the same PERIOD_BASES recombination canonicalize.ts already implements (lift it to a shared helper) before reading section refs; affects all split-tenure refs across all FIELD types, not just BUSINESS.
findings.evidence: DEVICE SPLIT EMISSION: LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:3386 (name:'businessPeriodValue') + 3392 (name:'businessPeriodUnit'); grep `name: 'businessPeriod'` = no matches. NARRATIVE RECOMBINES: canonicalize.ts:43 (PERIOD_BASES includes 'businessPeriod') + 107-113 recombine loop. RAW SECTION DOES NOT: sections.ts:106-142 mappedSections reads fieldsSource[ref] only, no period recombination; sectionMap.ts:234 `{ ref: 'businessPeriod', label: 'Business Period' }`. Narrative consumer fieldReportDefaults.ts:499 FD('business','business_period',...,'businessPeriod') + body 553 `{{pluralize business_period}}`.

findings.title: BUSINESS POSITIVE device fields `addressLocatable` + `addressStatus` (premises-held) are in NO business section and NO narrative column -> only the Additional-Details catch-all
findings.detail: The mobile BUSINESS POSITIVE field-set emits `addressLocatable` (line 3312) and `addressStatus` (line 3373, label 'Address Status', the premises-held/owned-rented status). Neither key appears in the business SECTION_MAP (sectionMap.ts:209-299) nor in the business narrative columns (fieldReportDefaults.ts:490-541). So both render only in the raw-view 'Additional Details' catch-all (correctly preventing data loss via sections.ts:130-140) and are absent from the narrative entirely. Note the inconsistency: builder and dsa-connector DO map their `addressStatus` to a 'Premises Held Status' section row + narrative column (sectionMap.ts:571/664; fieldReportDefaults.ts:1169/1364) — BUSINESS omits the equivalent mapping even though its POSITIVE form captures the same field. Lowest impact (value reaches Additional Details in the raw view), but the premises-held status never reaches the narrative and is never placed in a named section. FROZEN-SAFE FIX: add `addressStatus` (label 'Premises Held Status') to the business 'Business Details' section + a business narrative column, mirroring builder/dsa; `addressLocatable` is intentionally a gate field and can stay in Additional Details.
findings.evidence: DEVICE EMITS: LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:3312 (addressLocatable) and 3373 (addressStatus, label 'Address Status'). ABSENT FROM REPORT: grep over business section (sectionMap.ts:209-299) and business narrative columns (fieldReportDefaults.ts:490-541) returns only businessPeriod + approxArea — no addressStatus/addressLocatable. Builder/DSA DO map it: sectionMap.ts:571 `{ ref:'addressStatus', label:'Premises Held Status' }`, fieldReportDefaults.ts:1169 FD('builder','address_status','Premises Held Status','addressStatus'). Catch-all path: sections.ts:130-140.

findings.title: HOP 2 down-sync and HOP 4 up-sync verified CLEAN for BUSINESS — no field renamed/dropped/reshaped at those boundaries
findings.detail: Refuting the two non-report candidate concerns for this track. (1) DOWN-SYNC: the mobile upsert copies the full task contract column-by-column from the bare v2 envelope, including verificationUnit.{id,name,code} (code='BUSINESS'), verificationOutcome (null for a fresh task), and the entire formData JSON via JSON.stringify; reconcileTaskIdentity runs AFTER the upsert and only folds stale-id sibling rows by canonical id, touching no task field columns; for a fresh BUSINESS task formData/verificationOutcome are correctly null. No BUSINESS field is dropped or renamed at HOP 2. (2) UP-SYNC: the device blob is persisted verbatim under slug 'business' with jsonb_set and NO key normalization, so whatever device keys are emitted (incl the F1/F2/F3 keys) land in cases.form_data.business.formData exactly as sent — meaning the F1/F2/F3 mismatches are purely report-side ref bugs, not storage corruption. The 4 sync fixes this session (reconcile fold, watermark UPSERT preservation, stall lock, plain-language summary) do not alter task field columns. The outcome-CASING concern is also benign by design: the raw-section 'Verification Outcome' row shows the uppercase v2 CODE because sections.ts:154 reads the raw blob and only the narrative runs canonicalize — this is intended (raw view = as-captured; narrative = verbose label).
findings.evidence: DOWN-SYNC upsert: /Users/mayurkulkarni/Downloads/CRM-APP-MONOREPO-PROD/crm-mobile-native/src/sync/SyncDownloadService.ts:617-632 (verificationUnit.name/id/code + verificationOutcome + formData JSON.stringify), reconcile at 650 (post-upsert). v2 contract: packages/sdk/src/sync.ts:21-63 (MobileSyncTask). UP-SYNC verbatim store: apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:1418-1443 (jsonb_set, no normalization). Casing-by-design: sections.ts:124-128/154 (raw outcome) vs canonicalize.ts:96-99 (label only in narrative).

findings.title: Business 'Approx Area' renders EMPTY in BOTH the report section and the narrative — mobile submits officeApproxArea, report reads approxArea
findings.detail: For BUSINESS POSITIVE, the device captures the business floor area under the key 'officeApproxArea' (label 'Office Approx Area (Sq. Feet)'). The web report's BUSINESS section map row 'Business Approx Area' reads ref 'approxArea' (sectionMap.ts:238), and the BUSINESS narrative column 'business_approx_area' ALSO reads source key 'approxArea' (fieldReportDefaults.ts:503). The mobile BUSINESS form (lines 3300-4076) emits 'officeApproxArea' exactly once and never plain 'approxArea' (grep-confirmed: 1x officeApproxArea, 0x approxArea in that range). Net: the named 'Business Approx Area' row in the Business Details section is always empty, the narrative token {{area business_approx_area}} is always empty, and the captured value instead surfaces in the trailing 'Additional Details' catch-all (sections.ts:130-140) labeled 'Office Approx Area' (toLabel('officeApproxArea')). This is a double mismatch — the shared tracer flagged only the section row; the narrative column is broken identically. Designer fix: point the BUSINESS section row and the business_approx_area column at 'officeApproxArea' (or add a recombine/alias).
findings.evidence: mobile CRM-APP-MONOREPO-PROD/crm-mobile-native/src/screens/forms/LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:3398 (name 'officeApproxArea') vs crm2 apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sectionMap.ts:238 (ref 'approxArea') AND packages/sdk/src/fieldReportDefaults.ts:503 (FD('business','business_approx_area','Business Approx Area','approxArea')). Grep over LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts lines 3300-4076: officeApproxArea=1, 'approxArea'=0. findings.title: Split tenure rows (Business Period / Old Business Shifted Period / Current Company Period) render EMPTY in the raw report sections — only the narrative recombines them
findings.detail: The device emits business tenures only as split halves: businessPeriodValue+businessPeriodUnit (POSITIVE), oldOfficeShiftedPeriodValue+Unit and currentCompanyPeriodValue+Unit (SHIFTED). No combined 'businessPeriod' field is ever submitted. The narrative path recombines them (canonicalize.ts:39-48 PERIOD_BASES includes businessPeriod/oldOfficeShiftedPeriod/currentCompanyPeriod; recombine loop canonicalize.ts:107-113). But the RAW SECTION projection (sections.ts -> buildSections/mappedSections) has NO recombine logic at all (grep confirmed: no Value/Unit field handling in sections.ts). So the section rows 'Business Period' (ref businessPeriod, sectionMap.ts:234), 'Old Business Shifted Period' (ref oldOfficeShiftedPeriod, sectionMap.ts:247) and 'Current Company Period' (ref currentCompanyPeriod, sectionMap.ts:249) are always empty, and the split halves instead show up in 'Additional Details' as 'Business Period Value' / 'Business Period Unit' / etc. Since the narrative is latent in dev (no BUSINESS layout), the section projection is the ONLY rendered surface today -> the user sees split, mislabeled period halves and empty named rows. Designer fix: recombine the split tenures in sections.ts the same way canonicalize.ts does, or add the split-half keys to the section map.
findings.evidence: mobile LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:3386-3396 (businessPeriodValue/businessPeriodUnit), :3589-3611 (currentCompanyPeriodValue/Unit, oldOfficeShiftedPeriodValue/Unit) vs crm2 apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sectionMap.ts:234,247,249 (refs businessPeriod/oldOfficeShiftedPeriod/currentCompanyPeriod). Recombine exists ONLY in canonicalize.ts:39-48,107-113; sections.ts has no Value/Unit recombination (grep).

findings.title: addressLocatable (4 of 5 outcomes) and addressStatus (POSITIVE) are never placed in a named report section — they fall into Additional Details
findings.detail: The device captures 'addressLocatable' in POSITIVE/SHIFTED/NSP/ENTRY_RESTRICTED (4 of 5 BUSINESS outcomes; grep: 4 occurrences in lines 3300-4076) and 'addressStatus' in POSITIVE (1 occurrence). Neither key appears in ANY BUSINESS section in the section map (sectionMap.ts:209-299 — grep for addressLocatable/addressStatus across business returns nothing) nor in the BUSINESS narrative columns (fieldReportDefaults.ts BUSINESS_COLUMNS 490-541 — grep returns nothing). Both therefore land in the trailing 'Additional Details' catch-all (sections.ts:130-140), labeled 'Address Locatable' and 'Address Status'. 'addressLocatable' is a core verification answer (was the address found) that arguably belongs in the leading 'Verification Outcome & Status' section, not dumped at the bottom ungrouped. Not data loss, but mis-grouping that degrades the report's information hierarchy for nearly every business outcome. Designer fix: add ref 'addressLocatable' (and 'addressStatus') to the appropriate BUSINESS section in sectionMap.ts.
findings.evidence: mobile LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:3312 (addressLocatable POSITIVE), :3375 (addressStatus POSITIVE), plus addressLocatable in SHIFTED/NSP/ERT (grep count 4 in 3300-4076) vs crm2 sectionMap.ts:209-299 (no addressLocatable/addressStatus ref in any business section) and fieldReportDefaults.ts:490-541 (no address_locatable/address_status column). Catch-all path sections.ts:130-140.

findings.title: Raw report 'Verification Outcome' row shows the UPPERCASE v2 code (e.g. 'POSITIVE') while the narrative would say a verbose label ('Positive & Door Open') — inconsistent outcome presentation
findings.detail: In the raw section projection, the leading 'Verification Outcome' row is set directly from the raw blob['verificationOutcome'] (sections.ts:154, mappedSections unshift sections.ts:124-128) with NO canonicalization, so it shows the v2 code in caps: 'POSITIVE'/'SHIFTED'/'NSP'/'ENTRY_RESTRICTED'/'UNTRACEABLE'. Only the narrative path runs canonicalizeRenderContext (canonicalize.ts:76-119) which maps each code to the v1 verbose label ('Positive & Door Open'/'Shifted & Door Locked'/'ERT'/'Untraceable', deriveLabel canonicalize.ts:56-72). So the same report shows two different spellings of the outcome — an uppercase code in the section header and a verbose label inside the generated narrative (once a BUSINESS layout is activated). For the BUSINESS user-facing report, 'POSITIVE'/'ENTRY_RESTRICTED' in caps reads as a raw enum, not a human label. Designer call: canonicalize the section-header outcome row too, or confirm the uppercase code is the intended section display.
findings.evidence: crm2 apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sections.ts:124-128,154 (raw outcome unshift, no canonicalize) vs canonicalize.ts:56-72 (deriveLabel: POSITIVE->'Positive & Door {Open|Locked}', ENTRY_RESTRICTED->'ERT', etc., applied only inside renderNarrative). Web renders f.value verbatim at CaseDetailPage.tsx:1854-1858.

findings.title: FIELD_AGENT EXPAND (PINCODE/AREA) broadens case.view to other agents' case PII — case-view path lacks the assigned_to=self hard filter that sync has
findings.detail: FIELD_AGENT holds case.view (DB role_permissions: FIELD_AGENT -> {case.view, location.capture, task.execute}) AND has PINCODE+AREA wired as EXPAND scope dimensions (DB role_scope_dimensions). resolveScope (platform/scope/repository.ts:104-140) therefore produces scope={userIds:[self], expand:{PINCODE/AREA:[...]}}. composeScopePredicate (platform/scope/index.ts:49-69) ORs the EXPAND legs onto the hierarchy leg with no RESTRICT, and the PINCODE/AREA dimensions only have a casePredicate `cs.pincode_id = ANY` / `cs.area_id = ANY` with no taskPredicate (dimensions.ts:59-72). So the field-report caseScopePredicate (fieldReports/repository.ts:25-33) and the cases list/detail caseScopePredicate (cases/repository.ts:318-324) both evaluate to `(cs.created_by = ANY(self) OR assigned-task = ANY(self) OR cs.pincode_id = ANY(myPincodes) OR cs.area_id = ANY(myAreas))`. Result: a FIELD_AGENT with a PINCODE/AREA assignment can read the field-report (BUSINESS form_data incl. applicant name/mobile/PAN — fieldReports/repository.ts:47-96), the field-photos list + download + zip + GPS, and full case-detail for ANY case in their territory, INCLUDING cases/tasks assigned to a DIFFERENT field agent. This contradicts the system's own siloing model: down-sync is hard-bound to `ct.assigned_to = $1` (sync/repository.ts:98) and its comment (sync/repository.ts:50-53) asserts 'a device can only ever receive its own assigned tasks -> inherently leak-proof' — but the case.view web/API surface the same user can call has no equivalent assigned_to gate, only the OR-broadening EXPAND predicate. The mismatch is reachable via the same JWT the mobile app already issues to FIELD_AGENT.
findings.evidence: DB: role_permissions FIELD_AGENT={case.view,location.capture,task.execute}; role_scope_dimensions: FIELD_AGENT|PINCODE|EXPAND and FIELD_AGENT|AREA|EXPAND; user_scope_assignments: smokefa AND smokefb both AREA entity_id=1 (role FIELD_AGENT). Code: apps/api/src/platform/scope/index.ts:49-69 (OR-broadening, no hard self-filter); apps/api/src/platform/scope/dimensions.ts:59-72 (PINCODE/AREA casePredicate only, no taskPredicate); apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/repository.ts:25-33,96 (field-report scope = created_by OR assigned OR expand); apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:318-324 (same shape for case list/detail, no assigned_to=self hard filter); contrast apps/api/src/modules/sync/repository.ts:98 (hard `ct.assigned_to = $1`). Route guard apps/api/src/modules/cases/routes.ts:74-77 = authorize(CASE_VIEW) only.

findings.title: Attachment tamper signal (client_sha256/hash_verified) is stored + warn-logged but NEVER surfaced to any reviewer — a swapped/altered BUSINESS evidence photo is invisible to the office
findings.detail: On field-photo upload, the device's client_sha256 is checked against the bytes the server received; a mismatch is only `logger.warn('field photo hash mismatch', ...)` and the boolean is persisted to case_attachments.hash_verified — it is NEVER rejected (verification-tasks/service.ts:231-234,255) and NEVER read back. The office-facing field-photos list type CaseFieldPhoto (sdk/cases.ts:599-610) exposes id/taskId/unitName/photoType/originalName/geoLocation/reverseGeocodedAddress/createdAt — but NOT hashVerified or client_sha256. A repo-wide grep finds zero SELECT/response path that reads hash_verified. Compounding it, the server-side evidence hash (sha256, service.ts:253) is computed over the EXIF-STRIPPED, sharp-RE-ENCODED artifact (platform/photo.ts:25-31), not the original bytes — so the stored hash cannot attest the photo was not altered before upload; the only transit-integrity signal is hash_verified, which no reviewer ever sees. Net: for BUSINESS (which mandates >=5 field photos as the core evidence), a transit-tampered or re-encoded/substituted photo passes silently — there is no UI/report/audit surface for a failed integrity check.
findings.evidence: apps/api/src/modules/verification-tasks/service.ts:231-234 (warn, never reject), :253-255 (stores sha256 of stripped bytes + clientSha256 + hashVerified); apps/api/src/platform/photo.ts:25-31 (re-encode strips metadata -> server hash != original); packages/sdk/src/cases.ts:599-610 (CaseFieldPhoto omits hashVerified); DB `\d case_attachments` shows hash_verified + client_sha256 columns present; repo-wide grep for hash_verified/hashVerified in apps/api+apps/web+packages/sdk returns NO read/SELECT/response usage (only insert + the column comment).

findings.title: verification_units.pii_sensitive is a decorative admin flag — no enforcement in any access/masking/logging/retention path
findings.detail: The pii_sensitive boolean on verification_units is only ever written/read inside the verificationUnits CRUD module (service.ts:122,154,189; repository.ts:11,117,138,193,216) and mirrored in the SDK type (verificationUnit.ts:50,133). A repo-wide grep for piiSensitive/pii_sensitive across apps/api/src and packages/sdk/src finds it NOWHERE in sync, field-report, attachment, download, logging, masking, or retention logic. So even a unit flagged pii_sensitive=true gets no differential PII handling (no masking, no tighter scope, no redaction in logs). BUSINESS itself is pii_sensitive=false (DB-proven), so this track is unaffected in practice, but the flag gives a false impression of a PII control that does not exist. Flagging as INFO since the lens calls out 'PII handling per type (pii_sensitive units)' and the answer is: the flag does nothing.
findings.evidence: DB: verification_units pii_sensitive boolean NOT NULL default false; BUSINESS row pii_sensitive=f. Code references confined to apps/api/src/modules/verificationUnits/{service.ts:122,154,189; repository.ts:11,117,138,193,216} and packages/sdk/src/verificationUnit.ts:50,133. grep for piiSensitive/pii_sensitive excluding the verificationUnits module and tests across apps/api/src + packages/sdk/src = no matches (no enforcement).

findings.title: BUILDER report section-view orphans split tenure fields (Business/Current-Company/Old-Office Period) into mislabeled 'Additional Details' rows — the only view rendered for BUILDER today
findings.detail: The mobile BUILDER form emits every tenure as SPLIT tokens — businessPeriodValue/businessPeriodUnit (POSITIVE/NSP), currentCompanyPeriodValue/Unit + oldOfficeShiftedPeriodValue/Unit (SHIFTED) — never the combined token. The report section view (buildSections -> mappedSections) runs on RAW, non-canonicalized formData and the BUILDER sectionMap refs the COMBINED names ('businessPeriod' label 'Business Period', 'currentCompanyPeriod', 'oldOfficeShiftedPeriod'). Those combined keys are always null in the raw blob, so the intended named rows ('Business Period' under 'Builder / Business Details', 'Current Company Period'/'Old Office Shifted Period' under 'Shifting Details') are silently dropped; the split *Value/*Unit keys instead fall into the trailing 'Additional Details' catch-all as two separate auto-labeled rows ('Business Period Value' + 'Business Period Unit', etc.). Recombination exists ONLY in canonicalize.ts, which runs ONLY on the narrative path (when a layout exists). BUILDER has NO active FIELD_REPORT layout (DB report_layouts: only id1 RESIDENCE active), so the section view IS the BUILDER report — the verifier sees tenure split across two mislabeled, mis-sectioned rows instead of one clean clause. No data loss (catch-all keeps the values), but it is a real presentation/parity regression vs v1's combined tenure clause. Affects POSITIVE & SHIFTED (and NSP for businessPeriod). NOT covered by registry AUDIT-2 (scoped to the narrative generator) and contradicts the registry's 'raw-field display ... nothing dropped PASS'. Same pattern affects all 8 field types with split-period fields and no active layout; scoped here to BUILDER.
findings.evidence: Device emits split only: crm-mobile-native/src/screens/forms/LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:4184 businessPeriodValue, :4190 businessPeriodUnit (POSITIVE); currentCompanyPeriodValue/Unit + oldOfficeShiftedPeriodValue/Unit in SHIFTED set (grep: zero combined 'businessPeriod' field in the whole builder block). Report refs combined: crm2 apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sectionMap.ts:564 {ref:'businessPeriod',label:'Business Period'}, :578-580 {ref:'oldOfficeShiftedPeriod'} {ref:'currentCompanyPeriod'}. Section view uses raw + no recombine: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/service.ts:28 buildSections(ctx.formData) [raw], canonicalizeRenderContext only at :55 (narrative path); apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sections.ts:117 mappedSections drops null refs, :134-141 catch-all emits remaining keys via toLabel; toLabel('businessPeriodValue')='Business Period Value' (sections.ts:38-49). PERIOD_BASES recombine lives only in canonicalize.ts:40-48,107-113. DB: report_layouts has no BUILDER FIELD_REPORT row (only id1 RESIDENCE active). Registry: docs/COMPLIANCE_GAPS_REGISTRY.md:861-866 AUDIT-2 scopes split-period to narrative templates; same file lists 'generic raw-field display ... nothing dropped' as Verified PASS.

findings.title: Server-synced outcome set + display-label mirror is fully dead on mobile (long-code vs short-code key mismatch) — server-driven outcome relabel/add/remove never reaches the device dropdown
findings.detail: F2.7.1 was meant to let the server drive the on-device outcome dropdown (set + labels) from verification_unit_outcomes. The server's reference endpoint sends verificationTypeCode as the LONG code ('BUILDER','RESIDENCE',...). Mobile stores it verbatim and passes it verbatim into setOutcomesFromSync, which looks it up in FORM_TYPE_KEY_BY_VTYPE_CODE — a map keyed ONLY by SHORT codes (BV,RV,OV,...). Every lookup returns undefined -> 'continue' -> syncedOutcomesByFormType AND syncedLabelByOutcome are NEVER populated for ANY type. So getAllowedOutcomes and getOutcomeLabel always fall back to the hardcoded LEGACY_OUTCOMES_BY_FORM_TYPE / labelByOutcome. For BUILDER this is HARMLESS on the visible set today (hardcoded 5 == DB 5) but the entire dynamic-outcome feature is inert: if an admin relabels an outcome (e.g. NSP 'No Such Person' -> 'Person Absent'), reorders, or adds/removes a BUILDER outcome server-side, the device dropdown will NOT reflect it — a silent product-config regression. Mirror is dead for all 9 types; BUILDER's set happens to match the hardcoded fallback so there is no live visible defect, only loss of server-driven config. Not in the compliance registry.
findings.evidence: Server sends long code: crm2 apps/api/src/modules/reference/repository.ts:12-16 SELECT verification_type_code (= 'BUILDER'); reference.api.test.ts:45 asserts verificationTypeCode==='RESIDENCE'. Mobile stores+forwards verbatim: crm-mobile-native/src/sync/SyncDownloadService.ts:252-258 setOutcomesFromSync({verificationTypeCode:r.verificationTypeCode,...}); VerificationTypeOutcomesRepository.ts:36 verificationTypeCode:r.verification_type_code. Dead lookup: LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:154-164 FORM_TYPE_KEY_BY_VTYPE_CODE has only RV/RC/OV/EV/BV/NV/DV/PIV/PAV; :184-185 const key=FORM_TYPE_KEY_BY_VTYPE_CODE[r.verificationTypeCode]; if(!key) continue; -> 'BUILDER' is undefined -> skipped for all rows. Fallbacks: getAllowedOutcomes :221-225 returns LEGACY_OUTCOMES_BY_FORM_TYPE.builder; getOutcomeLabel :84-86 synced label never hits, falls to hardcoded labelByOutcome :119-126.

findings.detail: BUILDER has no active FIELD_REPORT report_layout (DB: only id1 RESIDENCE active), so findActiveByConfig returns null, narrative is null, and the web shows 'No report template configured for BUILDER'. The canonicalize pipeline (door-suffix labels, period recombine) is therefore never exercised for BUILDER in prod. findings.title: Split-period tenure fields (businessPeriod/currentCompanyPeriod/oldOfficeShiftedPeriod) render as raw *Value/*Unit fragments in BUILDER's only active report view; named period rows never render
findings.detail: The device stores every tenure split into `<base>Value` + `<base>Unit` (e.g. businessPeriodValue='5', businessPeriodUnit='Years') and NEVER a combined `businessPeriod`. The field-report service builds the raw section view with buildSections(ctx.formData) UNCONDITIONALLY on the RAW (non-canonicalized) blob (apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/service.ts:28). The recombination of split tenures into the combined token lives ONLY in canonicalizeRenderContext (apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/canonicalize.ts:106-113, PERIOD_BASES includes businessPeriod/currentCompanyPeriod/oldOfficeShiftedPeriod at :44-47), which is invoked ONLY inside the narrative branch (service.ts:55) — and that branch is dead for BUILDER (no active FIELD_REPORT report_layout; DB-confirmed only id1 RESIDENCE active). Consequence in the raw section view: sectionMap.ts builder refs 'businessPeriod' (:566), 'currentCompanyPeriod' (:582), 'oldOfficeShiftedPeriod' (:580) never match any stored key, so those named rows are skipped; the actual stored keys (businessPeriodValue/Unit etc., confirmed NOT in the section map) fall to the trailing 'Additional Details' catch-all (sections.ts:130-140) where toLabel renders them as two separate rows 'Business Period Value'='5' and 'Business Period Unit'='Years' (sections.ts:38-48). Affects SHIFTED (currentCompanyPeriod, oldOfficeShiftedPeriod) and POSITIVE/others (businessPeriod). Since BUILDER has no active narrative layout, this fragmented Additional-Details rendering is the ONLY report the user sees for BUILDER today.
findings.evidence: Mobile stores split, not combined: LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts POSITIVE set has businessPeriodValue/businessPeriodUnit (extracted from lines 4108-4327; NO 'businessPeriod' key), SHIFTED set has currentCompanyPeriodValue/Unit + oldOfficeShiftedPeriodValue/Unit (4328-4530). DB proof (crm2_dev, the one real field submission): `SELECT jsonb_object_keys(form_data->'residence'->'formData')` returns stayingPeriodValue + stayingPeriodUnit and NO stayingPeriod — same split convention BUILDER uses. Report side: service.ts:28 buildSections runs on raw ctx.formData; recombine only at canonicalize.ts:106-113 reached only via service.ts:55 (narrative branch); report_layouts has no BUILDER row (DB: only id1 FIELD_REPORT/RESIDENCE active). sectionMap.ts:566,580,582 reference the combined keys; sections.ts:117 `toValue(fieldsSource[ref])` returns null for those (absent) so the named rows drop; sections.ts:134-140 dumps businessPeriodValue/Unit into Additional Details via toLabel (sections.ts:38-48).

findings.title: Mobile setOutcomesFromSync is permanently dead: outcome-sync lookup keyed on SHORT codes (BV) but server/DB emit LONG codes (BUILDER) -> synced outcome label/order override never populates for any type
findings.detail: The server reference endpoint returns verification_unit_outcomes rows whose verificationTypeCode is the LONG unit code (DB-confirmed: BUILDER, RESIDENCE, ... — all 9 long-form), and the SDK contract documents this explicitly ('The v2 unit code the device matches a task on (= sync DTO verificationUnit.code)'). On the mobile side, setOutcomesFromSync looks each row up via FORM_TYPE_KEY_BY_VTYPE_CODE[r.verificationTypeCode], but that table is keyed ONLY by SHORT codes (RV/RC/OV/EV/BV/NV/DV/PIV/PAV). FORM_TYPE_KEY_BY_VTYPE_CODE['BUILDER'] is therefore undefined -> `if (!key) continue` skips every row -> syncedOutcomesByFormType and syncedLabelByOutcome are never populated for ANY verification type. getAllowedOutcomes / getOutcomeLabel then always fall through to the hardcoded LEGACY_OUTCOMES_BY_FORM_TYPE.builder = COMMON_LEGACY_OUTCOMES and the hardcoded labelByOutcome. For BUILDER this fallback happens to equal the 5 DB outcomes, so the dropdown is correct TODAY (harmless), BUT the entire server-driven outcome catalog (added/removed outcomes, sort-order changes, and admin label edits such as 'No Such Person' -> 'Person Absent') NEVER reaches the device. Note the same file's task-slug path uses the CORRECT long-code map (BACKEND_FORM_TYPE_TO_KEY['BUILDER']='builder', formTypeKey.ts:45) — so this is an internal short/long-code inconsistency, not a server contract issue. The comment-referenced rehydration helper loadOutcomesCacheFromDb does not exist in the codebase (only mentioned in comments), so there is no boot-time recovery path either. No test exercises setOutcomesFromSync.
findings.evidence: DB: `SELECT DISTINCT verification_type_code FROM verification_unit_outcomes` -> BUILDER, BUSINESS, DSA_CONNECTOR, NOC, OFFICE, PROPERTY_APF, PROPERTY_INDIVIDUAL, RESIDENCE, RESIDENCE_CUM_OFFICE (all long). SDK contract: packages/sdk/src/reference.ts:13-14. API: apps/api/src/modules/reference/repository.ts:10-18 selects verification_type_code verbatim. Mobile short-code map: LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:154-164 (RV/BV/...); skip on miss: :184-185 (`const key = FORM_TYPE_KEY_BY_VTYPE_CODE[r.verificationTypeCode]; if (!key) continue;`); maps consumed at getAllowedOutcomes :221-225 and getOutcomeLabel :83-85. Call site passes long code through: SyncDownloadService.ts:252-259. Correct long-code map exists for tasks: formTypeKey.ts:36-56 (BUILDER:'builder'). loadOutcomesCacheFromDb referenced only in comments (LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:149,176) — `grep -rn loadOutcomesCacheFromDb src/` returns only those two comment lines.

findings.title: Section-view 'Verification Outcome' row shows the raw uppercase CODE (POSITIVE) instead of the v1 verbose label; for BUILDER this is the only outcome label shown
findings.detail: In the raw section view, the leading 'Verification Outcome' row is set from the raw device blob.verificationOutcome with no canonicalization (sections.ts:154 `const outcome = toValue(blob['verificationOutcome'])`, emitted at :126-128). The code-to-verbose-label mapping (deriveLabel: POSITIVE+door -> 'Positive & Door Locked', etc.) lives only in canonicalize.ts:56-72, which runs only in the latent narrative branch. So the section view shows the bare uppercase code 'POSITIVE'/'SHIFTED'/'NSP'/'ENTRY_RESTRICTED'/'UNTRACEABLE'. Because BUILDER has no active narrative layout, the raw code is the ONLY outcome label any web user sees for a BUILDER task. Cosmetic/labeling drift (the casing+vocabulary disagree with the v1 verbose convention the templates use), no data loss.
findings.evidence: sections.ts:154 reads raw blob['verificationOutcome']; :126-128 unshifts it verbatim as the 'Verification Outcome' row. DB proof the stored value is the bare uppercase code: `SELECT form_data->'residence'->>'verificationOutcome'` = 'POSITIVE'. Verbose-label derivation that is NOT applied here: canonicalize.ts:56-72 (deriveLabel) + :96-100, reached only via service.ts:55 narrative branch, which is null for BUILDER (no active report_layout — DB-confirmed).

findings.title: BUILDER report section view never groups the period fields — split *Value/*Unit keys fall into 'Additional Details' with auto-labels (every period-capturing outcome)
findings.detail: The web Field Report's raw-field SECTIONS view (which always renders, layout or not) is built by buildSections() on the NON-canonicalized form_data (apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/service.ts:28). The BUILDER sectionMap references the COMBINED period tokens — businessPeriod (sectionMap.ts:566, 'Builder / Business Details'), currentCompanyPeriod (sectionMap.ts:582) and oldOfficeShiftedPeriod (sectionMap.ts:580, 'Shifting Details'). But the v2-native device only ever submits the SPLIT keys businessPeriodValue/businessPeriodUnit, currentCompanyPeriodValue/Unit, oldOfficeShiftedPeriodValue/Unit (mobile LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:4184-4194 POSITIVE, :4391-4427 SHIFTED) — there is NO un-suffixed combined field on the device (grep for name:'businessPeriod'/'currentCompanyPeriod'/'oldOfficeShiftedPeriod' returns nothing). Period RECOMBINATION (Value+Unit→combined) happens ONLY in canonicalizeRenderContext (canonicalize.ts:106-113, PERIOD_BASES), which feeds the NARRATIVE (service.ts:55), not buildSections. mappedSections does a literal fieldsSource[ref] lookup with no alias resolution (sections.ts:117), so the combined refs never match → the split keys are unplaced and swept into the trailing catch-all 'Additional Details' section (sections.ts:130-140) with toLabel-generated headers 'Business Period Value' / 'Business Period Unit' / 'Old Office Shifted Period Value' etc. Net UX effect: tenure data appears as 4-6 ungrouped, machine-labelled rows at the very bottom of the report instead of inside their intended Builder/Shifting sections — for POSITIVE, SHIFTED, and NSP (NSP only captures currentCompanyPeriod via... it doesn't; POSITIVE businessPeriod, SHIFTED currentCompanyPeriod+oldOfficeShiftedPeriod).
findings.evidence: web sectionMap combined refs: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sectionMap.ts:566 (businessPeriod), :580 (oldOfficeShiftedPeriod), :582 (currentCompanyPeriod). Sections built on RAW formData: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/service.ts:28 (buildSections(ctx.formData)) vs :55 (canonicalize only for narrative). Literal ref lookup, no alias: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sections.ts:117. Recombine lives only in canonicalize: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/canonicalize.ts:40-48,106-113. Device emits split keys only: mobile src/screens/forms/LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:4184-4194 (businessPeriodValue/Unit), :4391-4415 (currentCompanyPeriodValue/Unit), :4417-4427 (oldOfficeShiftedPeriodValue/Unit); grep for combined names returned zero hits.

findings.title: NSP BUILDER 'Business Existence' value is unmapped in the report and renders misspelled — drops into 'Additional Details' as 'Business Existance'
findings.detail: The NSP outcome captures a field named businessExistance (sic, misspelled key) with display label 'Business Existence' (mobile LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:4558-4563; mobile corrects the label via its own alias map LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:3279 businessExistance→businessExistence). The BUILDER web sectionMap has NO entry for ref 'businessExistance' — it only maps 'businessExistsStatus' (the ERT-only field, sectionMap.ts:547 'Business Exists Status (ERT)'). Confirmed by enumerating all 47 BUILDER sectionMap refs: businessExistance is absent. So for an NSP BUILDER submission the captured business-existence answer is never placed in a named section and is swept into the catch-all 'Additional Details' (sections.ts:130-140) with toLabel('businessExistance') = 'Business Existance' — a misspelled, ungrouped row. This is label drift between mobile capture ('Business Existence', correctly spelled, shown in the 'Verification Details' section) and the web report ('Business Existance', misspelled, in Additional Details).
findings.evidence: Mobile NSP field: src/screens/forms/LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:4558-4563 (name 'businessExistance', label 'Business Existence'); mobile label-fix alias at :3279. Web sectionMap has no businessExistance ref — enumerated refs from apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sectionMap.ts:541-633 (only businessExistsStatus present at :547). Catch-all + toLabel path: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sections.ts:130-140, toLabel at :38-48 (camelCase split preserves the misspelling verbatim).

findings.title: ENTRY_RESTRICTED BUILDER: 'feedbackFromNeighbour' is mislabeled 'Neighbour Feedback' in the report though the agent answered a met-person question
findings.detail: The feedbackFromNeighbour ref is reused across outcomes with DIFFERENT semantics on the device: for POSITIVE/SHIFTED/NSP its label is 'Feedback from Neighbour', but for ENTRY_RESTRICTED the same key is relabeled 'Feedback from Met Person' (mobile LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:4785-4787) because entry was restricted so no neighbour was canvassed — the agent rates the met person instead. The web sectionMap is outcome-blind: it maps the single ref 'feedbackFromNeighbour' to the fixed label 'Neighbour Feedback' (sectionMap.ts:625, 'Locality & Area Assessment'). So an ENTRY_RESTRICTED BUILDER report displays the agent's met-person feedback under the wrong noun ('Neighbour Feedback'), a semantic mislabel that could mislead the reviewer about who was assessed. Section grouping itself is fine (it lands in Locality & Area Assessment); only the label is wrong for this one outcome.
findings.evidence: Mobile ERT relabel: src/screens/forms/LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:4785-4787 (feedbackFromNeighbour → 'Feedback from Met Person', conditional on metPersonConfirmation≠'Not Confirmed'). Mobile other outcomes use 'Feedback from Neighbour' (e.g. POSITIVE :4308-4310). Web fixed label: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sectionMap.ts:625 ({ ref:'feedbackFromNeighbour', label:'Neighbour Feedback' }). sectionMap labels are static per ref, no outcome branch (sections.ts mappedSections :106-123 takes the def label verbatim).

findings.title: Dead duplicate sectionMap entry: 'metPersonName' is listed twice in BUILDER 'Met Person Details' — the second ERT label can never render
findings.detail: The BUILDER sectionMap lists ref 'metPersonName' twice within the same 'Met Person Details' section: 'Met Person Name' (sectionMap.ts:555) then 'Name of Met Person (ERT)' (sectionMap.ts:557). mappedSections dedupes by ref with first-occurrence-wins (sections.ts:116 `if (placed.has(ref)) continue`), so only 'Met Person Name' ever renders and the '(ERT)' variant is permanently dead — it can never produce a row and never produces a duplicate row either. No user-visible defect (no duplicate row, and 'Met Person Name' is an acceptable label for ERT too), but it is a dead, misleading map entry that implies an ERT-specific label exists when it cannot fire. Cleanup only.
findings.evidence: Duplicate ref in same section: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sectionMap.ts:555 and :557 (both ref 'metPersonName'). Dedup first-wins: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sections.ts:112-121 (placed Set, line 116 skip). ENTRY_RESTRICTED does capture metPersonName (mobile src/screens/forms/LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:4716-4721), so the first label 'Met Person Name' is what shows — never the ERT variant.

findings.title: Field-photo upload idempotency-replay is unscoped — cross-user leak of another agent's photo metadata + GPS via a client-controlled operation_id
findings.detail: On POST /api/v2/verification-tasks/:id/attachments the service derives operationBase from a fully client-controlled value — the Idempotency-Key header or the multipart operationId field (verification-tasks/controller.ts:75,82,88; service.ts:201) with NO format constraint (not required to be a UUID, not bound to the actor). Before storing, it does a replay check: cached = repo.fieldAttachmentsByOperation(operationBase); if cached.length > 0 it returns those rows immediately (service.ts:211-212). That repo query matches purely on split_part(operation_id,':',1) = $1 with NO actor / case / task filter (cases/repository.ts:1571-1579), and operation_id is a single GLOBAL namespace (uq_case_attachments_operation is unsegmented — confirmed in the case_attachments schema dump). The ownedCaseId(taskId, actor) call (service.ts:200) only validates the task in the URL belongs to the attacker; it does NOT constrain the cached rows that get returned. The returned DeviceAttachment exposes geo_location and storage_key (aliased to `url`) plus original filename + photoType (FIELD_ATTACH_COLS, cases/repository.ts:309-310; SDK shape sync.ts:91-104). So an authenticated FIELD_AGENT (route is gated TASK_EXECUTE only) who supplies another agent's operation_id while POSTing to their OWN task gets back that victim's field-photo storage-key paths (which embed the victim's caseId/taskId) + the verification GPS coordinates + filenames — cross-user, cross-case info disclosure. Bytes themselves are NOT retrievable from the bare storage_key (object store is private; the signed-URL endpoints are independently scope-guarded — service.ts:625-628, repository.ts:1703-1725), so the impact is metadata + location-PII, not photo exfiltration; honest operation_ids are uuidv4 (mobile SyncQueue.ts:322) so blind guessing is hard, but any leaked/observed/known id (e.g. from logs, a shared device, or the attacker's own prior-task ids enumerated) is directly replayable. Affects all 9 FIELD_VISIT types incl BUILDER; outcome-independent.
findings.evidence: apps/api/src/modules/verification-tasks/service.ts:200-212 (ownedCaseId only checks URL task; cached return is unscoped) + :218-219 (clientSha format-checked but operation_id is not); apps/api/src/modules/verification-tasks/controller.ts:75,82 (idem = req.headers['idempotency-key'] or body.operationId, arbitrary string); apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:1571-1579 (fieldAttachmentsByOperation — WHERE split_part(operation_id,':',1)=$1, no scope) and :309-310 (FIELD_ATTACH_COLS exposes geo_location + storage_key AS url); packages/sdk/src/sync.ts:91-104 (DeviceAttachment.url = storage key, geoLocation present); DB: case_attachments unique constraint uq_case_attachments_operation is global/unpartitioned (schema dump). Contrast the correctly-scoped sibling paths: cases/service.ts:542-553 (fieldPhotoDownload), :625-628 (attachmentUrl) and cases/repository.ts:1703-1725 (attachmentForAccess) all filter by resolveScope(actor)/taskScopePredicate.

findings.title: NOC field report's two surfaces disagree on the outcome label: sections show raw uppercase CODE ('POSITIVE'), narrative shows v1 verbose label ('POSITIVE & DOOR OPEN')
findings.detail: On the same Field Report panel in CaseDetailPage, the leading 'Verification Outcome' row (raw-field sections) prints the un-canonicalized blob value, which for a v2-native device submission is the bare uppercase CODE (e.g. 'POSITIVE'), while the generated narrative below prints the ADR-0057 canonicalized v1 verbose label ('NOC Remark: POSITIVE & DOOR OPEN'). Neither surface uses the DB display_label ('Positive', Title-case). The mismatch is structural, not data-dependent: service.ts builds sections from the RAW ctx.formData BEFORE canonicalization, then renders the narrative from the CANONICALIZED context. A reader sees 'Verification Outcome: POSITIVE' immediately above 'NOC Remark: POSITIVE & DOOR OPEN', and the casing/label vocabulary differs. This is a presentation-correctness regression vs v1, where the on-report outcome was always the verbose label (v1 stored verificationOutcome AS the verbose label). Affects all 5 NOC outcomes and is cross-cutting to all 9 FIELD types.
findings.evidence: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/service.ts:28 buildSections(ctx.formData) [RAW] vs :55 renderNarrative(..., canonicalizeRenderContext(ctx)) [CANONICALIZED]; sections.ts:154 const outcome = toValue(blob['verificationOutcome']) reads the raw blob, sections.ts:126-127 unshift {label:'Verification Outcome', value: outcome} verbatim; toValue does String(x) with no case transform. canonicalize.ts:96-100 + deriveLabel:69-72 build the verbose label only for the narrative path. v1 baseline: CRM-BACKEND/src/utils/formTypeDetection.ts:67,73,159 store verificationOutcome='Positive & Door Open'/'NSP & Door Locked'. DB display_label (clean): verification_unit_outcomes NOC -> Positive/Shifted/No Such Person/Entry Restricted/Untraceable (queried live).

findings.title: NOC has NO usable narrative report path in production — no active FIELD_REPORT layout for NOC, so every NOC report renders 'No report template configured for NOC'
findings.detail: The fieldReports service only renders a narrative when reportLayoutRepository.findActiveByConfig(clientId, productId, 'FIELD_REPORT', verificationType='NOC') returns an active layout with a templateBody. On the dev box the only active FIELD_REPORT layout is verification_type='RESIDENCE' (client 1/product 1). For NOC the lookup misses, service.ts returns narrative:null, and the web shows literally 'No report template configured for NOC.' Net product effect: a completed NOC verification yields a report that is ONLY the raw-field sections (with the raw-code outcome row from NOC-1) — the polished v1-faithful NOC narrative (NOC issuance grammar, TPC sentences, conclusion) never reaches the customer. The entire canonicalize.ts code->label chain + the 8 NOC_BODY branches are exercised only by unit tests, never in the live render path, until someone seeds/activates a NOC FIELD_REPORT layout. This is a completeness gap: 8 of 9 FIELD types (everything except RESIDENCE) share it, but it is real for NOC specifically. The SDK ships FIELD_REPORT_DEFAULTS['NOC'] = {NOC_BODY, NOC_COLUMNS} ready to be seeded — the defaults exist but are not provisioned as active layouts.
findings.evidence: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/service.ts:30-44 findActiveByConfig(...'FIELD_REPORT', ctx.verificationType) then 'if (!layout || !layout.templateBody) return {... narrative:null}'; repository.ts:51 verificationType = vu.code (='NOC'). Live DB: SELECT id,name,kind,verification_type,is_active FROM report_layouts -> only (1, RESIDENCE REPORT E2E, FIELD_REPORT, RESIDENCE, active) + (2, CASE_REPORT); report_templates table count=0. Web fallback copy: apps/web/src/features/cases/CaseDetailPage.tsx:1866-1868 'No report template configured for {verificationType}.' Ready-but-unseeded default: packages/sdk/src/fieldReportDefaults.ts:1719 NOC: {templateBody: NOC_BODY, columns: NOC_COLUMNS}.

findings.title: addressLocatable — a REQUIRED NOC device field for POSITIVE/SHIFTED/NSP — is never surfaced in any named report section or the narrative (only via the Additional-Details catch-all)
findings.detail: The NOC mobile form makes addressLocatable a required select in the POSITIVE, SHIFTED, and NSP outcome field sets, but the report layer references it nowhere: it is absent from the NOC sectionMap, absent from NOC_COLUMNS, and absent from NOC_BODY. So it never appears under a meaningful heading and never feeds a narrative clause; it only lands in the trailing 'Additional Details' catch-all (so no field is LOST, but it is semantically demoted). The narrative instead asserts 'The given address is locatable and rated as {address_rating}' as a hardcoded constant rather than reflecting the agent's actual addressLocatable answer — if an agent marked the address NOT locatable, the narrative would still claim it is locatable. Same pattern affects the NSP-only businessExistance field (also unmapped). Minor today because the narrative is latent (NOC-2), but it is a v1-parity/data-fidelity gap that surfaces the moment a NOC layout is activated.
findings.evidence: Required device field: CRM-MOBILE LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:4978-4982 (POSITIVE addressLocatable required:true), :5098-5102 (SHIFTED), :5285-5288 (NSP); businessExistance :5309-5314. Report layer: grep 'addressLocatable' over apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/ and packages/sdk/src/fieldReportDefaults.ts -> ZERO matches; sectionMap.ts noc block (727-806) has addressRating but no addressLocatable. Hardcoded locatable claim: fieldReportDefaults.ts:1596 'The given address is locatable and rated as {{address_rating}}'. Catch-all that absorbs it: sections.ts:130-140 'Additional Details'.

findings.title: Hardcoded mobile fallback display labels for NOC SHIFTED/NSP contain doubled-word typos ('Shifted & Door Locked Shifted', 'NSP & NSP Door Locked')
findings.detail: getOutcomeLabel's hardcoded labelByOutcome fallback map renders SHIFTED as 'Shifted & Door Locked Shifted' and NSP as 'NSP & NSP Door Locked' — both contain a duplicated word. This is the outcome label the field agent sees when choosing an outcome on the device. It is display-only (the submitted verificationOutcome value is the CODE, not this label, so it does not corrupt the report), and it is MITIGATED in production because server-synced labels from verification_unit_outcomes.display_label win over the hardcoded map (getOutcomeLabel returns the synced label first), and the DB labels are clean ('Shifted', 'No Such Person'). The typo only shows on a fresh install before the first successful outcome sync, or if the outcome sync fails/returns empty. Verify against intended copy and fix the literals so the fallback is also clean.
findings.evidence: Typo'd fallback: CRM-MOBILE LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:117-123 labelByOutcome { SHIFTED: 'Shifted & Door Locked Shifted', NSP: 'NSP & NSP Door Locked', ... }. Server-synced-wins guard: same file :82-86 'const synced = syncedLabelByOutcome.get(...); if (synced) return synced;', populated from server rows at :178-190 setOutcomesFromSync. Clean DB labels (live): verification_unit_outcomes NOC SHIFTED='Shifted', NSP='No Such Person'.

findings.title: Report SECTIONS path never recombines the device's split tenure fields (<base>Value/<base>Unit), so SHIFTED-NOC's 'Old Office Shifted Period' / 'Current Company Period' fall out of 'Shifting Details' into 'Additional Details' as two raw rows
findings.detail: The v2-native mobile contract (ADR-0054) submits every tenure as TWO split keys: e.g. SHIFTED NOC captures `oldOfficeShiftedPeriodValue`+`oldOfficeShiftedPeriodUnit` and `currentCompanyPeriodValue`+`currentCompanyPeriodUnit` (LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts SHIFTED field set). The report has two surfaces from one service call: the NARRATIVE recombines via canonicalize.PERIOD_BASES (oldOfficeShiftedPeriod/currentCompanyPeriod are listed) → renders combined token correctly; but the always-rendered SECTIONS path does NOT. sections.ts:117 reads `fieldsSource[ref]` directly for the sectionMap's declared ref `oldOfficeShiftedPeriod`/`currentCompanyPeriod` (sectionMap.ts:754,756) — those COMBINED keys do not exist in the raw blob (only the split keys do), so toValue returns null and mappedSections SKIPS them (sections.ts:118). The two split keys are not in SKIP_KEYS, so they land in the trailing 'Additional Details' catch-all (sections.ts:134-140) as separate rows 'Old Office Shifted Period Value: 6' and 'Old Office Shifted Period Unit: Months' under a generic header — NOT the intended single 'Old Office Shifted Period: 6 Months' row in the 'Shifting Details' section. service.ts:28 calls buildSections(ctx.formData) on the RAW (pre-canonicalize) context, and sections.ts imports only SECTION_MAP (no canonicalize), confirming no recombination. Field is never LOST (never-lose-a-field holds) but is MISPLACED and split. Affects every type whose sectionMap declares a combined period ref the device splits: NOC (Shifting Details), Business/Office/RCO/Builder (same oldOfficeShiftedPeriod/currentCompanyPeriod) plus stayingPeriod/businessPeriod/workingPeriod/establishmentPeriod across residence-family sections.
findings.evidence: Device split keys (mobile): /Users/mayurkulkarni/Downloads/CRM-APP-MONOREPO-PROD/crm-mobile-native/src/screens/forms/LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts SHIFTED NOC set → fields `oldOfficeShiftedPeriodValue`,`oldOfficeShiftedPeriodUnit`,`currentCompanyPeriodValue`,`currentCompanyPeriodUnit` (verified via grep over lines 5096-5282). Section ref expects COMBINED: /Users/mayurkulkarni/Downloads/crm2/apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sectionMap.ts:754 {ref:'oldOfficeShiftedPeriod'}, :756 {ref:'currentCompanyPeriod'}. Sections path reads ref verbatim, no recombine: /Users/mayurkulkarni/Downloads/crm2/apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sections.ts:117 `const value = toValue(fieldsSource[ref])`, :118 skip-if-null; catch-all at :134-140. Narrative recombines but sections do NOT: /Users/mayurkulkarni/Downloads/crm2/apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/service.ts:28 buildSections(ctx.formData) [RAW] vs :55 renderNarrative(...,canonicalizeRenderContext(ctx)); canonicalize PERIOD_BASES includes both keys at /Users/mayurkulkarni/Downloads/crm2/apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/canonicalize.ts:40-48. grep confirmed zero 'Value'/'Unit'/recombine logic in sectionMap.ts.

findings.title: Same NOC report page disagrees on the outcome label: SECTIONS show the raw uppercase CODE ('POSITIVE'), the NARRATIVE shows the v1 verbose label ('Positive & Door Open'), and the DB Title-case display_label ('Positive') is used by neither
findings.detail: The Field Report body renders both surfaces together. The leading 'Verification Outcome' row in sections is built from the RAW (un-canonicalized) blob: sections.ts:154 reads `blob['verificationOutcome']` and emits it verbatim (sections.ts:96/126), so for a NOC POSITIVE submission it shows the uppercase code 'POSITIVE'. The narrative branches on the canonicalized verbose label via canonicalize.deriveLabel (code+officeStatus → 'Positive & Door Open'/'Positive & Door Locked'). Web renders both in the same FieldReportBody (sections rows then the Generated Report). Meanwhile DB verification_unit_outcomes.display_label for NOC POSITIVE is Title-case 'Positive' — used by neither report surface. Cosmetic inconsistency, not data loss; the code value is unambiguous. Currently LATENT for NOC on the dev box because no active NOC FIELD_REPORT layout exists, so the narrative is null and only the raw-code sections row shows.
findings.evidence: Raw-code sections row: /Users/mayurkulkarni/Downloads/crm2/apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sections.ts:154 `const outcome = toValue(blob['verificationOutcome'])`, surfaced :96/:126 as 'Verification Outcome'. Verbose-label narrative: /Users/mayurkulkarni/Downloads/crm2/apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/canonicalize.ts:69-71 (POSITIVE→'Positive'+doorSuffix), :52-53 doorSuffix. Both rendered on one page: /Users/mayurkulkarni/Downloads/crm2/apps/web/src/features/cases/CaseDetailPage.tsx:1847-1858 (sections) + :1859-1869 (narrative). DB display_label (psql crm2_dev): NOC POSITIVE→'Positive', SHIFTED→'Shifted', NSP→'No Such Person', ENTRY_RESTRICTED→'Entry Restricted', UNTRACEABLE→'Untraceable' — Title-case, used by neither surface.

findings.title: Device-submitted NOC outcome does not round-trip via down-sync (verificationOutcome stays NULL until office-complete) — confirmed INTENTIONAL per ADR-0032 D1 single-layer, not a gap
findings.detail: Investigated the candidate that an agent's submitted NOC outcome is lost on re-sync. Confirmed the mechanism but it is by design. Up-sync (submitForm) writes the outcome CODE only into case_tasks.form_data['noc'].verificationOutcome as EVIDENCE; submitTaskByDevice transitions ASSIGNED/IN_PROGRESS→SUBMITTED and explicitly does NOT set ct.verification_outcome. The official ct.verification_outcome column is set only later at office-complete (completeTask) from input.result supplied by the office actor. Down-sync's task.verificationOutcome sources ct.verification_outcome (sync/repository.ts:61, mapped sync/service.ts:38 only-if-non-null), so a re-synced NOC task carries verificationOutcome=NULL even after the agent submitted, while the full form_data blob (incl the agent's evidence outcome + all split keys) DOES round-trip. findings.title: Attachment tamper-detection verdict (hash_verified) is computed and stored but never surfaced on any read path — the integrity check is effectively unobservable
findings.detail: NOC requires ≥5 photos + selfie + GPS. On device upload the API verifies the device's client_sha256 against the bytes it received and stores the boolean result in case_attachments.hash_verified, explicitly for 'tamper-detection across the upload boundary' (mobile schema.ts:91-94). But hash_verified is WRITE-ONLY: a repo-wide grep shows it is read by NO serving path. listFieldPhotos (the web Field Photos card source) selects only id/taskId/unitName/photoType/originalName/geoLocation/reverseGeocodedAddress/createdAt — not hash_verified, sha256, or client_sha256 (cases/repository.ts:1621-1631). Down-sync's MobileSyncTask carries no integrity field (sdk/sync.ts) and the mobile attachments table has no column to receive a server verdict (schema.ts:71-97). The field-report never references it. So when a device's hash does NOT match the received bytes, the code only logs a warn and continues (verification-tasks/service.ts:232-234, 'logged, never rejected') — and no office reviewer, report, or downstream system can ever tell a NOC photo failed transit integrity. The mechanism the schema comment advertises as tamper-detection produces a verdict no human or system ever sees. This is a defense-in-depth/observability gap, not an active exposure; it is v1-parity behavior and cross-cutting (all 9 FIELD types, not NOC-specific), hence LOW.
findings.evidence: WRITE: apps/api/src/modules/verification-tasks/service.ts:232 `const hashVerified = clientSha !== null && clientSha === sha256(f.buffer);` :233-234 warn-only on mismatch; :255 stored. apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:1542 INSERT includes hash_verified. READ: grep `hash_verified|hashVerified` across apps/ + packages/ (excluding dist/test/migrations) returns ONLY the two write sites — zero read sites. apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:1621-1631 listFieldPhotos SELECT omits it. Mobile receives no verdict: CRM-MONOREPO-PROD/crm-mobile-native/src/database/schema.ts:91-97 attachments has client_sha256 (sent) but no hash_verified column. DB proof: `SELECT hash_verified, client_sha256 IS NOT NULL, count(*) FROM case_attachments WHERE kind='FIELD_PHOTO'` → all 6 rows t/t (no surfaced consumer).

findings.title: Stored NOC photo evidence hash is sha256(EXIF-stripped) while client_sha256 is sha256(raw capture) — the two hashes are intentionally non-comparable, so chain-of-custody continuity across the strip boundary is unverifiable after upload
findings.detail: The server EXIF-strips each NOC photo (processFieldPhoto) before storing, then stores sha256(stripped) as the evidence hash and client_sha256 (the device's raw-capture hash) verbatim. The transit check compares client_sha256 against sha256(RAW received bytes) — correct for transit — but the persisted evidence hash is of the DIFFERENT (stripped) bytes, so client_sha256 and the stored sha256 can never be equal by design. There is no persisted hash of the raw bytes, so once the request completes there is no way to re-derive or prove the stored stripped artifact descends from the device-captured bytes (the only linkage was the in-memory transit comparison whose boolean result is the unsurfaced hash_verified from SEC-NOC-1). This is an accepted consequence of server-side EXIF stripping (privacy vs immutable chain-of-custody) and is correct as designed; flagged INFO for completeness so the team is aware the on-disk hash is NOT the device hash and the two must never be compared directly in any future integrity tooling.
findings.evidence: apps/api/src/modules/verification-tasks/service.ts:235 `const { stripped, thumbnail } = await processFieldPhoto(f.buffer);` :253 `sha256: sha256(stripped), // evidence hash of the stored (stripped) artifact` :254 `clientSha256: clientSha` (device raw hash) :232 transit compares against `sha256(f.buffer)` (raw). Comment at cases/repository.ts:1517 confirms `sha256` is the SERVER hash of the stored (EXIF-stripped) bytes; `clientSha256` the device's [raw].

findings.title: DSA raw-section report shows empty curated period rows + dumps split tenure into 'Additional Details' (POSITIVE, SHIFTED) — buildSections never recombines *Value/*Unit
findings.detail: The field-report 'sections' (the ONLY rendered DSA report surface, since DSA has no active narrative layout) groups fields via SECTION_MAP['dsa-connector'], whose tenure refs are the COMBINED keys businessPeriod / currentCompanyPeriod / oldOfficeShiftedPeriod (sectionMap.ts:657, 678-680). But the v2-native device emits these SPLIT into businessPeriodValue+businessPeriodUnit / currentCompanyPeriodValue+Unit / oldOfficeShiftedPeriodValue+Unit and NEVER emits the combined key (ADR-0057 §2, canonicalize.ts:5-8,39-48). The recombine (canonicalize.ts:107-113) runs only on the narrative path; buildSections is called on the RAW ctx.formData (service.ts:28) and has no recombine logic. Result for DSA POSITIVE (has businessPeriod*) and DSA SHIFTED (has currentCompanyPeriod* + oldOfficeShiftedPeriod*): the curated 'Business Period' / 'Current Company Period' / 'Old Office Shifted Period' rows in their named sections render EMPTY (toValue(undefined)=null, sections.ts:117), and the split values are instead collected into the trailing 'Additional Details' bucket (sections.ts:130-140) labelled 'Business Period Value', 'Business Period Unit', etc. via toLabel(). No data is lost, but the report layout is a parity regression vs the v1-grounded grouping the ADR designed: the tenure reads as two raw split rows in a catch-all instead of one labelled row in 'Business/Connector Details' or 'Shifting Details'. The ADR-0057 section-grouping doc's per-slug 'X in named sections + 0 in Additional Details' coverage proof (field-report-section-grouping-2026-06-22.md:166,245) was computed against the COMBINED-key v2 catalog (fieldReportDefaults.ts:1359 uses 'businessPeriod'), not the actual split-key device payload, so it is incorrect against live device data.
findings.evidence: sectionMap.ts:657 {ref:'businessPeriod'}, :678 {ref:'oldOfficeShiftedPeriod'}, :680 {ref:'currentCompanyPeriod'} (combined keys) — grep '*Value/*Unit' refs in sectionMap.ts = 0. Device emits split: LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:1589 'businessPeriodValue', :1595 'businessPeriodUnit', :2746 'currentCompanyPeriodValue', :2758 'oldOfficeShiftedPeriodValue'. Per-outcome scan: POSITIVE -> businessPeriodValue+Unit; SHIFTED -> currentCompanyPeriodValue+Unit + oldOfficeShiftedPeriodValue+Unit; NSP/ENTRY_RESTRICTED/UNTRACEABLE -> none (unaffected). Recombine is canonicalize-only: service.ts:28 'buildSections(ctx.formData)' (raw) vs :55 'canonicalizeRenderContext(ctx)' (narrative only); canonicalize.ts:107-113 recombine; ADR-0057 lines 36,88 explicitly state 'raw-fields section view (buildSections) is unchanged'. No live DSA task masks it: psql crm2_dev -> case_tasks WHERE verification_unit_id=7 AND form_data ? 'dsa-connector' = 0 rows. No sections test feeds split keys (only canonicalize.render.test.ts does).

findings.title: Raw 'Verification Outcome' report row shows the UPPERCASE CODE ('NSP'/'ENTRY_RESTRICTED') instead of the human display_label
findings.detail: In the raw-sections view, the leading 'Verification Outcome' row is read straight from blob['verificationOutcome'] (sections.ts:154) — the raw uppercase device CODE — and unshifted onto the first section unchanged (sections.ts:124-128). canonicalize (which would turn POSITIVE→'Positive & Door Open', NSP→'NSP', ENTRY_RESTRICTED→'ERT', etc.) runs only on the narrative path (service.ts:55), never on buildSections (service.ts:28). So a desk user reading the field-section table sees raw codes: 'NSP' and 'ENTRY_RESTRICTED' (not the DB display_labels 'No Such Person' / 'Entry Restricted' from verification_unit_outcomes, ids 33/34). This is label drift between what the agent picked (a human outcome) and what the desk report shows (a code), most jarring for NSP/ENTRY_RESTRICTED where the code is non-obvious. Note this affects all FIELD types, but for DSA the 5 codes include the opaque NSP/ENTRY_RESTRICTED. DB proof: verification_unit_outcomes for DSA_CONNECTOR → 33 NSP/'No Such Person', 34 ENTRY_RESTRICTED/'Entry Restricted', 35 UNTRACEABLE/'Untraceable'.

findings.title: Field-photo evidence hash is computed over server-STRIPPED bytes while client_sha256 is over the device file — the persisted integrity pair can never re-verify (intentional, v1-parity, logged-never-rejected)
findings.detail: On the up-sync attachment boundary, the device computes clientSha256 over the final watermarked file (CRM-MOBILE CameraService.ts:297, sha256OfFile of finalDestPath). The API verifies hashVerified = (clientSha === sha256(received buffer)) — a TRANSIT check only — then EXIF-strips the bytes and persists sha256(stripped) as the evidence hash plus client_sha256 verbatim (verification-tasks/service.ts:232-254). Because the stored evidence hash is of the server-MUTATED (stripped) artifact, it will never equal the persisted client_sha256, so the chain-of-custody pair cannot be re-verified against the stored object after the fact. A mismatch is logged.warn only and NEVER rejects the upload (service.ts:233-234), so a corrupted-in-transit or substituted image is still stored as evidence. This is the documented ADR-0034 / v1-parity design (FROZEN), affects all 9 FIELD types incl DSA_CONNECTOR equally, and is not a regression in this track — flagged so reviewers know the 'tamper-detection' is advisory, not enforcing.
findings.evidence: apps/api/src/modules/verification-tasks/service.ts:231-254 (hashVerified vs received bytes; sha256(stripped) stored; logged-never-rejected at :233-234); CRM-MOBILE-NATIVE/crm-mobile-native/src/services/CameraService.ts:297 + src/utils/fileHash.ts:31-33 (client hashes the final watermarked file, pre-strip)

findings.title: DSA_CONNECTOR verification_units.pii_sensitive=false and the flag drives no runtime masking despite the 'DPDP masking' UI label — applicant PII (name/mobile/PAN) flows into the DSA report unmasked
findings.detail: verification_units.pii_sensitive is true only on the 20 KYC_DOCUMENT types (PAN/Aadhaar/etc.); DSA_CONNECTOR (id 7) is false (DB query confirmed). I grepped the API/SDK/web for any consumer of pii_sensitive: it is read ONLY by the verificationUnits CRUD/export/record-page (repository.ts:11/117/193, service.ts:122/154, VerificationUnitRecordPage.tsx:237 labeled 'PII sensitive (DPDP masking)'). No report/render/serialization path masks or redacts based on this flag anywhere. The DSA FIELD_REPORT render context embeds applicant name, mobile and PAN in cleartext (fieldReports/repository.ts:84-89) for any case.view holder in scope. This is consistent with the frozen design (a verification report legitimately needs applicant identity, and access is gated by case.view + data scope), so it is NOT an exposure defect — but the 'DPDP masking' label implies a runtime control that does not exist, which is worth noting for the DPDP-compliance posture. No DSA-specific leak.
