findings.title: Server catalog offers PROPERTY_APF NEGATIVE as a top-level field outcome, but mobile form + report canonicalize don't handle the NEGATIVE code → selecting it yields an EMPTY APF narrative
findings.detail: PROPERTY_APF is designed so the field agent picks one of 3 top-level outcomes on mobile (POSITIVE/ENTRY_RESTRICTED/UNTRACEABLE) and the POSITIVE picker is the combined 'Positive & Negative' form, with the actual verdict captured in finalStatus (SEEN path) / finalStatusNegative (STOP/VACANT path) = Positive|Negative|Refer|Fraud. The report's narrative derives the verbose 'Positive'/'Negative' branch from that field, NOT from a top-level NEGATIVE code. THE DEFECT: the server outcome catalog (verification_unit_outcomes) lists PROPERTY_APF→NEGATIVE as a 4th top-level outcome (sort 2, is_active=t). The reference endpoint /verification-type-outcomes returns ALL active rows (referenceRepository.listOutcomes is unfiltered), and mobile's refreshVerificationTypeOutcomes pushes them verbatim into setOutcomesFromSync, which casts r.outcomeCode as AllOutcome and stores the full 4-item list (incl NEGATIVE) in syncedOutcomesByFormType['property-apf']. getAllowedOutcomes returns the SYNCED list when present, so the on-device outcome picker (VerificationFormScreen outcomeOptions, getAllowedOutcomesForFormType) renders a 'Negative' option for APF. Because NEGATIVE IS now in allowedOutcomes, the 'keep selected outcome valid' guard (VerificationFormScreen.tsx:289-312) does NOT coerce it away. The agent's selection of NEGATIVE renders the POSITIVE form (normalizedPropertyApfOutcome falls through to 'POSITIVE'), but submit sends verificationOutcome:'NEGATIVE' (FormSubmissionService passes selectedOutcome verbatim). On the report side, canonicalize.ts only derives a label when code ∈ OUTCOME_CODES={POSITIVE,SHIFTED,NSP,ENTRY_RESTRICTED,UNTRACEABLE} — NEGATIVE is absent, so verificationOutcome stays the raw 'NEGATIVE'. The SDK APF_BODY branches on {{#eq outcome "Negative"}} and the eq helper is strict (a===b), so 'NEGATIVE' !== 'Negative' → NO body branch matches → the APF narrative body (including the Conclusion line, which lives inside each branch) renders COMPLETELY EMPTY. v1 actively handled an APF 'Negative' outcome in its report mapping (TemplateReportService snapshot: 'PROPERTY_APF | Negative' -> 'NEGATIVE_STOP'), so this is a parity regression for the NEGATIVE path. Currently LATENT because no active PROPERTY_APF FIELD_REPORT layout exists on the box (narrative is null for ALL APF today, sections still render); it goes live the instant an APF layout is activated.
findings.evidence: DB: PGPASSWORD=... psql -d crm2_dev -c "SELECT outcome_code,is_active FROM verification_unit_outcomes WHERE verification_type_code='PROPERTY_APF' AND outcome_code='NEGATIVE'" -> NEGATIVE | sort 2 | t. Server: apps/api/src/modules/reference/repository.ts:10-17 (listOutcomes, no per-type filtering) served via routes.ts:10 /verification-type-outcomes. Mobile ingest: SyncDownloadService.ts:238-259 (refreshVerificationTypeOutcomes -> setOutcomesFromSync); LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:178-197 (setOutcomesFromSync casts r.outcomeCode as AllOutcome into syncedOutcomesByFormType), :214-227 (getAllowedOutcomes returns synced list first). Picker: VerificationFormScreen.tsx:248-252 (outcomeOptions = getAllowedOutcomesForFormType), :289-312 (guard only coerces when NOT in allowedOutcomes). Submit: FormSubmissionService.ts:75-82 (verificationOutcome: selectedOutcome verbatim). Form fallback: LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:6978-6987 (normalizedPropertyApfOutcome -> POSITIVE). Report break: canonicalize.ts:18 (OUTCOME_CODES omits NEGATIVE), :96-100 (derive only if code in OUTCOME_CODES); fieldReportDefaults.ts:1098-1099 ({{#eq outcome "Positive"}}...) and the {{#eq outcome "Negative"}} branch; helpers.ts:306-310 (eq is strict a===b). Office verdict path is unaffected (KYC_RESULTS {POSITIVE,NEGATIVE,REFER,FRAUD}, cases.ts:95, shown via KYC_RESULT_LABELS).

findings.title: finalStatusNegative is labelled differently across the two APF report surfaces on the STOP/VACANT negative path (narrative 'Final Status' vs sections 'Final Status Negative')
findings.detail: On the STOP/VACANT (constructionActivity != SEEN) path the device captures the verdict in finalStatusNegative, not finalStatus. The NARRATIVE survives because canonicalize copies finalStatusNegative -> finalStatus before render and the APF_COLUMNS 'final_status' column reads property-apf.formData.finalStatus, so the narrative conclusion shows it under the v1 label 'Final Status'. But the SECTIONS surface is built from RAW formData (buildSections, NOT canonicalized), and SECTION_MAP['property-apf'] declares finalStatus but NOT finalStatusNegative; the field therefore falls into the 'Additional Details' catch-all and is auto-labelled via toLabel('finalStatusNegative') = 'Final Status Negative'. So the same verdict value appears under two different labels depending on which report surface (raw sections vs narrative) the user reads, and lands in a generic 'Additional Details' bucket in the sections view rather than the 'Verification Outcome & Status' group where finalStatus sits. Value is never lost (it is captured under a label), so this is cosmetic/consistency, not data loss; but it is a product-polish defect for the APF negative-verdict path.
findings.evidence: service.ts:28 (sections = buildSections(ctx.formData), raw) vs :55 (narrative on canonicalizeRenderContext(ctx)). canonicalize.ts:102-104 (copy finalStatusNegative -> finalStatus, narrative only). sectionMap.ts:469-529 SECTION_MAP['property-apf'] lists finalStatus (in 'Verification Outcome & Status') but has no finalStatusNegative entry anywhere. sections.ts:133-140 (catch-all 'Additional Details' uses toLabel(k)). SDK APF_COLUMNS maps final_status <- property-apf.formData.finalStatus only (fieldReportDefaults.ts:1083).

findings.title: Mobile embedded APF fallback outcome list and synced list disagree on order and membership (3 items, no NEGATIVE vs 4 items with NEGATIVE)
findings.detail: The embedded first-install-offline fallback LEGACY_OUTCOMES_BY_FORM_TYPE['property-apf'] = ['UNTRACEABLE','ENTRY_RESTRICTED','POSITIVE'] (3 items, deliberately excludes NEGATIVE — matching the intended 3-code design), but after the first successful sync the synced list (from the server catalog) replaces it with a 4-item list that re-introduces NEGATIVE and a different order (POSITIVE, NEGATIVE, ENTRY_RESTRICTED, UNTRACEABLE by sort_order). This means (a) the APF picker contents/order silently change between a fresh-install offline state and a post-sync state, and (b) it is the mechanism that surfaces the un-handled NEGATIVE option (the root enabler of APF-1). Flagged separately because even if APF-1 is fixed by filtering NEGATIVE server-side, this fallback/synced divergence is a standalone product-consistency smell worth aligning so the device's source-of-truth for the APF outcome set is singular.
findings.evidence: LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:145 (embedded fallback ['UNTRACEABLE','ENTRY_RESTRICTED','POSITIVE']) vs :178-197 setOutcomesFromSync (synced list built from server rows, ordered by sortOrder) and :214-227 getAllowedOutcomes (synced wins over embedded). DB sort order: psql verification_unit_outcomes PROPERTY_APF -> POSITIVE(1),NEGATIVE(2),ENTRY_RESTRICTED(3),UNTRACEABLE(4).

findings.title: NEGATIVE outcome is selectable on the mobile APF picker and breaks the narrative: device sends verificationOutcome='NEGATIVE', server canonicalize omits NEGATIVE from OUTCOME_CODES, so all four case-sensitive narrative branches fail and the APF report body renders empty
findings.detail: PROPERTY_APF NEGATIVE is designed to be captured implicitly inside the combined 'Positive & Negative' (POSITIVE) branch via finalStatus/finalStatusNegative (Path A — works, and is the only path the tests exercise). But a second, broken path exists (Path B): (1) the server reference endpoint returns ALL active outcome rows including PROPERTY_APF/NEGATIVE; (2) the device's setOutcomesFromSync casts each synced code as AllOutcome and stores it, so syncedOutcomesByFormType.get('property-apf') = [POSITIVE,NEGATIVE,ENTRY_RESTRICTED,UNTRACEABLE]; (3) getAllowedOutcomes returns the synced list (synced wins over the 3-element embedded fallback), so the picker maps a selectable {value:'NEGATIVE', label:'Negative'} option (label from the synced label map); (4) when the user picks it, the 'keep selected outcome valid' effect checks allowedOutcomes.includes(selectedOutcome) — NEGATIVE IS in the synced allowed list, so NO coercion fires and selectedOutcome stays 'NEGATIVE'; the form template still builds correctly because normalizedPropertyApfOutcome('NEGATIVE') falls through to POSITIVE; (5) FormSubmissionService sends verificationOutcome:selectedOutcome = 'NEGATIVE' verbatim. On the server: canonicalize OUTCOME_CODES = {POSITIVE,SHIFTED,NSP,ENTRY_RESTRICTED,UNTRACEABLE} has NO NEGATIVE, so the guard at canonicalize.ts:97 fails, deriveLabel never runs, and blob.verificationOutcome stays the raw 'NEGATIVE'. The narrative OUT column binds outcome <- property-apf.verificationOutcome = 'NEGATIVE'; APF_BODY branches are {{#eq outcome "Positive"|"Negative"|"ERT"|"Untraceable"}} and the eq helper uses strict === (helpers.ts:307), so 'NEGATIVE' !== 'Negative' and NONE of the four branches match -> the entire APF narrative body renders EMPTY (no remark text). Note finalStatus IS still copied for sections (canonicalize.ts:102-105 runs unconditionally), but that does not rescue the narrative because the outcome branch itself is skipped. No test covers verificationOutcome='NEGATIVE' (canonicalize.render.test.ts:94-112 and defaults.property-family.render.test.ts:138-210 all pass device code 'POSITIVE').
findings.evidence: DB (psql crm2_dev): verification_unit_outcomes PROPERTY_APF NEGATIVE display_label='Negative' sort_order=2 is_active=t. Server feeds it: reference/repository.ts:10-18 selects ALL active rows; mobile SyncDownloadService.ts:238-259 -> setOutcomesFromSync. Mobile LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:195 (list.map(r => r.outcomeCode as AllOutcome) — casts NEGATIVE), :221-224 (synced override wins in getAllowedOutcomes), :189 (syncedLabelByOutcome 'property-apf:NEGATIVE'='Negative'), :84-86 (getOutcomeLabel returns synced label first). Picker VerificationFormScreen.tsx:248-253 (maps every allowed outcome to an option, no filter); validation effect :288-312 (NEGATIVE in synced allowed list -> no coercion); submit FormSubmissionService.ts:81 (verificationOutcome:selectedOutcome). Server canonicalize.ts:18 (OUTCOME_CODES omits NEGATIVE), :96-100 (guard skips), :59-61 (deriveLabel only reached for known codes). SDK fieldReportDefaults.ts:96-102 (OUT binds outcome<-property-apf.verificationOutcome), :1104 ({{#eq outcome "Negative"}}). Strict eq: helpers.ts:306-310 (a===b).

findings.title: finalStatusNegative (STOP/VACANT path) is declared in neither sectionMap nor SDK APF_COLUMNS — sections surface labels it 'Final Status Negative' via the catch-all while the narrative labels it 'Final Status', a cross-surface label divergence
findings.detail: On the negative STOP/VACANT path the device writes the verdict to finalStatusNegative (not finalStatus). The narrative survives only because canonicalize copies finalStatusNegative -> finalStatus (canonicalize.ts:103-104) before render, and APF_COLUMNS maps final_status <- property-apf.formData.finalStatus only. But canonicalize is applied to the narrative context only (service.ts:55), NOT to the sections source (service.ts:28 buildSections runs on raw ctx.formData). sectionMap['property-apf'] lists finalStatus but not finalStatusNegative, so on the sections surface finalStatusNegative is unmapped and falls into the 'Additional Details' catch-all with toLabel('finalStatusNegative') = 'Final Status Negative' (sections.ts:130-140). Net effect: for the same submitted negative-path verdict, the narrative says 'Final Status' while the verbatim sections card says 'Final Status Negative' — two labels for the same datum on the two report surfaces of the same task.
findings.evidence: Mobile field exists on STOP/VACANT path: LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts ~6806 (finalStatusNegative). sectionMap.ts:473 declares finalStatus, no finalStatusNegative in the property-apf block (sectionMap.ts:465-539). SDK fieldReportDefaults.ts:1083 (only final_status<-finalStatus column). canonicalize.ts:103-104 (copies finalStatusNegative->finalStatus, narrative only). service.ts:28 (buildSections on raw ctx.formData) vs :55 (narrative on canonicalizeRenderContext(ctx)). sections.ts:134-138 + toLabel catch-all.

findings.title: Sections surface always shows the RAW uppercase outcome code while the narrative shows the verbose v1 label — canonicalize is intentionally applied to the narrative only, so the two report surfaces present different outcome vocabulary
findings.detail: buildSections leads with the raw blob.verificationOutcome verbatim (sections.ts:124-128), e.g. 'POSITIVE'/'NEGATIVE'/'ENTRY_RESTRICTED', because canonicalize is applied to the narrative context only (service.ts:55) and never to the sections source (service.ts:28). The narrative meanwhile shows the canonicalized v1 verbose label ('Positive'/'Negative'/'ERT'/'Untraceable'). This is documented as intended (canonicalize.ts:14 'The raw-fields section view is unaffected — it keeps showing exactly what was captured'), so it is by-design verbatim parity, not a defect. Flagged only as a casing/vocabulary divergence between the two surfaces that a reviewer might otherwise mistake for the APF-1 bug; confirmed intentional.
findings.evidence: service.ts:28 (buildSections(ctx.formData) — raw) vs :55 (renderNarrative(..., canonicalizeRenderContext(ctx))). sections.ts:124-128 (raw outcome row, no label derivation). canonicalize.ts:14 (design comment: sections unaffected, verbatim).

findings.title: Mobile outcome picker shows a top-level 'Negative' option that has no APF form, silently loads the POSITIVE form, and (latent) renders an empty narrative
findings.detail: The outcome catalog is DB-driven and synced to the device, but the mobile APF form model only knows 3 outcomes. DB verification_unit_outcomes has a PROPERTY_APF NEGATIVE row (is_active=t, sort_order 2). The backend reference endpoint returns ALL active rows unfiltered (apps/api/src/modules/reference/repository.ts:10-18). Mobile refreshVerificationTypeOutcomes() pipes every row into setOutcomesFromSync, which blindly casts r.outcomeCode as AllOutcome (LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:195) and stores it in syncedOutcomesByFormType. getAllowedOutcomes returns the synced list when present (LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:221-224), so after sync property-apf's allowed set becomes [POSITIVE, NEGATIVE, ENTRY_RESTRICTED, UNTRACEABLE]. VerificationFormScreen builds outcomeOptions straight from that list and renders one Picker.Item per option (VerificationFormScreen.tsx:248-253, 706-712), so the field agent sees a 4th picker entry labeled 'Negative' (DB display_label, which wins via syncedLabelByOutcome at LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:84-85,189). But legacyPropertyApfFieldsByOutcome has no NEGATIVE key (LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:6989-6996) and normalizedPropertyApfOutcome('NEGATIVE') returns 'POSITIVE' (LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:6978-6987), so selecting 'Negative' silently loads the POSITIVE 'Positive & Negative' combined form while selectedOutcome/verificationOutcome is set to the raw 'NEGATIVE' code (VerificationFormScreen.tsx:399-408,418). The intended design captures the negative verdict INSIDE the POSITIVE form via finalStatus/finalStatusNegative, so 'Negative' as a separate top-level pick is a redundant, confusing duplicate path. Downstream (HOP-5, latent): canonicalize.ts OUTCOME_CODES omits NEGATIVE (canonicalize.ts:18), so deriveLabel never runs (canonicalize.ts:96-100); the raw 'NEGATIVE' reaches the narrative and the case-sensitive {{#eq outcome "Negative"}} branch (fieldReportDefaults.ts:1104) does not match 'NEGATIVE' -> the entire APF narrative renders empty. Latent today: only RESIDENCE has an active FIELD_REPORT layout (verified), so APF narrative is null until a layout is activated.
findings.evidence: DB: psql 'SELECT outcome_code,display_label,sort_order,is_active FROM verification_unit_outcomes WHERE verification_type_code=PROPERTY_APF' -> NEGATIVE | Negative | 2 | t. Backend serves all active: apps/api/src/modules/reference/repository.ts:10-18 (no outcome filter). Mobile injects: crm-mobile-native/src/sync/SyncDownloadService.ts:238-259; crm-mobile-native/src/screens/forms/LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:178-197 (cast :195), :214-226 (synced wins :221-224). Picker renders it: crm-mobile-native/src/screens/forms/VerificationFormScreen.tsx:248-253,706-712. No NEGATIVE form / silent POSITIVE fallback: LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:6978-6996. Narrative gate skips NEGATIVE: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/canonicalize.ts:18,96-100; case-sensitive branch apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/fieldReportDefaults.ts:1104.

findings.title: Report sectionMap omits addressLocatable, tpcConfirmation1/2 and finalStatusNegative -> they fall to 'Additional Details' with malformed auto-labels ('Tpc Confirmation1')
findings.detail: sectionMap['property-apf'] (sectionMap.ts:465-539) places 33 refs but never lists four fields the mobile APF forms actually capture: addressLocatable (POSITIVE+ERT, required - LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:6513,6822), tpcConfirmation1 and tpcConfirmation2 (POSITIVE - LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:6681,6711), and finalStatusNegative (STOP/VACANT verdict - LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:6805-6817). mappedSections() routes every unplaced key into a trailing 'Additional Details' section labeled via toLabel() (sections.ts:130-141). I ran the actual toLabel algorithm: tpcConfirmation1 -> 'Tpc Confirmation1' (no space before the digit, 'Tpc' not 'TPC'), tpcConfirmation2 -> 'Tpc Confirmation2', addressLocatable -> 'Address Locatable', finalStatusNegative -> 'Final Status Negative'. So on the always-rendered sections view (CaseDetailPage.tsx:1847-1858) the agent's TPC-confirmation answers and the address-locatable answer appear under a generic 'Additional Details' heading with the ugly 'Tpc Confirmation1' label instead of being grouped into the existing 'TPC (Third-Party Confirmation)' / 'Verification Outcome & Status' sections with clean labels. Pure grouping/label-quality defect; values are not lost (never-lose-a-field), but the placement and casing are wrong. Note this is a TYPE-WIDE pattern: the same tpcConfirmation1/2 omission exists in the property-apf block while OTHER slug blocks DO map them (sectionMap.ts:91,180,...).
findings.evidence: sectionMap property-apf refs (full unique set) does NOT contain addressLocatable/tpcConfirmation1/tpcConfirmation2/finalStatusNegative: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sectionMap.ts:465-539. Catch-all + toLabel: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sections.ts:38-48,130-141. toLabel output reproduced by running the exact algorithm: tpcConfirmation1->'Tpc Confirmation1', addressLocatable->'Address Locatable', finalStatusNegative->'Final Status Negative'. Mobile captures these: crm-mobile-native/src/screens/forms/LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:6513,6681,6711,6805-6817,6822. Web sections render: apps/web/src/features/cases/CaseDetailPage.tsx:1847-1858.

findings.title: Duplicate metPersonName entry in property-apf sectionMap is dead config (second 'Name of Met Person (ERT)' row never renders)
findings.detail: The 'Met Person Details' section lists metPersonName twice: 'Met Person Name' (sectionMap.ts:492) then 'Name of Met Person (ERT)' (sectionMap.ts:494). mappedSections dedups by ref, first occurrence wins (sections.ts:116-119), so the second row is unreachable for every outcome. For an ERT submission, metPersonName IS captured (LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:6853) and renders under the POSITIVE-path label 'Met Person Name' rather than the ERT-specific 'Name of Met Person (ERT)'. Cosmetic only (the value renders), but the second map entry is dead and the ERT label intent is silently lost.
findings.evidence: Duplicate ref: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sectionMap.ts:492 and :494 (both ref:'metPersonName'). Dedup-first-wins: apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sections.ts:116-119. ERT captures metPersonName: crm-mobile-native/src/screens/forms/LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:6853-6857.

findings.title: Mobile APF capture labels for the TPC pair are swapped vs their field semantics (report self-corrects by ref)
findings.detail: On the POSITIVE form the two TPC fields are labeled inversely to what they capture: tpcMetPerson1 (a select - the person/relation) is labeled 'Third Party Confirmation 1' and tpcName1 (a text - the name) is labeled 'TPC Met Person 1' (LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:6661-6679). v1 semantics confirm tpcMetPerson1=tpc_met_person1 (relation) and tpcName1=tpc_name1 (name) (CRM-BACKEND/src/utils/propertyApfFormFieldMapping.ts:76-78), and the crm2 report relabels by ref correctly (tpcName1->'TPC 1 Name', tpcMetPerson1->'TPC 1 Relation', sectionMap.ts:516-517). So the field agent on mobile reads confusing/swapped labels at capture time, but the web report shows the correct labels. Defect is mobile-capture-side UX only; the ERT form labels the same fields more neutrally ('TPC Met Person 1' / 'TPC Name 1', LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:6865-6866), so the labeling is also inconsistent between APF outcomes. Mobile lives in the separate branch-only repo, so this is informational for the crm2 audit.

findings.title: Field-photo upload idempotency-replay returns another user's attachment rows (geoLocation/storage-key/filename) with no actor/case binding
findings.detail: In the device FIELD_PHOTO upload path, the idempotency replay short-circuit is keyed ONLY by the attacker-controlled operationBase (the `Idempotency-Key` header or `operationId` form field) with no ownership check. uploadAttachments() first calls ownedCaseId(taskId, actor) -- which only proves the attacker owns the URL task -- then immediately does `const cached = await repo.fieldAttachmentsByOperation(operationBase); if (cached.length > 0) return uploadResult(cached, [], caseId, taskId, ...)` (service.ts:200,211-212). fieldAttachmentsByOperation runs `SELECT <FIELD_ATTACH_COLS> FROM case_attachments WHERE kind='FIELD_PHOTO' AND deleted_at IS NULL AND split_part(operation_id,':',1)=$1` (repository.ts:1571-1578) with NO case_id / task_id / uploaded_by filter. operation_id is GLOBALLY unique (DB: `uq_case_attachments_operation` UNIQUE on operation_id, no per-user scope -- confirmed via psql). FIELD_ATTACH_COLS exposes `geo_location, storage_key AS url, original_name, thumbnail_key AS thumbnail_url, id, created_at` (repository.ts:309-310), and DeviceAttachment (sdk/sync.ts:91-103) carries geoLocation (exact lat/lng/accuracy/timestamp of the victim's verification location -- PII), url (storage_key), originalName, id straight into the JSON response. The returned caseId/taskId come from the ATTACKER's URL, masking the mismatch. So any FIELD_AGENT (holds task.execute) who supplies a victim's operationId as their Idempotency-Key receives the victim's field-photo metadata + precise GPS for any verification type (PROPERTY_APF incl POSITIVE/NEGATIVE/ENTRY_RESTRICTED/UNTRACEABLE). The binary download stays scope-guarded (the storage_key string is leaked but the /field-photos/:id/download route 404s out-of-scope), so the concrete leak is the geoLocation + filenames + ids, not the image bytes. Mitigating factor: the legitimate client generates operationId via uuidv4 (mobile src/services/SyncQueue.ts:322 etc.), so exploitation requires obtaining/guessing a victim's random UUID -- this is a defense-in-depth gap, not a trivially-enumerable IDOR. There is NO crm2-side idempotency middleware to scope the key (the mobile comment 'Backend's idempotencyMiddleware' in AttachmentUploader.ts:117 refers to v1; grep of apps/api/src/http + platform finds none).
findings.evidence: apps/api/src/modules/verification-tasks/service.ts:200-212 (ownedCaseId then unscoped fieldAttachmentsByOperation replay); apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:1571-1578 (replay query keyed only on split_part(operation_id,':',1)); apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:309-310 (FIELD_ATTACH_COLS exposes geo_location + storage_key); packages/sdk/src/sync.ts:91-103 (DeviceAttachment.geoLocation/url in response); controller.ts:75,82,88 (operationBase from attacker-controlled idempotency-key header / operationId body); DB proof: `uq_case_attachments_operation UNIQUE btree(operation_id) WHERE operation_id IS NOT NULL` (psql, no user/case scope)

findings.title: Attachment tamper-detection result (hash_verified / client_sha256 mismatch) is recorded but never surfaced to any reviewer
findings.detail: The client_sha256 transit-tamper check is computed at upload (clientSha === sha256(received bytes)) and, on mismatch, only logger.warn'd server-side ('field photo hash mismatch') and stored as case_attachments.hash_verified=false; the photo is accepted regardless (service.ts:232-234, matching the documented v1 'verified-or-logged, never rejected' design). hash_verified and client_sha256 are written by insertFieldAttachment (repository.ts:1530,1542,1555-1556) but NEVER read back by any read path -- listFieldPhotos (repository.ts:1621-1631) selects geo_location/reverse_geocoded_address/original_name but not hash_verified; the field report (fieldReports/*) and web CaseDetailPage Field Photos card never reference it (grep finds zero non-test, non-insert reads of hash_verified/client_sha256 across cases, fieldReports, sdk, web). Net effect: a photo whose transit hash failed (possible tamper / corruption in flight) flows through to the office report with no visible indicator; an auditor cannot tell a verified photo from an unverified one. This is integrity-VISIBILITY, not an integrity break (the stored sha256 of the stripped bytes is self-consistent with what the download serves). Applies to all FIELD types incl PROPERTY_APF all outcomes. Matches a documented design tradeoff, so a deferred ratchet (surface a 'tamper-check failed' chip on the Field Photos card / report) rather than an immediate blocker.
findings.evidence: apps/api/src/modules/verification-tasks/service.ts:232-234 (mismatch -> logger.warn only, never rejected); apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:1530,1542,1555-1556 (hash_verified/client_sha256 written) vs repository.ts:1621-1631 (listFieldPhotos read set omits them); grep across cases/fieldReports/sdk/web: no non-test read of hash_verified/clientSha256

findings.detail: flatStatus is the PROPERTY_INDIVIDUAL form's primary visit-status field (door Open/Closed), collected as required in POSITIVE/NSP/ENTRY_RESTRICTED forms (mobile LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:7093,7279,7431; option alias flatStatus->flatStatusApf Open/Closed at :7028-7040). Consequence: flatStatus is preserved (never-lose-a-field catch-all, sections.ts:140) but surfaces ONLY in the trailing unlabeled 'Additional Details' section auto-Title-cased to 'Flat Status', never in the primary status group. This is a v1-parity regression: v1 explicitly gave flatStatus its OWN dedicated column 'matches residence house_status / office office_status pattern' (CRM-BACKEND/src/utils/propertyIndividualFormFieldMapping.ts:36-39, and a first-class RELEVANT field at :174,:291). The door-open/locked sense IS still reflected in the canonicalized narrative LABEL (canonicalize.ts:64-65 reads flatStatus), so this is a presentation/grouping defect (operator sees the field in the wrong, generic section), not data loss or a broken flow.

findings.title: Outcome-picker labels/order drift: synced DB labels are silently discarded (short-vs-full verification_type_code key mismatch) → agent sees hardcoded "Positive & Door Locked" / typo "NSP & NSP Door Locked"
findings.detail: The mobile outcome picker (VerificationFormScreen.tsx:248-253) builds each option's label via getOutcomeLabelForFormType → getOutcomeLabel, which prefers a server-synced label (LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:83-86) and only falls back to a hardcoded map. The synced labels/outcomes are loaded by setOutcomesFromSync() (LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:178-196), which resolves each row's verification_type_code through a PRIVATE map FORM_TYPE_KEY_BY_VTYPE_CODE that ONLY contains SHORT codes: PIV/RV/OV/PAV/… (LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:154-164). But the API reference endpoint returns the FULL code: repository.ts:12 selects verification_type_code, db.ts:18-19 camelizes it, and the DB stores only full codes. So FORM_TYPE_KEY_BY_VTYPE_CODE['PROPERTY_INDIVIDUAL'] is undefined → `if (!key) continue;` (line 185) skips EVERY row → both syncedLabelByOutcome and syncedOutcomesByFormType stay empty. Consequences for PI: (a) labels fall back to the hardcoded individualLabelByOutcome (LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:101-107): POSITIVE → "Positive & Door Locked" (presumptive — the POSITIVE form supports BOTH flatStatus Open and Closed, lines 7093/7102/7109, so labeling the option "Door Locked" before the agent has chosen is misleading) and NSP → "NSP & NSP Door Locked" (doubled "NSP" — a label typo), instead of the DB display_labels "Positive" / "No Such Person"; (b) the allowed-outcome list falls back to the embedded LEGACY_OUTCOMES_BY_FORM_TYPE['property-individual'] = ['NSP','ENTRY_RESTRICTED','POSITIVE','UNTRACEABLE'] (lines 139-144), presenting NSP (a negative outcome) FIRST, vs the DB sort_order POSITIVE(1)/NSP(2)/ENTRY_RESTRICTED(3)/UNTRACEABLE(4). The app's general resolver BACKEND_FORM_TYPE_TO_KEY (formTypeKey.ts:52) DOES map the full code correctly, proving the short-code-only map in setOutcomesFromSync is the lone outlier, not a system-wide convention.
findings.evidence: Mobile: crm-mobile-native/src/screens/forms/LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:154-164 (FORM_TYPE_KEY_BY_VTYPE_CODE has only PIV/RV/OV/…), :184-185 (`const key = FORM_TYPE_KEY_BY_VTYPE_CODE[r.verificationTypeCode]; if (!key) continue;`), :101-107 (hardcoded individualLabelByOutcome POSITIVE='Positive & Door Locked', NSP='NSP & NSP Door Locked'), :139-144 (embedded order NSP first), :83-86 (synced wins only when present); src/screens/forms/VerificationFormScreen.tsx:248-253 (picker uses getOutcomeLabelForFormType); src/utils/formTypeKey.ts:52 (BACKEND_FORM_TYPE_TO_KEY correctly maps full 'PROPERTY_INDIVIDUAL'→'property-individual'). API/DB: apps/api/src/modules/reference/repository.ts:10-18 (SELECT verification_type_code … FROM verification_unit_outcomes); apps/api/src/platform/db.ts:18-19 (camelize → verificationTypeCode); packages/sdk/src/reference.ts:14 (verificationTypeCode: string). DB proof (crm2_dev): `SELECT DISTINCT verification_type_code FROM verification_unit_outcomes` → {BUILDER,BUSINESS,DSA_CONNECTOR,NOC,OFFICE,PROPERTY_APF,PROPERTY_INDIVIDUAL,RESIDENCE,RESIDENCE_CUM_OFFICE} (no short codes); `… WHERE verification_type_code='PROPERTY_INDIVIDUAL' ORDER BY sort_order` → display_label = Positive / No Such Person / Entry Restricted / Untraceable (sort 1-4).

findings.title: Attachment tamper-detection (hash_verified / client_sha256) is captured but never surfaced or enforced — evidence tampering is silent
findings.detail: On field-photo upload the server computes hashVerified = (clientSha256 === sha256(received bytes)) and persists hash_verified + client_sha256 on every case_attachments FIELD_PHOTO row (apps/api/src/modules/verification-tasks/service.ts:233-235,255-256; cases/repository.ts:1542-1556). The transit check itself is correct. But a mismatch is logged-only (logger.warn at service.ts:233-234) and NEVER rejected — and, critically, hash_verified is dead data: a repo-wide grep shows it is WRITTEN but never READ by any query, report assembler, or web surface. The field-report read context (fieldReports/repository.ts:47-89) and the CaseReportPhoto block (caseReports/service.ts:108-118: id, photoType, url, lat, lng, accuracy, reverseGeocodedAddress, captureTime, mapImage) carry no integrity flag. Consequence for PROPERTY_INDIVIDUAL (>=5 mandatory evidence photos per task): a device that uploads a substituted/edited image with a non-matching client hash produces hash_verified=false, and no verifier or auditor in the web case-detail or generated report can ever see that the integrity check failed. The captured tamper signal is non-actionable. Separately, the at-rest sha256 column stores sha256(EXIF-stripped bytes) (repository.ts:1517) which by design cannot be re-derived from client_sha256, so there is also no durable at-rest integrity re-verification — but the surfacing gap is the core defect.
findings.evidence: WRITE side: apps/api/src/modules/verification-tasks/service.ts:233 `if (clientSha && !hashVerified) logger.warn(...)`, :255 `hashVerified,` into insert; apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:1542 INSERT lists `client_sha256, hash_verified`. READ side (absent): grep of apps/api/src/modules + apps/web/src for hash_verified/hashVerified/client_sha256 returns ONLY the write-side rows (no consumer). caseReports/service.ts:108-118 CaseReportPhoto shape has no integrity field; fieldReports/repository.ts:47-89 ctx has no attachment integrity. findings.title: REFUTED: report/photo/download routes are IDOR-safe and scope-guarded — no cross-user/cross-type leak
findings.detail: I checked every PROPERTY_INDIVIDUAL evidence-exposure route for IDOR and confirmed each is permission-gated (CASE_VIEW) AND scope-guarded in SQL. attachmentUrl/deleteAttachment resolve via attachmentForAccess (repository.ts:1703-1730) which AND-binds id+case_id and applies taskScopePredicate; fieldPhotoDownload/fieldPhotosZip/fieldPhotoStaticMap resolve via listFieldPhotoFiles/fieldPhotoForGeocode/caseVisible (repository.ts:1471,1639,1703) all applying caseScopePredicate/taskScopePredicate; the field-report read is keyed on taskId AND caseId with caseScopePredicate (fieldReports/repository.ts:40-46) and joins the real verification_unit_id so a task always renders its true type. Presigned URLs are minted only after the scope check (cases/service.ts:626-628). The Google key is server-side-only: fieldPhotoStaticMap returns PNG Buffer bytes, the controller streams image/png with nosniff (controller.ts:302-306), the key is appended in createGoogleStaticMap (staticmap/index.ts:70) and never serialized to the client; caseReports inlines the map as a data URI for the same reason (service.ts:96). Recorded as refuted so reviewers don't re-litigate.
findings.evidence: apps/api/src/modules/cases/routes.ts:51-93 (all field-photo/attachment/report routes authorize(PERMISSIONS.CASE_VIEW)); repository.ts:1703-1730 attachmentForAccess AND id=$1 AND case_id=$2 + scoped taskLeg; repository.ts:1639-1665 listFieldPhotoFiles scoped taskLeg; service.ts:626-628 attachmentUrl signs only after attachmentForAccess; platform/staticmap/index.ts:61-90 key server-side, returns Buffer; controller.ts:297-309 streams PNG.

findings.title: REFUTED: mobile clearAllData covers all PII tables on user-change — no prior-user PROPERTY_INDIVIDUAL PII leak
findings.detail: I enumerated the mobile schema (15 tables) and confirmed clearAllData wipes the 13 that can hold a prior user's PII/operational data on user-change, including the cross-user-read projection tables and form_templates added at the ADR-0054 review gate. The PROPERTY_INDIVIDUAL evidence chain on-device (tasks, form_submissions blobs with met-person/owner/neighbour names, attachments rows + the photos/ filesystem dir, notifications carrying case/task numbers, the unscoped task_list/detail/dashboard projections, and user_session) are ALL in the wipe list and the photos directory is unlinked+recreated. The only 2 omitted tables, verification_type_outcomes and revoke_reasons, are static reference catalogs (id/code/label/sort_order) with no user PII (schema.ts:302-327). Recorded as refuted.

findings.title: Uploaded KYC verification document never appears in any client report — the verdict is reported without the evidence it rests on
findings.detail: For a KYC_DOCUMENT unit the uploaded document IS the verification evidence (every KYC unit has required_attachments=[{min:1,type:DOCUMENT}], DB-verified). But KYC documents are stored as kind='OFFICE_REF' (insertAttachment omits kind → DB default), and the CASE_REPORT — the client-facing deliverable — only surfaces (a) per-task outcome+remark text and (b) a Photo Manifest filtered to kind='FIELD_PHOTO'. So the client report for a PAN/Aadhaar/Passport task prints e.g. 'PAN Verification — Positive' with NO trace of the actual document that was checked. The per-task 'Field Report' web section is likewise FIELD-shaped (form_data sections + FIELD_REPORT narrative, both empty for KYC) and shows 'No field submission yet for this task.' under a header literally titled 'Field Report'. Net: the KYC track delivers an outcome with no attached evidence anywhere in the report surface — a document verification whose document is invisible to the report consumer.
findings.evidence: Photo manifest is FIELD_PHOTO-only: apps/api/src/modules/caseReports/repository.ts:37 `WHERE ca.case_id = $1 AND ca.kind = 'FIELD_PHOTO'`. KYC docs default to OFFICE_REF: DB chk_case_attachments_kind = {OFFICE_REF,FIELD_PHOTO}; insert at apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts (insertAttachment omits the kind column → default OFFICE_REF). Per-task report carries only outcome/remark/narrative/sections: apps/api/src/modules/caseReports/service.ts:140-145. KYC has no form_data → fieldReports buildSections returns [] and no FIELD_REPORT layout → narrative null (apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/service.ts:28,36-44). Web KYC body renders 'No field submission yet for this task.' apps/web/src/features/cases/CaseDetailPage.tsx:1843-1844. DB: every KYC unit required_attachments='[{"min":1,"type":"DOCUMENT"}]' (59/59, queried live).

findings.title: No guard binds verification-unit kind to the operator-chosen visit type — a KYC unit can be created/assigned as a FIELD task and down-synced to a device that has no KYC form (uncompletable)
findings.detail: The add-task unit picker lists ALL CPV-enabled units with no kind exposed, and visit type is a free manual pick (FIELD/OFFICE). The task INSERT stores visit_type verbatim with no check against the unit's kind/worker_role, and the eligible-assignee pool keys off assignment_pool_roles[visitType] (FIELD→FIELD_AGENT) independent of unit kind. So an operator can create a KYC_DOCUMENT unit (worker_role=KYC_VERIFIER, no mobile form) as visitType='FIELD', assign it to a field agent, and it will down-sync to the device (the sync filter is purely assigned_to=device-user with NO kind exclusion). The mobile app has no KYC form template (FormTemplateService maps only the 9 FIELD slugs), so the field agent receives a task they cannot complete — a structurally broken flow. Latent today (0 such tasks in dev) but unblocked by operator error; there is no DB FK/check nor service guard.

Visit type is a free pick: AddTasksForm.tsx:372-396. INSERT stores visit_type verbatim, no kind check: apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:644-666. Down-sync filter has no kind exclusion: apps/api/src/modules/sync/repository.ts:98 `where=['ct.assigned_to = $1', ...]`. Mobile form map is FIELD-only: CRM-APP-MONOREPO-PROD/crm-mobile-native/src/services/forms/FormTemplateService.ts:365-373 (residence/RCO/property-apf… no KYC/PAN_CARD). DB: assignment_pool_roles FIELD→FIELD_AGENT, OFFICE→KYC_VERIFIER (queried live).

findings.title: A KYC document-verification task can be completed with ZERO documents — required_attachments (min 1 DOCUMENT on every KYC unit) is never enforced at completion
findings.detail: Every KYC unit carries required_attachments=[{min:1,type:DOCUMENT}] and the SDK validator even fails a KYC unit that lacks it. But that requirement is enforced ONLY in the verification-units admin CRUD — nothing on the completion path checks it. completeTask validates only result (enum) + remark (non-empty) + status transition + OCC version; there is no attachment-count check against the unit's required_attachments. So a back-office completer can record 'PAN Verification — Positive' with no PAN document uploaded at all. The whole point of the KYC desk path (document review) can be bypassed, producing a verified-positive outcome with no underlying document.
findings.evidence: required_attachments enforced only in admin: grep shows the column is read/written/imported solely under apps/api/src/modules/verificationUnits/* and SDK packages/sdk/src/verificationUnit.ts:99-100; zero references in cases/sync/fieldReports completion paths. Completion checks only result+remark+status+version: apps/api/src/modules/cases/service.ts:356-363 (CompleteTaskSchema.parse + requireVersion + status guard, no attachment check); schema is just {result, remark}: packages/sdk/src/cases.ts:492-495. DB: all 59 KYC units have required_attachments='[{"min":1,"type":"DOCUMENT"}]' (queried live).

findings.title: Per-unit result_set is editable in admin but completely dead — KYC completion outcomes are hardwired to KYC_RESULTS, so customizing a unit's result set silently has no effect
findings.detail: Each KYC unit exposes an editable result_set column (admin grid + import/export), defaulting to {Positive,Negative,Refer,Fraud}. An admin can edit it per unit, expecting to change the outcomes a completer may pick for that unit. But result_set is consumed ONLY in the verificationUnits admin module — the completion form and CompleteTaskSchema are both hardwired to the constant KYC_RESULTS={POSITIVE,NEGATIVE,REFER,FRAUD} regardless of the unit. So per-unit result_set customization is a no-op: the picker always offers the same 4 outcomes. This is a misleading-affordance / dead-config product defect (and the silent casing split — Title-case result_set vs UPPERCASE KYC_RESULTS — is a latent trap, though I confirmed no code currently compares the two so there is no live mismatch bug).
findings.evidence: result_set consumed only in admin CRUD: grep result_set/resultSet hits only apps/api/src/modules/verificationUnits/{repository,service}.ts and packages/sdk/src/verificationUnit.ts — none in cases/fieldReports/caseReports. Completion form hardwired to KYC_RESULTS: apps/web/src/features/cases/CaseDetailPage.tsx:1116-1118; CompleteTaskSchema.result = z.enum(KYC_RESULTS): packages/sdk/src/cases.ts:493 with KYC_RESULTS at :95. DB: result_set Title-case {Positive,Negative,Refer,Fraud} identical for all 59 KYC units; chk_case_task_outcome UPPERCASE {POSITIVE,NEGATIVE,REFER,FRAUD} (both queried live).

findings.title: No binding between verification unit kind/worker_role and operator-chosen visitType at create OR assign -> a KYC_DOCUMENT unit can be routed FIELD, down-synced to mobile, and bricked (null form template)
findings.detail: At task creation the operator freely picks visitType (FIELD/OFFICE) with zero validation against the verification unit's own kind/worker_role. The web unit picker shows only the unit name (AddTasksForm.tsx:343-356) and visitType is a free manual <select> over VISIT_TYPES (AddTasksForm.tsx:372-396). The SDK AvailableUnit interface carries only {verificationUnitId, code, name} with no kind/reportTemplateType/workerRole (cases.ts:280-284) and the availableUnits SELECT does not select vu.kind (repository.ts:508-519). AddTasksSchema validates only field-presence refinements, never unit.kind vs visitType (cases.ts:410-453). Server-side service.addTasks checks only (1) CPV-enabled, (2) applicant ownership, (3) assignee in the visit-type pool (service.ts:226-251) -- it never fetches or compares the unit's kind/worker_role. The INSERT stores visit_type verbatim from operator input (repository.ts:644-685). eligibleAssigneesForNew keys the pool purely off assignment_pool_roles[visitType] (repository.ts:794-823), NOT the unit's worker_role: visitType='FIELD' -> FIELD_AGENT pool. The same gap exists on the later assign path (AssignTaskSchema takes a free visitType, cases.ts:477-482). CONSEQUENCE chain: a KYC_DOCUMENT unit assigned visitType='FIELD' to a FIELD_AGENT who holds the task's territory down-syncs to that device because the sync filter is purely ct.assigned_to=$1 with NO kind/report_template_type filter (sync/repository.ts:96-101); on the device the form builder returns null for any non-FIELD type -- buildLegacyTemplateForFormType default branch (LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:7752-7753) -- so the task has no fillable form, no >=5 photos path, and cannot be submitted: a dead, stuck task on the device. Conversely a FIELD_VISIT unit could be mis-set OFFICE, skipping the territory leg and dropping it on the KYC_VERIFIER desk pool.

findings.title: KYC required_attachments (>=1 DOCUMENT) is never enforced at completion -- a KYC verification can be finalized with zero document evidence
findings.detail: Every KYC_DOCUMENT unit mandates at least one DOCUMENT attachment, enforced ONLY at unit-definition time (SDK verificationUnit.ts:99-100 fails a KYC unit defined with no requiredAttachments). But that requirement is never checked when a KYC task is completed. completeTask validates only result (KYC_RESULTS), remark, OCC version, and status transition (service.ts:356-377; CompleteTaskSchema cases.ts:492-495) -- it does not count case_attachments for the task or consult verification_units.required_attachments. A grep across apps/api/src, packages/sdk/src, apps/web/src finds required_attachments/requiredAttachments referenced ONLY in the verificationUnits admin module (grid/import/export + the definition-time schema check) -- there is ZERO consumer in the cases/completion path. So a BACKEND_USER/MANAGER/TEAM_LEADER can record a Positive/Negative/Refer/Fraud KYC result on a task that has no uploaded document at all, defeating the document-upload premise of the KYC desk path. NOTE: the shared trace asserted KYC required_attachments='[]'; the live DB shows '[{"min":1,"type":"DOCUMENT"}]' for all KYC units, so the requirement is real and the non-enforcement is a genuine integrity gap.
findings.evidence: DB proof (port 54329): SELECT DISTINCT required_attachments FROM verification_units WHERE kind='KYC_DOCUMENT' -> [{"min":1,"type":"DOCUMENT"}] (single distinct value, all 59 units). Definition-time enforcement only: /Users/mayurkulkarni/Downloads/crm2/packages/sdk/src/verificationUnit.ts:99-100 (KYC_DOCUMENT requires >=1 required attachment). Completion never checks it: /Users/mayurkulkarni/Downloads/crm2/apps/api/src/modules/cases/service.ts:356-377 (completeTask -- only schema parse + version + status guard), /Users/mayurkulkarni/Downloads/crm2/apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:935-957 (UPDATE sets verification_outcome/status='COMPLETED', no attachment count). Grep of apps/api/src, packages/sdk/src, apps/web/src: required_attachments/requiredAttachments appears only in verificationUnits/{repository,service}.ts and verificationUnit.ts -- zero hits in any cases/* file.

findings.title: Web 'Field Report' card renders KYC desk tasks unfiltered under a FIELD-only header ('No field submission yet') -- KYC-vs-FIELD label drift
findings.detail: MobileReportSection iterates data.tasks with no filter on visit_type / report_template_type / unit kind, rendering a TaskFieldReport row for every task including KYC desk tasks, under a header literally labelled 'Field Report' (CaseDetailPage.tsx:1774-1796, wired at :218). A KYC task has no form_data, so the field-report endpoint returns a clean 200 with sections=[] and narrative=null (service.ts:20-57; buildSections(null) -> []), and the web body then prints 'No field submission yet for this task.' (CaseDetailPage.tsx:1843-1844). This is purely cosmetic -- no data corruption, no wrong outcome set, and I confirmed there is no slug collision: verificationType=vu.code (e.g. PAN_CARD, repository.ts:51) never matches a FIELD_REPORT layout so narrative stays null. But it is a real KYC-vs-FIELD labeling drift: a desk/document-upload task is shown to the office under a 'Field Report' heading as if it had a field path it does not.

findings.title: REFUTED: Title-case result_set vs UPPERCASE completion enum is latent (display/admin-only, no completion-path consumer)
findings.detail: I checked the candidate casing-split drift and it is NOT a runtime defect. verification_units.result_set is Title-case {Positive,Negative,Refer,Fraud} (live DB) while the completion outcome enum KYC_RESULTS and chk_case_task_outcome are UPPERCASE {POSITIVE,NEGATIVE,REFER,FRAUD}. A grep of apps/api/src, packages/sdk/src, apps/web/src shows result_set is read/written ONLY in the verificationUnits admin module (grid value, import default, persistence) -- it has ZERO consumers in the cases/completion path. The completion form uses the hardcoded UPPERCASE KYC_RESULTS for every task (CaseDetailPage.tsx:1116) and the write persists that to verification_outcome (repository.ts:944,950), matching the DB check. So per-unit result_set customization would have no effect on the pickable outcomes, and no code compares a stored outcome to result_set verbatim. Latent inconsistency worth a future cleanup, but not a current matching defect. I likewise refuted (a) the 4 KYC_RESULTS being offered on FIELD tasks -- that is the by-design single office final-status verdict (ADR-0025, CaseDetailPage.tsx:1089-1091 comment), distinct from the field form's POSITIVE/SHIFTED/NSP/... outcome codes -- and (b) OFFICE_REF as the KYC attachment kind -- that is the intended generic desk-doc default (chk_case_attachments_kind={OFFICE_REF,FIELD_PHOTO}).

verificationTypes: All 59 KYC_DOCUMENT units (ids 10-68: PAN_CARD, AADHAR_CARD, PASSPORT, VOTER_ID, DRIVING_LICENSE, ... ); also affects any OFFICE_REF case-level attachment on FIELD cases
findings.title: IDOR: case-level KYC documents (PAN/Aadhaar/passport, pii_sensitive) are served and deletable cross-scope via the attachment URL/delete routes
findings.detail: The serve handler caseService.attachmentUrl (apps/api/src/modules/cases/service.ts:625) and deleteAttachment (service.ts:632) both call repo.attachmentForAccess(caseId, attachmentId, scope) WITHOUT the repo.caseVisible() precondition that every sibling handler enforces (listAttachments service.ts:493, listFieldPhotos :500, fieldPhotosZip :563, uploadAttachment :603 all call caseVisible first). attachmentForAccess (repository.ts:1703-1725) builds its task-leg as `AND (ca.task_id IS NULL OR EXISTS(... task assigned-to / case created-by in scope ...))`. For a CASE-LEVEL attachment (ca.task_id IS NULL) the left disjunct is always TRUE, so the WHERE collapses to `ca.id=$1 AND ca.case_id=$2 AND deleted_at IS NULL` with NO data-scope filter at all. KYC reference documents are uploaded case-level by default: the web office-reference upload (apps/web/src/features/cases/CaseDetailPage.tsx:1282) appends `?taskId=` ONLY when a task is targeted; the bare-case upload stores task_id NULL. The routes are gated only by the coarse role permission authorize(PERMISSIONS.CASE_VIEW) (routes.ts:92-93), NOT by per-case scope. Net: any authenticated user whose role holds case.view (a wide set incl. KYC_VERIFIER, FIELD_AGENT, every desk role) can, with a caseId+attachmentId pair (UUIDs, but discoverable via referers, logs, the list endpoint on any one in-scope case, or enumeration of attachment ids surfaced elsewhere), obtain a presigned download URL for a KYC identity document (PAN/Aadhaar/passport, verification_units.pii_sensitive=true for ids 10-68) on a case in a DIFFERENT territory / not created by them / with no task assigned to them. The same gap lets them soft-delete that document and best-effort remove the object (deleteAttachment service.ts:632-636) -> cross-scope tamper/erasure of PII evidence. The inline comment 'scope checked here ... IDOR-safe' (service.ts:623-624) is incorrect for case-level rows.
findings.evidence: Skipped guard: apps/api/src/modules/cases/service.ts:625 (attachmentUrl) and :632 (deleteAttachment) call attachmentForAccess directly; contrast service.ts:493/:500/:563/:603 which all caseVisible() first. Broken predicate: apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:1712-1716 `AND (ca.task_id IS NULL OR EXISTS(...))`. DB proof (crm2_dev :54329) that the scope leg is TRUE for an unrelated actor on a task_id-NULL row: `WITH synthetic AS (SELECT gen_random_uuid() id, gen_random_uuid() case_id, NULL::uuid task_id) SELECT (s.task_id IS NULL OR EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM case_tasks ct JOIN cases cs ON cs.id=ct.case_id WHERE ct.id=s.task_id AND (ct.assigned_to = ANY('{11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111}'::uuid[]) OR cs.created_by = ANY('{11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111}'::uuid[])))) FROM synthetic s;` -> scope_leg_satisfied = t. Case-level default upload: apps/web/src/features/cases/CaseDetailPage.tsx:1282 `?taskId=${target}` only when target set. PII flag: DB `SELECT code,pii_sensitive FROM verification_units WHERE kind='KYC_DOCUMENT'` -> pii_sensitive=t for all KYC units. Route gating: apps/api/src/modules/cases/routes.ts:92-93 (authorize CASE_VIEW only).

findings.title: KYC reference documents are stored under the generic OFFICE_REF kind with no required-document / mime-binding to the unit, so a wrong or empty document set cannot be detected
findings.detail: KYC documents persist via insertAttachment (repository.ts:1490-1512) which omits the kind column -> DB default 'OFFICE_REF' (case_attachments.kind default + chk {OFFICE_REF,FIELD_PHOTO}, DB-confirmed). There is no KYC_DOCUMENT-specific attachment kind, no linkage between the stored doc and the verification unit's required_attachments jsonb (which is '[]' for KYC units), and no count/type enforcement. Combined with the fact that completion (the BACKEND_USER/MANAGER/TEAM_LEADER side) can set verification_outcome=POSITIVE without the API checking that any document was actually attached for that KYC task, the integrity guarantee 'a POSITIVE KYC result is backed by the right uploaded document' is not enforced server-side. This is an integrity/assurance gap (a verifier could relay, and a completer could close POSITIVE, with the wrong document or none), not a confidentiality leak. The upload itself does sniff bytes by magic number, not the declared type (service.ts:600-601), so the mime is trustworthy -- it is the document-to-task binding and presence that is unenforced.
findings.evidence: insertAttachment omits kind: apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:1502-1508 (no kind col -> DB default). DB default+chk: `SELECT column_default FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name='case_attachments' AND column_name='kind'` -> 'OFFICE_REF'; chk constraint kind IN (OFFICE_REF, FIELD_PHOTO). required_attachments for KYC units = '[]' (per tracer + chk_vu_kyc_document forces no form/photos). Magic-number sniff (good): apps/api/src/modules/cases/service.ts:600-601 detectAttachment(bytes).

findings.title: bulkAssign re-points a LIVE ASSIGNED task in place with no revoke + no mandatory reason (ADR-0055 invariant breach, reachable from web Pipeline)
findings.detail: ADR-0055's core product rule is that a live ASSIGNED/IN_PROGRESS task leaves an agent ONLY via Revoke with a mandatory reason. The single-assign path enforces this (cases/service.ts:314-323 throws TASK_NOT_ASSIGNABLE unless status==='PENDING'), and the web case-detail UI removed in-place Reassign/Unassign per ADR-0055. But the bulk-assign path skips that guard: tasks/service.ts:266 treats both PENDING and ASSIGNED as assignable, then line 272 calls caseRepository.assignTask directly, whose UPDATE WHERE clause guards only the OCC version (version=$8), not status. Net effect: a live ASSIGNED task is silently re-pointed to a new agent (assigned_to changes, status stays ASSIGNED, a REASSIGNED history row is written) with NO revoke transition and NO mandatory reason -- the auditable 'why did this leave the agent' record that ADR-0055 exists to guarantee is never created. This is reachable from the live web UI: PipelinePage.tsx:278 BulkAssignAction sends selection.rows (PipelinePage.tsx:316-322) without filtering out ASSIGNED rows, so an operator who ticks live ASSIGNED tasks and clicks 'Assign...' re-points them in place. Per-agent commission/accountability and the revoke-reason audit trail are bypassed. This is the known follow-up (memory SHIP-1) and is CONFIRMED still live in HEAD.
findings.evidence: apps/api/src/modules/tasks/service.ts:266 (`row.status !== 'PENDING' && row.status !== 'ASSIGNED'` -> ASSIGNED falls through to assignable), :272 (`caseRepository.assignTask(...)`); apps/api/src/modules/cases/service.ts:314-323 (single-assign enforces status==='PENDING' else TASK_NOT_ASSIGNABLE); apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts assignTask UPDATE guards only `version=$8`, no status check; apps/web/src/features/pipeline/PipelinePage.tsx:316-322 (web bulk-assign POST sends selected rows, no PENDING-only filter); contrast the in-place-never-repoint promise documented at apps/web/src/features/cases/CaseDetailPage.tsx:727-730.

outcomes: all 9 revoke_reasons: NOT_MY_AREA, WRONG_PINCODE, ADDRESS_NOT_WORKING, NOT_WORKING, CUSTOMER_LEFT_AREA, LEFT_AREA, WRONG_ADDRESS, WRONG_INCOMPLETE_ADDRESS, OTHER
findings.title: Revoke reason is never canonicalized to the 9 revoke_reasons and no structured revocation record is kept -- v1 parity regression (v1 had revoke_reason_id + task_revocations)
findings.detail: v1 captured a structured revocation: TaskRevocationService.resolveReasonId() mapped the free-text reason to a canonical revoke_reason_id (case-insensitive label OR code match), and recordRevocation() wrote a dedicated task_revocations row carrying revoke_reason, revoke_reason_id, revoked_by_role, revoked_from_user_id, previous_status, and reassigned tracking -- a queryable, analyzable revoke audit. v2 drops ALL of this: revokeTaskInPlace (cases/repository.ts:1383-1387) writes only the raw reason string into the generic case_tasks.remark column; there is NO revoke_reason_id resolution at any layer (RevokeTaskSchema is z.string().trim().min(1) with no enum -- sdk/cases.ts:545-547; mobile RevokeSchema likewise), and a DB scan shows the ONLY revoke-related column in v2 is auth_refresh_tokens.revoked_at (session tokens, unrelated). The result is that case_tasks.remark is un-joinable to revoke_reasons.code -- DB-proven: of 4 REVOKED rows, is_code=false for all 4, only 2 even match a label. The business consequences: you cannot reliably report/filter 'how many tasks were revoked as NOT_MY_AREA vs WRONG_PINCODE', cannot distinguish who-role revoked (BACKEND vs MANAGER vs field self-revoke), and the revoke remark is overwritten on the next transition (e.g. reassign-after-revoke). The 9 canonical reasons exist (DB verified) but feed ONLY the mobile dropdown.
findings.evidence: v2: apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:1383-1387 (UPDATE case_tasks SET ... remark=$4 -- reason into generic remark); packages/sdk/src/cases.ts:545-547 (RevokeTaskSchema reason z.string, no enum); DB proof `SELECT remark, remark IN (SELECT code FROM revoke_reasons) is_code, remark IN (SELECT label...) is_label FROM case_tasks WHERE status='REVOKED'` -> is_code=f for all 4 rows, is_label=t for only 2 ('Not my area' x2), 'Wrong address - reassign needed' and 'E2E lifecycle...' match neither; `SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.columns WHERE column_name ILIKE '%revoke%'` -> only auth_refresh_tokens.revoked_at. v1: CRM-BACKEND/src/services/taskRevocationService.ts:68-88 (resolveReasonId), :90-119 (recordRevocation INSERT INTO task_revocations with revoke_reason_id, revoked_by_role, previous_status); CRM-BACKEND/src/controllers/verificationTasksController.ts:2190 (resolveReasonId call on revoke).

outcomes: all 9 revoke_reasons via the office path are free-text, not constrained
findings.title: Office/backend revoke uses a free-text box while field revoke uses the 9-reason dropdown -- divergent reason vocabularies into the same column
findings.detail: The two revoke actors write structurally different reason vocabularies into the identical case_tasks.remark column. The MOBILE field self-revoke presents the canonical 9 reasons as a Picker (TaskRevokeModal.tsx:157-159) hydrated from the synced revoke_reasons mirror. The WEB office/backend revoke -- which per ADR-0055 is the PRIMARY revoke actor (BACKEND_USER/MANAGER/TEAM_LEADER) -- presents a bare free-text Input (CaseDetailPage.tsx ReasonForm, lines 1148-1192, placeholder 'Why a revisit is needed', validity = reason.trim().length>0). Web never fetches the canonical 9 at all (grep for getRevokeReasons/revoke-reasons/RevokeReasonList in apps/web/src returns nothing; the reference interface comment even scopes it to 'the device's revoke-task dropdown', sdk/reference.ts:28). So the office can type literally anything ('Wrong address - reassign needed', 'E2E lifecycle: revoke+sync demo' -- both real DB rows), while the field is constrained to canonical labels. From a product standpoint the office revoke reasons are unstructured and un-analyzable, and there is no shared vocabulary across the two revoke surfaces even though they target the same workflow and the same audit purpose.
findings.evidence: Web free-text: apps/web/src/features/cases/CaseDetailPage.tsx:854-865 (Revoke opens ReasonForm), :1148-1192 (ReasonForm = plain <Input>, valid = reason.trim().length>0), :528-532 (POST {reason}). Mobile dropdown: CRM-MOBILE TaskRevokeModal.tsx:148-160 (Picker over the 9 hydrated reasons). Web never consumes the canonical list: grep `getRevokeReasons|revoke-reasons|RevokeReasonList` over apps/web/src returns empty; packages/sdk/src/reference.ts:28 comment 'the device's revoke-task dropdown'. DB: case_tasks.remark held free text 'Wrong address - reassign needed' / 'E2E lifecycle: revoke+sync demo' from the web path.

outcomes: all 9 revoke_reasons -- past revoke reasons on a re-revoked task lineage are not viewable in web
findings.title: Preserved revoke history is not surfaced in any web UI -- only the current REVOKED snapshot's remark is shown
findings.detail: The immutable audit_log DOES preserve every revoke event with its reason (5 case_task UPDATE rows with after_data {status:REVOKED, reason:...} confirmed in DB), so a multi-revoke lineage (revoke -> reassign-after-revoke -> revoke again) is technically recoverable. But NO web UI exposes it. The only task-level revoke surface in web is the inline remark rendered on the current REVOKED task row (CaseDetailPage.tsx:693-694: ' — {t.remark}'), which is the latest snapshot only and is overwritten by subsequent transitions. The VerdictHistory panel (CaseDetailPage.tsx:402-420) that one might expect to show revoke history actually queries /verdict-history, whose backing query filters entity_type='case' (case-finalize events), NOT case_task revoke events (repository.ts verdictHistory WHERE a.entity_type='case'). So an operator reviewing a case cannot see the history of revokes on a task lineage (who revoked, when, with what reason, how many times) -- a completeness gap versus the v1 task_revocations record that was queryable. The reason data is not lost (audit_log is append-only), but the workflow to view it is missing.
findings.evidence: audit_log preservation DB proof: `SELECT entity_type, action, after_data FROM audit_log WHERE entity_type='case_task' AND after_data::text ILIKE '%REVOKED%'` -> 5 rows each {reason, status:REVOKED}; audit_log is immutable (trigger trg_audit_log_immutable BEFORE DELETE OR UPDATE). Web surfaces only the snapshot: apps/web/src/features/cases/CaseDetailPage.tsx:693-694. verdict-history is case-finalize only: CaseDetailPage.tsx:404-405 (GET /verdict-history), apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts verdictHistory query `WHERE a.entity_type = 'case'` (repository.ts:~1091). No endpoint queries audit_log for entity_type='case_task' revoke events.

outcomes: 4 missing offline: ADDRESS_NOT_WORKING, CUSTOMER_LEFT_AREA, WRONG_ADDRESS, OTHER
findings.title: Mobile offline FALLBACK_REASONS lists only 5 of the 9 canonical revoke reasons (fresh-install offline revoke is missing 4 valid reasons)
findings.detail: When the local revoke_reasons mirror is empty (fresh install before first successful sync) and a field agent revokes offline, the picker falls back to a hardcoded list of only 5 entries: NOT_MY_AREA, WRONG_PINCODE, NOT_WORKING, LEFT_AREA, WRONG_INCOMPLETE_ADDRESS (TaskRevokeModal.tsx:39-45). The 4 canonical reasons ADDRESS_NOT_WORKING, CUSTOMER_LEFT_AREA, WRONG_ADDRESS, and OTHER are absent, so a field agent who must revoke before their first sync cannot pick the most accurate reason and is forced into a near-miss (e.g. NOT_WORKING vs the more precise ADDRESS_NOT_WORKING) -- a minor product-completeness gap. Because mobile submits the LABEL (Picker.Item value={r.label}, TaskRevokeModal.tsx:158) rather than the code, even the online canonical path stores a label, but the offline path additionally narrows the available business reasons. Impact is bounded (fresh-install + offline + must-revoke is rare) but it is a genuine vocabulary-completeness regression against the 9-reason master.
findings.evidence: CRM-MOBILE-NATIVE/src/components/tasks/TaskRevokeModal.tsx:39-45 (FALLBACK_REASONS = 5 entries: NOT_MY_AREA, WRONG_PINCODE, NOT_WORKING, LEFT_AREA, WRONG_INCOMPLETE_ADDRESS); DB `SELECT code FROM revoke_reasons` -> 9 rows; the 4 missing offline = ADDRESS_NOT_WORKING, CUSTOMER_LEFT_AREA, WRONG_ADDRESS, OTHER. Label-not-code submit: TaskRevokeModal.tsx:158 `value={r.label}`.

findings.title: Masked server-revoke on mobile: is_revoked=1 written unconditionally while status stays un-flipped -> draft/attachment PII not wiped AND revoked form remains editable
findings.detail: In SyncDownloadService.upsertTaskFromServer the local tasks row gets is_revoked/revoked_at/revoked_by_name/revoke_reason written UNCONDITIONALLY from task.* (the v2 payload), but the status column is set from mergedState.status produced by SyncConflictResolver. The resolver has two branches that override the server status with the fresher LOCAL status: (a) hasInFlightQueueItems -> early-return status=localStatus (true whenever a PENDING/IN_PROGRESS/retrying FORM_SUBMISSION or TASK_STATUS sits in sync_queue for the task -- i.e. the agent has unsynced work), and (b) the non-PENDING localHasFreshEdits branch -> status=localStatus, despite its comment claiming it accepts 'status revocations'. Net masked state: is_revoked=1 but status='IN_PROGRESS' (or ASSIGNED/SUBMITTED). Two concrete harms: (1) the B-148 justRevoked wipe of local attachments + form_submissions keys on mergedState.status==='REVOKED' && prev!=='REVOKED', so it does NOT fire -> the revoked task's local photo attachments and draft form_data survive on-device (PII retention + storage); (2) TaskListScreen.tsx tap-handler routes on status: a status='IN_PROGRESS' card opens VerificationForm (line 462-464), and the revoked-task no-op guard (line 472, status IN ASSIGNED/REVOKED -> return) is never reached -> the field agent can keep editing/submitting a task the office already revoked. The is_revoked banner shows (TaskDetailScreen keys on isRevoked||status) but the action gating is inconsistent across screens (TaskListScreen on status, TaskCard/TaskDetail on isRevoked). This is a field-level reshape defect: status and is_revoked are sourced from different merge logics in the same upsert and can disagree.
findings.evidence: Mobile write-side (unconditional is_revoked, status from merge): crm-mobile-native/src/sync/SyncDownloadService.ts:611 (status <- mergedState.status), :633-636 (is_revoked/revoked_at/revoked_by_name/revoke_reason <- task.* unconditional), :565-568 (ON CONFLICT sets is_revoked=excluded.is_revoked... unconditional). Wipe keyed on status: SyncDownloadService.ts:657-658 justRevoked = mergedState.status==='REVOKED' && previousStatus!=='REVOKED'. Resolver masking: crm-mobile-native/src/sync/SyncConflictResolver.ts:110-116 (hasQueuedChanges -> status=localStatus||status, early return) and :159-167 (localHasFreshEdits -> status=localStatus||status). hasInFlightQueueItems true on queued FORM_SUBMISSION/TASK_STATUS: SyncConflictResolver.ts:50-70. UI keying split: crm-mobile-native/src/screens/tasks/TaskListScreen.tsx:462-464 (status IN_PROGRESS -> open VerificationForm) vs :472-473 (status REVOKED -> no-op); banner uses OR: src/screens/tasks/TaskDetailScreen.tsx:371; schema has BOTH columns + composite index: crm-mobile-native/src/database/schema.ts:35 (status),57 (is_revoked),338 (idx on is_revoked,status,is_saved). Server side always emits status='REVOKED' + isRevoked together: packages/sdk/src/sync.ts:40,56-59 ; apps/api/src/modules/sync/service.ts:43-50 -- so the divergence is purely a mobile merge defect.

verificationTypes: all task types assignable via bulk-assign (FIELD_VISIT + OFFICE/KYC relay) -- the missing guard is status-level, independent of verification unit
findings.title: bulkAssign re-points a live ASSIGNED task in place, bypassing the ADR-0055 revoke-before-reassign guard (no mandatory reason)
findings.detail: ADR-0055 requires a live ASSIGNED/IN_PROGRESS task to leave an agent ONLY via Revoke with a mandatory reason. The single-assign service path enforces this (state.status!=='PENDING' -> 409 TASK_NOT_ASSIGNABLE). bulkAssign does NOT: it treats row.status==='ASSIGNED' as assignable and calls caseRepository.assignTask directly. assignTask's UPDATE WHERE clause guards only on version=$8 (optimistic concurrency), NOT on status -- so a live ASSIGNED task is silently re-pointed (assigned_to changed, status stays ASSIGNED, a REASSIGNED task_assignment_history row written) with NO revoke and NO reason captured. This breaches the invariant that every agent change carries an auditable revoke reason; the reassign is recorded but the WHY is lost. Asymmetry between the single and bulk paths is the smoking gun.
findings.evidence: Bulk path allows ASSIGNED + calls assignTask: apps/api/src/modules/tasks/service.ts:266 (else if row.status!=='PENDING' && row.status!=='ASSIGNED' -> NOT_ASSIGNABLE; i.e. ASSIGNED passes) and :272 (await caseRepository.assignTask(...)). assignTask UPDATE guards version only: apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:884 (WHERE case_tasks.id = p.id AND case_tasks.version = $8 -- no status predicate), writes REASSIGNED history at :910. Single-assign enforces PENDING-only (the guard bulk skips): apps/api/src/modules/cases/service.ts:320-323 (if (state.status !== 'PENDING') throw AppError.conflict('TASK_NOT_ASSIGNABLE') with ADR-0055 comment). Contrast with the revoke transition which DOES guard status: apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:1385 (WHERE ... status IN ('ASSIGNED','IN_PROGRESS')).

An agent revoking offline on a fresh device can only pick from a truncated, non-canonical vocabulary. The 5th entry also pairs code WRONG_INCOMPLETE_ADDRESS with a label drawn from the RevokeReason enum (not the DB master), reinforcing finding REVOKE-CTO-3's code/label drift.
