findings.title: Web office-revoke is a free-text box; mobile field-revoke is the 9-reason dropdown — same 'Reason:' label, two incompatible vocabularies into one column
findings.detail: The web office/backend Revoke UI presents a plain free-text <Input> with placeholder 'Why this task is revoked (required)' and validates only reason.trim().length>0 — it never fetches or offers the 9 canonical revoke_reasons that GET /api/v2/reference/revoke-reasons exposes. The mobile field-revoke UI (TaskRevokeModal) is a constrained Picker over those 9 reasons. Both write to the same case_tasks.remark column and both render under an identically-labeled 'Reason:' field (web: inline ' — {t.remark}'; mobile: 'Reason: {task.revokeReason}'). The result is label drift / vocabulary divergence for one conceptual field: an office user can revoke with arbitrary prose ('Wrong address - reassign needed', 'E2E lifecycle: revoke+sync demo') that visually sits in the same place a field user's canonical 'Not my area' chip would. From a UX standpoint the office user is given a different, unconstrained control for what the design treats as a controlled-vocabulary field, and the two reasons read inconsistently in the case history.
findings.evidence: WEB free-text: apps/web/src/features/cases/CaseDetailPage.tsx:854-866 (ReasonForm required, placeholder 'Why this task is revoked (required)') + ReasonForm body lines 1167-1192 (plain Input, valid=reason.trim().length>0); web never imports revoke-reasons (grep of CaseDetailPage shows no /reference/revoke-reasons usage). MOBILE dropdown: crm-mobile-native/src/components/tasks/TaskRevokeModal.tsx:148-160 (Picker over the 9). Canonical list exists & is mobile-only: apps/api/src/modules/reference/routes.ts:11 + reference/repository.ts:21-24. DB proof (crm2_dev): SELECT status,remark FROM case_tasks WHERE status='REVOKED' returned remark values 'Not my area','Not my area','Wrong address - reassign needed','E2E lifecycle: revoke+sync demo' — a mix of mobile labels and arbitrary office free text in the same column.

findings.title: Mobile offline fallback shows only 5 of 9 revoke reasons and mispairs one code with its label
findings.detail: On a fresh install before the first successful sync (the local revoke_reasons mirror is empty), TaskRevokeModal falls back to a hardcoded FALLBACK_REASONS of only 5 entries — NOT_MY_AREA, WRONG_PINCODE, NOT_WORKING, LEFT_AREA, WRONG_INCOMPLETE_ADDRESS. The 4 canonical reasons ADDRESS_NOT_WORKING, CUSTOMER_LEFT_AREA, WRONG_ADDRESS, and OTHER are absent, so an offline field agent literally cannot pick them — a UX coverage gap where the available options silently differ from the documented 9. Worse, the only 'wrong address' entry pairs code 'WRONG_INCOMPLETE_ADDRESS' with the label string from enum RevokeReason.WrongAddress='Wrong/incomplete address'; the DB has BOTH a WRONG_ADDRESS (label 'Wrong address') and a WRONG_INCOMPLETE_ADDRESS (label 'Wrong/incomplete address'), so the fallback collapses two distinct reasons into one and the agent has no way to express plain 'Wrong address' offline. Because the Picker submits the label anyway (see UX-REVOKE-3), the practical effect is a reduced, partially-mislabeled menu offline vs the full menu online.
findings.evidence: crm-mobile-native/src/components/tasks/TaskRevokeModal.tsx:39-45 (FALLBACK_REASONS, 5 entries) and lines 80-82 (fallback used when rows.length===0). Enum: crm-mobile-native/src/types/api.ts:4-10 (RevokeReason has only 5 members; WrongAddress='Wrong/incomplete address' on line 9). DB proof (crm2_dev): SELECT code,label FROM revoke_reasons returned 9 rows including the missing ADDRESS_NOT_WORKING, CUSTOMER_LEFT_AREA, WRONG_ADDRESS, OTHER, and a distinct WRONG_ADDRESS='Wrong address' separate from WRONG_INCOMPLETE_ADDRESS='Wrong/incomplete address'.

findings.title: Mobile revoke banner's 'Revoked on {date}' and revoker name are proxied from generic updated_at / updated_by, not a real revoke timestamp/actor
findings.detail: The mobile TaskDetailScreen and TaskCard render a revoke banner with 'Reason:', 'By: {revokedByName}', and 'Revoked on {revokedAt}'. But there is no dedicated revoked_at column or revoked_by column — the sync service emits revokedAt = iso(updatedAt) and revokedByName is derived from a JOIN on updated_by. Any later UPDATE to the REVOKED row that touches updated_at/updated_by (without re-revoking) would make the banner show a misleading revoke date and mis-attribute the revoker to whoever last edited the row. This is a render-fidelity risk for a user-facing timestamp/name pair: the labels assert 'Revoked on / By' but the underlying values are last-modified semantics. Today the values happen to be correct because revoke is the last write, but the display labels over-promise relative to the data.
findings.evidence: Sync mapping: apps/api/src/modules/sync/service.ts:46 (revokedAt: iso(r.updatedAt)) and line 48 (revokedByName from r.revisedByName, which sync/repository.ts:84 derives via reviser JOIN on ct.updated_by). Mobile render: crm-mobile-native/src/screens/tasks/TaskDetailScreen.tsx:399-420 ('Reason:', 'By:') and lines 421+ ('Revoked on'); TaskCard.tsx:112-113 (`Revoked on ${new Date(task.revokedAt)...}`). DB proof: information_schema.columns for case_tasks has only 'remark' for the reason and no revoked_at/revoked_by columns.

findings.title: No consolidated task-level revoke-history view on web — reason shows only as an inline remark suffix; repeated revokes of a lineage are not surfaced together
findings.detail: On the web case-detail, the revoke reason renders only as an inline muted suffix on the REVOKED task row (' — {t.remark}'). The dedicated history panel on the page is the case-finalize VerdictHistory (/verdict-history), which is NOT revoke history. So for a task lineage that is revoked, reassigned-after-revoke, and revoked again, the office user has no single panel showing who revoked, when, and why across the lineage — each revoke's reason is only visible as a transient suffix on a row that gets superseded by the replacement task. From a UX standpoint the audit story (who/when/why for each revoke) is fragmented across row remarks and the underlying audit log, with no first-class revoke-history surface to match the mandatory-reason workflow's intent.
findings.evidence: Inline-only render: apps/web/src/features/cases/CaseDetailPage.tsx:693-695 (status==='REVOKED' && t.remark -> ' — {t.remark}'). VerdictHistory is case-finalize, not revoke: CaseDetailPage.tsx:402-420 + comment line 261 ('before/after when a revisit re-opened the case'). Reassign-after-revoke records the NEW reason into task_assignment_history (repository.ts:1252-1266 audit after.reason=input.reason), which is a separate reason from the original revoke remark — the two are never shown together in one UI.

findings.title: bulkAssign re-points a live ASSIGNED task in place, bypassing the mandatory-revoke-reason UX entirely (state issue, outside pure UX lens)
findings.detail: Outside my UX lens but confirmed and worth recording for the track: bulkAssign treats row.status==='ASSIGNED' as assignable and calls caseRepository.assignTask, whose UPDATE WHERE guards only the OCC version (not status). A live ASSIGNED task is silently re-pointed to a new agent with status staying ASSIGNED and NO mandatory revoke reason ever collected — meaning the 'Reason:' control (the heart of the revoke UX) is skipped, and the case history gets a REASSIGNED row with no revoke reason. The single-assign path correctly enforces PENDING-only (cases/service.ts), so this is an asymmetry where the bulk flow can produce a reassignment with no captured reason, which would render as an unexplained agent change in the UI. Flagged INFO since the defect is correctness/state, not a render/label issue.
findings.evidence: apps/api/src/modules/tasks/service.ts:261-282 (status 'PENDING' OR 'ASSIGNED' treated assignable ~line 266; calls caseRepository.assignTask ~line 272). assignTask UPDATE in apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts guards only version (OCC), not status. Single-assign guard for contrast: apps/api/src/modules/cases/service.ts:314-323 (state.status==='PENDING' else 409 TASK_NOT_ASSIGNABLE).

outcomes: outcome-independent: any ASSIGNED/IN_PROGRESS FIELD_VISIT task with local drafts/attachments at revoke time
findings.title: Down-sync masks server REVOKED status when local has queued/fresher edits -> revoked-task PII (photos, GPS, form drafts) is NOT wiped from the device
findings.detail: When a server-side (web/office) revoke arrives via GET /sync/download while the device has in-flight queue items OR a fresher local updatedAt for that task, SyncConflictResolver.resolveTaskState short-circuits and sets status = localStatus (e.g. IN_PROGRESS), masking the REVOKED status. mergedState.status is then used by SyncDownloadService.upsertTaskFromServer for BOTH the row write AND the justRevoked PII-wipe gate (mergedState.status==='REVOKED' && previousStatus!=='REVOKED'), so the wipe is SKIPPED. Meanwhile is_revoked=1, revoked_at, revoke_reason are written UNCONDITIONALLY from task.* on the same row. Net inconsistent state: is_revoked=1 + status=IN_PROGRESS, and the task's local attachments (geotagged field photos + selfie) and form_submissions drafts (customer name/phone/address/answers = PII) are retained on a device that no longer owns the task. The list projection hides the row (is_revoked=1 filter) so the lingering PII is invisible to the agent but still resident in op-sqlite/SQLCipher until the 45-day retention sweep eventually catches it, and only if no pending sync work remains. The masking branch is reachable because hasInFlightQueueItems returns true for any PENDING/IN_PROGRESS/retrying-FAILED queue row (a realistic state when a revoke lands mid-edit) and resolveTaskState returns BEFORE applying the server status.
findings.evidence: Masking (early return, status=localStatus): crm-mobile-native/src/sync/SyncConflictResolver.ts:110-116 (hasQueuedChanges branch) and :159-167 (localHasFreshEdits branch). hasInFlightQueueItems true for PENDING/IN_PROGRESS/retrying-FAILED: SyncConflictResolver.ts:59-70. Unconditional is_revoked/revoke_reason write from task.*: crm-mobile-native/src/sync/SyncDownloadService.ts:633-636. PII-wipe gated on the MASKED mergedState.status: SyncDownloadService.ts:656-682 (DELETE FROM attachments / form_submissions). Retention only catches it later and only when SYNCED w/ no pending work: crm-mobile-native/src/repositories/DataCleanupRepository.ts:4-30,39-71. The device self-revoke path DOES wipe correctly (TaskRepository.ts:248-267), confirming the wipe is intended on revoke; the down-sync path is the gap.

outcomes: outcome-independent: any ASSIGNED task selected in bulk-assign
findings.title: bulkAssign re-points a LIVE ASSIGNED task in place with no revoke + no mandatory reason (ADR-0055 invariant bypass; reason=NULL audit gap)
findings.detail: ADR-0055 mandates a live ASSIGNED/IN_PROGRESS task leaves an agent ONLY via Revoke with a mandatory reason. The single-assign service enforces state.status==='PENDING' (else 409 TASK_NOT_ASSIGNABLE), but tasks/service.ts bulkAssign treats row.status==='ASSIGNED' as assignable and calls caseRepository.assignTask directly. assignTask's UPDATE WHERE guards ONLY the OCC version (case_tasks.version = $8), no status guard, so a live ASSIGNED task is silently re-pointed (assigned_to changed, status stays ASSIGNED) with no revoke and no mandatory reason. The displaced agent's removal is recorded only as a REASSIGNED row in task_assignment_history with previous_assigned_to set but NO reason (the INSERT has no reason column), so there is NO documented reason for pulling the task, defeating the revoke-reason audit trail for the bulk path. The down-sync purge to the displaced agent still works (sourced from task_assignment_history.previous_assigned_to, which assignTask writes), so the old agent does drop the task, but the integrity/audit invariant is breached. The path is scope-checked (tasksForAssignment(ids, scope)) so it is NOT a cross-scope IDOR, but it IS reachable from the web Pipeline page under CASE_ASSIGN.
findings.evidence: bulkAssign allows ASSIGNED + calls assignTask: apps/api/src/modules/tasks/service.ts:266 (row.status !== 'PENDING' && row.status !== 'ASSIGNED') and :272. assignTask UPDATE has NO status guard, only version: apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:874-896 (WHERE case_tasks.id = p.id AND case_tasks.version = $8). REASSIGNED history row written with NO reason column: repository.ts:902-918. Single-assign guard that bulk skips: apps/api/src/modules/cases/service.ts:314-323. Web reachability: apps/web/src/features/pipeline/PipelinePage.tsx:316 (POST /bulk-assign); route guard apps/api/src/modules/tasks/routes.ts:15 authorize(CASE_ASSIGN).

outcomes: outcome-independent: applies to every revoke regardless of verification type/outcome
findings.title: Revoke reason is free-text end-to-end (no enum validation) and stored in generic case_tasks.remark, un-joinable to the 9 canonical revoke_reasons.code
findings.detail: The revoke reason is the primary audit/tamper artifact recording WHY a live task was pulled from an agent, but it is never validated against the 9 canonical revoke_reasons at any layer. Web RevokeTaskSchema (sdk cases.ts) and the device verification-tasks RevokeSchema are both z.string().trim().min(1).max(N): pure free text, no enum. Both web and mobile revoke funnel into cases/repository.ts revokeTaskInPlace, which writes the raw reason into case_tasks.remark (no dedicated revoke_reason column). Web sends arbitrary free text; mobile submits the LABEL (not the code). DB proof: the 4 REVOKED rows in crm2_dev hold 'Not my area' (mobile label), 'Wrong address - reassign needed' and 'E2E lifecycle: revoke+sync demo' (arbitrary web/test free text); none store a canonical code, so case_tasks.remark cannot be joined back to revoke_reasons.code for reporting/MIS/tamper-audit. Office (free text) and field (label) use divergent reason vocabularies into the same column. MEDIUM not HIGH because there is no cross-user/PII leakage; this is an audit-integrity / data-quality gap on a security-relevant field.
findings.evidence: No enum, web schema: packages/sdk/src/cases.ts:545-547 (reason z.string().trim().min(1).max(MAX_REMARK)). No enum, device schema: apps/api/src/modules/verification-tasks/service.ts:28-29 (reason z.string().trim().min(1).max(MAX_REASON=2000)). Stored into remark, no dedicated column: apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:1383-1387. DB proof (crm2_dev 2026-06-24): SELECT status,remark FROM case_tasks WHERE status='REVOKED' returned 'Not my area','Not my area','Wrong address - reassign needed','E2E lifecycle: revoke+sync demo'; none equal any revoke_reasons.code. Canonical set: SELECT code,label,is_active FROM revoke_reasons = 9 active rows (NOT_MY_AREA, WRONG_PINCODE, ADDRESS_NOT_WORKING, NOT_WORKING, CUSTOMER_LEFT_AREA, LEFT_AREA, WRONG_ADDRESS, WRONG_INCOMPLETE_ADDRESS, OTHER).

findings.title: revokedByName / revokedAt are proxied from updated_by / updated_at (not dedicated revoke columns) -> a later non-revoke UPDATE mis-attributes the revoker and revoke time
findings.detail: The down-sync exposes who/when a task was revoked via revokedByName and revokedAt, but neither is sourced from a dedicated column. revokedByName comes from a JOIN on ct.updated_by (the LAST writer of the row, not specifically the revoker) and revokedAt is proxied from ct.updated_at. revokeTaskInPlace keeps assigned_to and sets updated_by=actor/updated_at=now() at revoke time, so immediately after revoke these are correct, BUT any subsequent UPDATE to a REVOKED row that bumps updated_by/updated_at (e.g. a recomputeCaseStatus-driven touch, an audit/backfill, or a re-revoke by a different actor) would silently re-attribute the revoke to the wrong user and time on the next down-sync, corrupting the revoke audit shown on the field device. No dedicated revoked_by / revoked_at column exists on case_tasks to anchor this. LOW: latent mis-attribution on an audit-display field, not a live exposure; steady-state revokes display correctly.
findings.evidence: Proxy source in sync SELECT: apps/api/src/modules/sync/repository.ts:70,84 (reviser.name AS revised_by_name, LEFT JOIN users reviser ON reviser.id = ct.updated_by). revokedAt proxied from updatedAt: apps/api/src/modules/sync/service.ts:43-50 (revokedAt: iso(r.updatedAt), revokedByName: r.revisedByName, emitted only when status==='REVOKED'). No dedicated revoke columns; revokeTaskInPlace writes only remark/updated_by/updated_at: apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:1383-1387.

findings.title: Mobile offline FALLBACK_REASONS exposes only 5 of 9 revoke reasons and mispairs one code/label
findings.detail: On a fresh install before the first successful sync of the revoke_reasons mirror, the mobile revoke picker falls back to a hardcoded list of only 5 of the 9 canonical reasons (NOT_MY_AREA, WRONG_PINCODE, NOT_WORKING, LEFT_AREA, WRONG_INCOMPLETE_ADDRESS), omitting ADDRESS_NOT_WORKING, CUSTOMER_LEFT_AREA, WRONG_ADDRESS, OTHER. It also pairs code 'WRONG_INCOMPLETE_ADDRESS' with the RevokeReason.WrongAddress enum value 'Wrong/incomplete address', while the DB defines BOTH a distinct WRONG_ADDRESS ('Wrong address') and WRONG_INCOMPLETE_ADDRESS ('Wrong/incomplete address'). Because the picker submits the LABEL (free text stored in remark per SEC-REVOKE-3), this is a reason-vocabulary completeness/quality gap, not an injection or exposure, hence LOW. It compounds SEC-REVOKE-3 (the displaced-agent removal reason can be wrong or absent in the offline window) but is meaningless to canonical reporting because no code is stored anyway.
findings.evidence: FALLBACK_REASONS (5 entries, code/label mispair): crm-mobile-native/src/components/tasks/TaskRevokeModal.tsx:39-45. Enum source: crm-mobile-native/src/types/api.ts:4-9 (RevokeReason.WrongAddress = 'Wrong/incomplete address'; only 5 enum members). Canonical 9 from DB (crm2_dev) includes distinct WRONG_ADDRESS='Wrong address' AND WRONG_INCOMPLETE_ADDRESS='Wrong/incomplete address', plus ADDRESS_NOT_WORKING, CUSTOMER_LEFT_AREA, OTHER not in the fallback.

findings.title: Server-driven outcome/label sync is permanently inert: mobile keys on short verification codes (RV/PAV) but the reference endpoint and DB emit long codes (RESIDENCE/PROPERTY_APF), so every synced row is dropped
findings.detail: The point of /reference/verification-type-outcomes + the local verification_type_outcomes mirror (F2.7.1) is to let admins edit the per-type outcome SET and DISPLAY LABELS centrally and have them reach every field device on the next sync. That feature is dead. SyncDownloadService.refreshVerificationTypeOutcomes() fetches the rows (long codes), calls VerificationTypeOutcomesRepository.replaceAll() (stores them correctly), then hands them to setOutcomesFromSync(). setOutcomesFromSync looks each row up in FORM_TYPE_KEY_BY_VTYPE_CODE which contains ONLY short codes {RV,RC,OV,EV,BV,NV,DV,PIV,PAV}; the server's verificationTypeCode is the long unit code {RESIDENCE,OFFICE,PROPERTY_APF,...}. So FORM_TYPE_KEY_BY_VTYPE_CODE[r.verificationTypeCode] is always undefined and every row hits `if (!key) continue` and is discarded. syncedOutcomesByFormType + syncedLabelByOutcome stay empty, so getAllowedOutcomes() and getOutcomeLabel() always fall through to the embedded LEGACY_OUTCOMES_BY_FORM_TYPE / hardcoded label maps. There is no boot-time rehydration either: loadOutcomesCacheFromDb is referenced only in stale comments (lines 149,176) and does not exist as a function, and setOutcomesFromSync is the ONLY caller. The contradiction is local and obvious: the SIBLING map BACKEND_FORM_TYPE_TO_KEY (formTypeKey.ts:36-55) used to pick the actual form template already keys on the LONG codes correctly (RESIDENCE->residence, PROPERTY_APF->property-apf) from the same down-sync verificationUnit.code, so the form renders but the outcome list/labels for it never honor admin config. Product impact: (1) an admin who renames a label (e.g. 'No Such Person' -> 'Person Absent', the exact example in the code comments) or adds/removes an outcome sees ZERO effect on devices; (2) the embedded PROPERTY_INDIVIDUAL fallback order (NSP,ENTRY_RESTRICTED,POSITIVE,UNTRACEABLE) is frozen and diverges from the DB sort_order (POSITIVE,NSP,ENTRY_RESTRICTED,UNTRACEABLE) and can never be corrected via admin. It works only because the seed sets happen to match the embedded fallback sets. Suggested fix: change FORM_TYPE_KEY_BY_VTYPE_CODE to the long codes (or reuse BACKEND_FORM_TYPE_TO_KEY), which is the minimal surgical change.
findings.evidence: BROKEN map (short codes): /Users/mayurkulkarni/Downloads/CRM-APP-MONOREPO-PROD/crm-mobile-native/src/screens/forms/LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:154-164 (RV/RC/OV/EV/BV/NV/DV/PIV/PAV) + drop site lines 184-185 `const key = FORM_TYPE_KEY_BY_VTYPE_CODE[r.verificationTypeCode]; if (!key) continue;`. CORRECT sibling map (long codes): src/utils/formTypeKey.ts:36-55 (RESIDENCE->residence, PROPERTY_APF->property-apf). Server emits LONG codes: crm2 apps/api/src/modules/reference/repository.ts:12 selects verification_type_code from verification_unit_outcomes; apps/api/src/modules/reference/__tests__/reference.api.test.ts:45,56 assert verificationTypeCode==='RESIDENCE' and ==='PROPERTY_APF'; SDK packages/sdk/src/reference.ts:14 documents verificationTypeCode '= sync DTO verificationUnit.code'. DB proof (crm2_dev:54329): SELECT verification_type_code FROM verification_unit_outcomes -> RESIDENCE/OFFICE/PROPERTY_APF/... (43 rows, all long form); SELECT code FROM verification_units WHERE kind='FIELD_VISIT' -> RESIDENCE..PROPERTY_INDIVIDUAL (long). Single caller: src/sync/SyncDownloadService.ts:249-252.

findings.title: verificationOutcome name collision across the boundary: down-sync top-level field carries the office KYC result but feeds the field form's outcome prefill; benign only because the column is null during field work
findings.detail: The down-sync task field verificationOutcome (toMobileTask, from ct.verification_outcome) is the OFFICE KYC result vocabulary (Positive/Negative/Refer/Fraud, Title case, null until office-complete per ADR-0032 D1). The FIELD outcome vocabulary (POSITIVE/SHIFTED/NSP/ENTRY_RESTRICTED/UNTRACEABLE, UPPERCASE) is a different value set that rides separately inside formData[<slug>].verificationOutcome. Mobile VerificationFormScreen prefills the field outcome dropdown from the top-level task.verificationOutcome via coerceLegacyOutcomeForFormType. This is correct in the normal field workflow ONLY because, for a task an agent is actively filling, ct.verification_outcome is still NULL (office writes it after the field submits), so the prefill guard `!selectedOutcome && taskVerificationOutcome` never fires. The fragility: if this field ever became non-null while a field task is re-opened on device (a completed/relay task re-entered), normalizeOutcome maps KYC 'Negative'/'Refer'/'Fraud' to the field code 'POSITIVE' (default fall-through), silently prefilling the WRONG field outcome — a business-meaning error, not a crash. Two vocabularies sharing one field name across the sync boundary is the root smell; recommend an explicit guard that ignores the top-level office result for field-form prefill, or disambiguation of the field name.
findings.evidence: crm2 apps/api/src/modules/sync/service.ts:38 maps r.verificationOutcome (=ct.verification_outcome, KycResult) to the task field; SDK packages/sdk/src/cases.ts:245 types CaseTaskView.verificationOutcome as KycResult|null. Mobile reads it as the FIELD prefill: /Users/mayurkulkarni/Downloads/CRM-APP-MONOREPO-PROD/crm-mobile-native/src/screens/forms/VerificationFormScreen.tsx:156 (taskVerificationOutcome = task.verificationOutcome) -> 262-283 (coerceLegacyOutcomeForFormType -> setSelectedOutcome). Coercion fall-through to 'POSITIVE' for unknown values: src/screens/forms/LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:27-63. DB proof: 0 rows currently carry a non-null case_tasks.verification_outcome on crm2_dev, so the bad-prefill path is unexercised on live data today.

findings.title: Server-driven outcome sync is permanently inert for ALL 9 field types — long-vs-short verification-type code key mismatch drops every synced row
findings.detail: The reference master /reference/verification-type-outcomes emits verificationTypeCode = the LONG verification unit code (RESIDENCE, OFFICE, PROPERTY_APF, ...), which the SDK explicitly documents as '= sync DTO verificationUnit.code' and which is DB-verified (verification_units.code and verification_unit_outcomes.verification_type_code are both long codes; query returned RESIDENCE/OFFICE/PROPERTY_APF/.../PROPERTY_INDIVIDUAL). But the mobile hydration setOutcomesFromSync looks each row up in FORM_TYPE_KEY_BY_VTYPE_CODE, a map keyed ONLY on SHORT codes (RV/RC/OV/EV/BV/NV/DV/PIV/PAV). Every synced row therefore hits `if (!key) continue` and is silently dropped; syncedOutcomesByFormType stays empty and getAllowedOutcomes always returns the embedded LEGACY_OUTCOMES_BY_FORM_TYPE. Net effect: admin edits to outcome sets/labels (e.g. renaming 'No Such Person' or activating/deactivating an outcome) NEVER reach the device — the form outcome dropdown is frozen to the compiled-in defaults. This is unambiguously a bug, not an intentional short-code scheme: the app's canonical resolver src/utils/formTypeKey.ts:37-40 DOES map the long codes correctly (RESIDENCE->'residence' etc.) and is used elsewhere (FormUploader). Only this one outcome-sync map is short-code-only. Bounded impact today because the embedded fallback happens to match the current DB catalog, so the dropdown is correct — but the entire server-driven outcome/label override path (F2.7.1) is dead, and any future admin catalog change diverges device-from-server with no signal.
findings.evidence: DB (crm2_dev:54329): SELECT DISTINCT verification_type_code FROM verification_unit_outcomes -> RESIDENCE,OFFICE,PROPERTY_APF,... (long); SELECT id,code FROM verification_units WHERE kind='FIELD_VISIT' -> all long codes. API source: apps/api/src/modules/reference/repository.ts:12-16 selects verification_type_code verbatim; SDK packages/sdk/src/reference.ts:13-14 ('= sync DTO verificationUnit.code'). Sync DTO code source: apps/api/src/modules/sync/repository.ts:66 (vu.code AS unit_code) -> service.ts:31 (verificationUnit.code). Mobile defect: src/screens/forms/LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:154-164 (FORM_TYPE_KEY_BY_VTYPE_CODE has only RV/RC/OV/EV/BV/NV/DV/PIV/PAV) used at line 184 (setOutcomesFromSync: `const key = FORM_TYPE_KEY_BY_VTYPE_CODE[r.verificationTypeCode]; if (!key) continue;`). Correct long-code map that should be reused: src/utils/formTypeKey.ts:37-40. Hydration call site: src/sync/SyncDownloadService.ts:252-259.

findings.title: verificationOutcome naming collision can leak an OFFICE KYC result into the FIELD outcome on a form-upload retry
findings.detail: `verificationOutcome` carries two different vocabularies across the boundary under one name. The down-sync task field verificationOutcome maps from case_tasks.verification_outcome — the OFFICE KYC result (KycResult: Positive/Negative/Refer/Fraud, Title-case; null until office-complete) — and is written into the device tasks.verification_outcome column at upsert. The FIELD outcome (POSITIVE/SHIFTED/NSP/ENTRY_RESTRICTED/UNTRACEABLE, UPPERCASE) is a separate value the agent picks at submit, carried as payload.verificationOutcome. The FormUploader re-hydration reads the LOCAL tasks.verification_outcome column and, if payload.verificationOutcome is empty, copies it into payload.verificationOutcome before POSTing the field form. Today this is latent — DB query shows zero field tasks carry a non-null verification_outcome (ADR-0032 D1: the device field path never writes it, only office writes it at COMPLETE), so freshOutcome is null and the branch is a no-op. The hazard: if an OFFICE completes a SUBMITTED field task and writes a KYC result, a re-sync stamps that Title-case KYC value into the device's tasks.verification_outcome; a queued/retried form upload that lacks an explicit outcome would then submit 'Positive'/'Negative' (a KYC vocabulary value) as the FIELD verificationOutcome — a vocabulary/casing mismatch the field-form endpoint does not expect. Mitigated in practice because the form is normally submitted before office-complete and selectedOutcome populates the payload, but the cross-vocabulary fallback is unsound.
findings.evidence: Down-sync write (KYC value into local col): src/sync/SyncDownloadService.ts:618 (task.verificationOutcome -> verification_outcome column) sourced from apps/api/src/modules/sync/service.ts:38 + repository.ts:61 (ct.verification_outcome) typed KycResult|null at packages/sdk/src/cases.ts:245. Field-outcome up-sync fallback that reads the same local col: src/sync/uploaders/FormUploader.ts:151-157 (SELECT verification_outcome FROM tasks) and 193-196 (`if (freshOutcome && !payload.verificationOutcome) payload.verificationOutcome = freshOutcome`). Normal field-outcome source (correct, UPPERCASE): src/services/forms/FormSubmissionService.ts:81 (verificationOutcome: selectedOutcome). DB proof: SELECT count(*) field_tasks_with_outcome -> 0; SELECT DISTINCT verification_outcome -> 0 rows.

findings.title: Falsy-zero coercion drops a literal 0 latitude/longitude during down-sync (geographically immaterial for India)
findings.detail: The API preserves a 0 coordinate (toMobileTask guards with `r.latitude != null ? Number(r.latitude)`), but the mobile upsert binds `task.latitude || null`, so a literal 0 lat or 0 lng is coerced to NULL in the local tasks table. For ACS field coordinates (India, ~8-37N / 68-97E) a true 0 lat/lng is in the Atlantic Ocean and never a valid field reading, and the DB confirms zero such rows today, so this has no live impact — flagged only for completeness as a contract asymmetry (API keeps 0, device discards it). If coordinates from another source could legitimately be 0 it would silently drop the value.
findings.evidence: API preserves 0: apps/api/src/modules/sync/service.ts:27-28 (`...(r.latitude != null ? { latitude: Number(r.latitude) } : {})`). Mobile drops 0: src/sync/SyncDownloadService.ts:609-610 (`task.latitude || null`, `task.longitude || null`). DB proof: SELECT count(*) FILTER (WHERE latitude=0 OR longitude=0) -> 0.

outcomes: all 9 FIELD_VISIT types (label/order); specifically PROPERTY_APF NEGATIVE unselectable on device; PROPERTY_INDIVIDUAL order/labels drifted
findings.title: Server-driven outcome catalog is inert on ALL 9 field types (long-vs-short code key mismatch) — admin-edited outcome labels, order, and APF NEGATIVE never reach the mobile dropdown
findings.detail: The mobile field-form outcome dropdown is supposed to reflect the admin-managed verification_unit_outcomes catalog (codes + display_label + sort_order), synced each cycle via /reference/verification-type-outcomes and applied by setOutcomesFromSync(). That sync is completely inert. The reference endpoint emits verification_type_code as the LONG unit code (DB-verified: RESIDENCE, OFFICE, PROPERTY_APF, PROPERTY_INDIVIDUAL, ...; reference repository SELECTs verification_type_code from verification_unit_outcomes, and SDK reference.ts:13 documents verificationTypeCode = sync DTO verificationUnit.code = the long code). But mobile setOutcomesFromSync resolves each row's code through FORM_TYPE_KEY_BY_VTYPE_CODE, which contains ONLY short codes (RV/RC/OV/EV/BV/NV/DV/PIV/PAV — LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:154-164). Every synced row therefore yields key=undefined and is skipped (`if (!key) continue`, line 185), leaving syncedOutcomesByFormType and syncedLabelByOutcome empty. getAllowedOutcomes (221-225) and getOutcomeLabel (83-85) then permanently fall back to the embedded LEGACY_OUTCOMES_BY_FORM_TYPE + hardcoded label maps. UX consequences the field agent actually sees: (1) DROPDOWN ORDER drift — DB PROPERTY_INDIVIDUAL order is POSITIVE,NSP,ENTRY_RESTRICTED,UNTRACEABLE (sort 1-4) but the device fallback array is ['NSP','ENTRY_RESTRICTED','POSITIVE','UNTRACEABLE'] (line 139-144), so NSP shows first and POSITIVE third; APF DB order POSITIVE,NEGATIVE,ERT,UNTRACEABLE vs device ['UNTRACEABLE','ENTRY_RESTRICTED','POSITIVE'] (line 145). (2) LABEL drift — DB display_label 'No Such Person' (PIV NSP) vs device dropdown label 'NSP & NSP Door Locked' (getOutcomeLabel line 104); any admin edit to a display_label is silently ignored on the device. (3) SELECTABLE-SET drift — DB lists PROPERTY_APF NEGATIVE (sort 2, label 'Negative') as a selectable outcome but the device APF dropdown can never include it. The mechanism is the same code-key bug for all 9 types, so the entire admin outcome-configuration surface is decorative for the field app.
findings.evidence: API/DB: reference repository.ts:10-18 SELECTs verification_type_code FROM verification_unit_outcomes; DB query `SELECT DISTINCT verification_type_code FROM verification_unit_outcomes` returns long codes RESIDENCE/OFFICE/PROPERTY_APF/PROPERTY_INDIVIDUAL/...; verification_units.code = long codes (RESIDENCE, PROPERTY_APF ...). SDK packages/sdk/src/reference.ts:13-14 documents verificationTypeCode = sync verificationUnit.code. MOBILE: SyncDownloadService.ts:252-258 passes r.verificationTypeCode (long) straight into setOutcomesFromSync; LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:154-164 FORM_TYPE_KEY_BY_VTYPE_CODE has only short codes; setOutcomesFromSync line 184-185 `const key = FORM_TYPE_KEY_BY_VTYPE_CODE[r.verificationTypeCode]; if (!key) continue;` drops every long-code row; fallback LEGACY_OUTCOMES_BY_FORM_TYPE LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:130-146 (PIV order NSP-first, APF omits NEGATIVE). DB order proof: verification_unit_outcomes PROPERTY_INDIVIDUAL=POSITIVE(1),NSP(2),ENTRY_RESTRICTED(3),UNTRACEABLE(4); PROPERTY_APF=POSITIVE(1),NEGATIVE(2),ENTRY_RESTRICTED(3),UNTRACEABLE(4).

outcomes: PROPERTY_INDIVIDUAL POSITIVE and NSP (the Open/Closed door states); ENTRY_RESTRICTED/UNTRACEABLE don't capture flatStatus so unaffected
findings.title: property-individual report: the door field `flatStatus` is missing from sectionMap → renders in the trailing 'Additional Details' dump instead of grouped under 'Verification Outcome & Status'
findings.detail: The property-individual mobile form captures TWO status fields: buildingStatus (Building Status select) AND flatStatus (Flat Status = the door Open/Closed field that drives the outcome label, e.g. 'Positive & Door Locked' — canonicalize.ts deriveLabel reads flatStatus||premisesStatus). The web report's per-type grouping (SECTION_MAP['property-individual']) places buildingStatus in the lead 'Verification Outcome & Status' section (sectionMap.ts:399) but never lists flatStatus anywhere in the property-individual definition (grep confirms flatStatus appears in ZERO sectionMap entries). buildSections therefore treats flatStatus as an unplaced submitted key and dumps it into the trailing 'Additional Details' catch-all (sections.ts:130-140) with an auto-derived label 'Flat Status'. So on the web report the door-status that determines the whole outcome is visually divorced from Building Status — it appears at the very bottom in the ungrouped overflow section rather than at the top beside its peer. Not data loss (the never-lose-a-field catch-all saves it and toLabel happens to produce a readable 'Flat Status'), but a grouping/order defect: the most outcome-relevant field is mis-placed and de-prioritised relative to every other type, whose primary door/status field (houseStatus/officeStatus/businessStatus/resiCumOfficeStatus) IS in its 'Verification Outcome & Status' section.
findings.evidence: Mobile emits both: LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:7087 (buildingStatus, 'Building Status') and :7093 (flatStatus, 'Flat Status', door Open/Closed). Report uses flatStatus to derive the label: canonicalize.ts:31 STATUS_FIELD['property-individual']='flatStatus' and :64-65 `str(formData['flatStatus']) || str(formData['premisesStatus'])`. sectionMap.ts property-individual 'Verification Outcome & Status' (394-402) lists only addressRating, buildingStatus, finalStatus — NO flatStatus; `grep flatStatus apps/api/src/modules/fieldReports/sectionMap.ts` = 0 hits. Catch-all path sections.ts:133-140 collects unplaced keys into 'Additional Details'. Web renders sections in order: FieldReportBody CaseDetailPage.tsx:1847-1858.

outcomes: all 9 field types if an office NEGATIVE/REFER/FRAUD result were ever present on an editable field task — currently 0 such rows
findings.title: Mobile pre-selects the form outcome from task.verificationOutcome (the OFFICE KYC result, not a field code) → a NEGATIVE office result silently coerces the field form to POSITIVE
findings.detail: VerificationFormScreen pre-selects the outcome dropdown from task.verificationOutcome (taskVerificationOutcome, line 156; effect lines 257-283 calls coerceLegacyOutcomeForFormType(taskFormTypeKey, taskVerificationOutcome)). But on the v2-native down-sync contract task.verificationOutcome is the OFFICE KYC result (KycResult: POSITIVE/NEGATIVE/REFER/FRAUD, null until office-complete; SDK cases.ts:245 types it KycResult|null) — a different vocabulary from the FIELD outcome codes the form expects. normalizeOutcome (LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:27-64) does not recognise 'NEGATIVE'/'REFER'/'FRAUD' as field codes: 'NEGATIVE' falls through every branch and returns the default 'POSITIVE'. So if a task ever carries a non-null office KYC result of NEGATIVE/REFER/FRAUD and the agent re-opens the form, the outcome silently defaults to POSITIVE with only a Logger.warn — a wrong, label-drifted pre-selection. Currently INERT: DB query `SELECT count(*) FROM case_tasks WHERE verification_outcome IS NOT NULL` = 0, and for a normal ASSIGNED/IN_PROGRESS field task verification_outcome is NULL (ADR-0032 D1), so toMobileTask omits the field (service.ts:38) and the pre-select effect no-ops. Latent fragility, not a live defect, because the field-then-office-then-field-reopen path is not a live flow today.
findings.evidence: Mobile: VerificationFormScreen.tsx:156 taskVerificationOutcome = task?.verificationOutcome; :257-283 pre-select via coerceLegacyOutcomeForFormType; normalizeOutcome LegacyFormTemplateBuilders.ts:27-64 returns 'POSITIVE' for unrecognised 'NEGATIVE'. Contract: down-sync task.verificationOutcome = ct.verification_outcome (sync/repository.ts:61), the office KYC result; SDK cases.ts:245 CaseTaskView.verificationOutcome: KycResult|null; KYC_RESULT_LABELS only POSITIVE/NEGATIVE/REFER/FRAUD (cases.ts:112-117). DB proof: case_tasks with non-null verification_outcome = 0.

outcomes: any type, only for a task/photo with an exact-0 coordinate (not observed in real data)
findings.title: Falsy-zero coercion of latitude/longitude on upsert → a literal 0 coordinate is dropped to null, losing the GPS pin and static-map inset
findings.detail: In the mobile down-sync upsert the task coords are bound as `task.latitude || null` / `task.longitude || null` (SyncDownloadService.ts:609-610), and the API toMobileTask only emits them when `!= null` (sync/service.ts:27-28). A genuine coordinate of exactly 0 (lat 0 or lng 0) is falsy and would be coerced to null, so on the web report the photo/task would lose its coordinate → PhotoOverlay/StaticMapInset (CaseDetailPage.tsx:1944-1981) renders the 'No GPS' placeholder instead of the map thumbnail and coords link. Practically impossible for real ACS field work (India coords are ~8-37N / 68-97E, never 0), so this is an INFO-level robustness note, not a live defect. Flagged for completeness only.
findings.evidence: Mobile bind SyncDownloadService.ts:609-610 `task.latitude || null, task.longitude || null`; API emit sync/service.ts:27-28 spreads latitude/longitude only when `!= null`. Web GPS render path CaseDetailPage.tsx:1918-1920 (hasCoords requires typeof number) + StaticMapInset 1944-1981 ('No GPS' fallback).

findings.title: Attachment idempotency-replay lookup is unscoped (global by operation-id prefix) — cross-user/cross-task photo-row disclosure on operationId collision, mitigated only by client-side UUID randomness
findings.detail: On the device FIELD_PHOTO upload path, the idempotency replay short-circuit returns cached attachment rows looked up purely by operation-id prefix, with NO filter on uploaded_by, task_id, or case_id. `verificationTaskService.uploadAttachments` computes `operationBase = (idemKey ?? fields.operationId ?? '').trim()` from fully client-controlled input (service.ts:201), then `const cached = await repo.fieldAttachmentsByOperation(operationBase); if (cached.length > 0) return uploadResult(cached, [], caseId, taskId, ...)` (service.ts:211-212). `fieldAttachmentsByOperation` matches ONLY `split_part(operation_id, ':', 1) = $1` across the entire case_attachments table (repository.ts:1571-1579) — no actor, no task, no case, no scope. The returned `DeviceAttachment.url` is the raw `storage_key` (FIELD_ATTACH_COLS, repository.ts:309-310 aliases `storage_key AS url`), whose path is `field-photos/{caseId}/{taskId}/{uuid}.ext` (service.ts:236). So if a FIELD_AGENT (who holds task.execute legitimately) submits an operationId that collides with ANOTHER user's prior upload, the server returns that user's stored photo rows — disclosing their caseId+taskId UUIDs and the object-storage key structure — without ever validating that the cached rows belong to the requesting actor or to the `taskId`/`caseId` the request was scoped to (note: `caseId`/`taskId` in the returned envelope come from the CALLER's ownedCaseId, but the `attachments[]` rows come from the colliding owner). The DB UNIQUE on operation_id is global (`uq_case_attachments_operation`, DB-verified), confirming the namespace is shared across all users. Exploitability is near-zero in practice ONLY because the legitimate device generates operationId as a v4 UUID (SyncQueue.ts:322 `const id = uuidv4()`, passed as operationId at :329) — 122 bits of entropy an attacker cannot guess or enumerate. But the SERVER does not require or validate UUID format, so the protection is entirely client-side convention. The raw storage_key alone is not directly fetchable (the device must go through the scope-guarded `attachmentUrl` route to mint a presigned URL), so this is information-disclosure of identifiers, not direct byte access.
findings.evidence: apps/api/src/modules/verification-tasks/service.ts:201,211-212 (client-controlled operationBase + unscoped replay return); apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:1571-1579 (fieldAttachmentsByOperation matches only on operation-id prefix, no uploaded_by/task_id/case_id/scope); apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:309-310 (FIELD_ATTACH_COLS exposes storage_key AS url); DB: `uq_case_attachments_operation UNIQUE btree (operation_id) WHERE operation_id IS NOT NULL` (global, not per-user); mobile generates UUID key at CRM-APP-MONOREPO-PROD/crm-mobile-native/src/services/SyncQueue.ts:322,329.

findings.title: client_sha256 transit-tamper signal (`hash_verified`) is captured but never read, surfaced, or alerted — tamper-detection is inert downstream
findings.detail: The attachment upload boundary correctly computes a transit-integrity check: the device's `clientSha256` is compared against the SHA-256 of the bytes the server received, producing `hashVerified` (service.ts:232). By design this is logged-not-rejected (`if (clientSha && !hashVerified) logger.warn('field photo hash mismatch', ...)`, service.ts:233-234) — correct, because the device may legitimately re-encode and a transit check should not block evidence capture. The flag and the client hash are persisted to `case_attachments.hash_verified` / `client_sha256` (repository.ts:1542,1555-1556; DB-confirmed both columns exist). However, `hash_verified` is NEVER read anywhere: a full grep across apps/api, apps/web, and packages/sdk shows the column is only ever WRITTEN (insertFieldAttachment) and the JSDoc — it is not surfaced to any web view, not included in any report, not exposed in any SDK type consumers read, and not alerted on beyond the single `logger.warn`. The result: a photo that arrives transit-corrupted or tampered (clientSha mismatch) is stored as an ordinary attachment indistinguishable from a verified one to every operator and every downstream consumer. The integrity evidence exists in the DB but has zero observability, so the tamper signal cannot influence any verification/billing/audit decision. Severity is LOW because the stored bytes still get their own server-computed `sha256` evidence hash (service.ts:253), so post-storage integrity is independently provable; the gap is that the transit-mismatch signal is collected and then dropped on the floor.
findings.evidence: apps/api/src/modules/verification-tasks/service.ts:232-234 (compute + warn-only), :255 (passed to insert); apps/api/src/modules/cases/repository.ts:1542,1556 (hash_verified persisted); DB \d case_attachments shows `hash_verified boolean` + `client_sha256 text` columns present; grep across apps/api/src + apps/web/src + packages/sdk/src for `hash_verified|hashVerified` returns ONLY write-side references (repository.ts insert, service.ts compute) — zero read/surface/enforce sites.
